title: Senior Reliability Engineer - AV Labs
description:
### **About the Role**
We are looking for a hardware focused Senior Reliability Engineer to focus on sensor and hardware system reliability, owning the observability, alerting, and automation that ensures Uber’s in-vehicle sensor data collection systems operate reliably at scale.
This role is centered on maximizing sensor uptime, data yield, and supply hours across a large, geographically distributed fleet. You will design systems that determine _when to react_ to issues impacting data recording capability, whether caused by failing sensors, degraded onboard computers, software regressions, or systemic environmental factors.
As the technical owner for sensor reliability and observability, you will build the infrastructure that converts low-level signals into actionable intelligence and automated responses. This is a seniorrole requiring strong software engineering fundamentals, deep systems thinking, and the ability to drive cross-team technical direction without direct authority.
### **What You Will Do**
- **Architect Observability Systems:** Design and scale an observability platform capable of ingesting and analyzing real-time health telemetry from thousands of distributed vehicle nodes.
- **Build for Edge Constraints:** Develop systems that remain performant despite hardware diversity, intermittent connectivity, and rapid fleet scaling.
- **Define Criticality Models:** Establish alerting strategies that distinguish transient anomalies from systemic issues impacting sensor uptime and data yield.
- **Detect Complex Failure Modes:** Design detection logic for "silent" failures, such as sensor degradation, compute saturation, or recording pipeline stalls.
- **Scale Through Automation:** Design automated detection, triage, and mitigation mechanisms to eliminate manual intervention as the fleet grows.
- **Partner on Mitigation:** Collaborate with Operations and Engineering to build safe, automated responses to recurring hardware and software failure scenarios.
- **Drive Operational Efficiency:** Build technical interfaces to help Operations surface issues and Engineering diagnose and deploy mitigations rapidly (TTD/TTM).
- **Lead Technical Strategy:** Drive reliability-focused design reviews and translate operational pain points into concrete technical requirements and roadmaps.
- **Uncover Proactive Insights:** Apply advanced data analytics to identify latent patterns in fleet telemetry, enabling the proactive detection of systemic regressions and hardware degradation before they impact operations.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- **5+ years of relevant industry experience** in software engineering, site reliability, or systems engineering
- **Distributed Systems:** Experience with modern observability platforms (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, ELK) in edge, IoT, or hardware-integrated environments.
- **Language Proficiency:** coding skills in one or more of **Go, Python, or C++**, with experience building and operating production systems.
- **Systems Expertise:** Proficiency in **Linux internals** and shell scripting for triaging and debugging edge devices or hardware-adjacent systems.
- **Engineering Fundamentals:** Ability to debug across services, containers (Docker), and networking stacks.
- **Reliability Experience:** Proven track record owning reliability, infrastructure, or platform systems for large-scale production workloads.
- **Observability Tooling:** Experience designing and operating observability systems (metrics, logging, alerting, and dashboards).
- **Metrics-Driven:** Experience defining and implementing **SLIs and SLOs** for system availability or data yield.
- **Networking Knowledge:** Deep understanding of networking protocols (TCP/IP, gRPC, or MQTT) and data handling in bandwidth-constrained environments.
- **Leadership:** Experience driving complex technical projects and architectural reviews across multiple teams from design through production.
### **Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience with modern observability platforms (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, ELK) in edge, IoT, or hardware-integrated environments.
- Knowledge of sensor data protocols (e.g., Camera, LiDAR, Radar) or hardware-to-cloud data ingestion pipelines.
- Experience with "Grey Failure" detection and management in complex, distributed systems.
- Proven track record in 'Fleet Health' for large-scale hardware deployments (e.g., cloud infrastructure, global server fleets, or industrial IoT) where automation was used to replace manual intervention.

Uber Advertising is building the future of retail and commerce media, helping brands connect with consumers through one of the world’s largest mobility and delivery platforms. title: 2026 Operations Internship, Madrid
description:
## **About the Internship**
Operations are the heart and soul of Uber. These teams are bridging the gap between the virtual and the physical world every day by enabling movement. Movement of people, things, food, and more. We are looking for highly motivated problem-solvers who are passionate about Operations. You will work alongside Operations Managers and partner with teams across our Mobility business.
You will get the chance to work on impactful projects that involve analysing data sets, providing creative solutions and leaning into the execution. You will help support decision making within your teams through your critical thinking, strategic analysis, and data-driven approach.
**What You'll Do**
- **Embark** on a chance to reimagine the way the world moves by focusing on real-time, impactful projects
- **Learn** the vast inner workings of Uber - you will have a direct impact on Riders, Drivers, Consumers, Couriers, Restaurants and/or the Marketplace
- **Solve** key strategic problems through analytical frameworks and insights to advise the team on key decisions
- **Experiment** with new products and efficiencies aiming to improve our operations, spanning pricing, demand building, segmentation, customer operations and much more.
- **Collaborate** and engage with a number of functions across the business - operations, finance, policy, communications, product, marketing, legal, and customer support.
**Basic Qualifications**
- Enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program for the full duration of the internship
- Available to start on July 7th and work full time for 6 months
- Work authorization in Spain - No visa sponsorship is available for internships
- Fluency in Spanish and English
- Graduation Timeline: Able to join in a full-time role \[if an opportunity opens up\] shortly after the internship ends
## **Preferred Qualifications**
- **Communication**: Using interpersonal skills and situational awareness to clearly deliver information to a variety of audiences
- **Planning and Organization**: Setting up and maintaining systems to appropriately structure and keep track of tasks and projects
- **Problem Solving**: Effectively resolving issues that involve people, things, and processes by using logic and common sense
- **Stakeholder Management**: Taking time to build meaningful relationships; earning the trust of others within the organization through strong situational awareness and reliability
- **Teamwork**: Demonstrates optimism, respects others, and works well in a team
- **Skills:** Knowledge of data analytics, SQL and experience with statistical packages or basic programming skills (ie Python) is highly regarded.
**Internships @ Uber**
Perks: We offer a competitive monthly internship allowance, Uber credits and much more!
You have the rare chance to create a global impact - we’re moving real people and assets and reinventing transportation and logistics. We offer expansive mentorship from Uber employees to guide you through your internship experience and for you to build personal connections and friendships with hardworking people who share your passion for technology.

title: Senior Software Engineer - Embedded, AV Labs
description:
**About the Role**
Uber is launching AV Labs to accelerate the autonomous technology ecosystem. We're building out a high-velocity team of multi-disciplinary experts to turn real-world operations into high-quality data for our autonomous partners. This team is focused on the hardest problem in AV today: unlocking real-world, long-tail driving data. Autonomy is now a data race—and Uber has an edge: We collect rare, real-world driving data at a scale and capital efficiency no one else can match.
Our AV Labs Hardware team is pioneering the next-generation E/E architecture for our cutting-edge L4 data collectioncapable vehicle fleet.You will drive critical projects ranging from initial 3rd-party hardware driver and firmware development to the embedded development, bring-up, and deployment of our in-house designed production hardware. As a Senior Embedded Engineer, your technical expertise will directly accelerate our path to production, playing a key role in shaping the reliability of our autonomous data collection fleet.
**What You Will Do**
- **Develop Core Firmware & Drivers:** Design, write, and maintain robust firmware and low-level device drivers for 3rd-party automotive-grade components and critical AV sensors (LiDAR, radar, cameras) to enable immediate, high-fidelity data collection and shadow mode operations.
- **Drive In-House Hardware Bring-Up:** Execute the transition to production by taking ownership of board bring-up, hardware validation, and low-level software deployment for our custom, in-house designed autonomous computing platforms.
- **Collaborate on HW/SW Co-Design:** Partner closely with electrical and hardware engineering teams to implement hardware-software interfaces, participate in schematic reviews, and ensure component selection aligns with firmware requirements.
- **Debug & Optimize Complex Systems:** Utilize hardware debugging tools (oscilloscopes, logic analyzers) and software profiling to troubleshoot intricate system-level issues, optimize sensor data pipelines, and ensure strict timing and network synchronization across the vehicle.
- **Contribute to Team Excellence:** Act as a technical resource and mentor within the hardware team, actively contributing to best practices for embedded software architecture, code quality, and scalable deployment.
**Basic Qualifications**
- **Embedded Software Experience:** Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, accompanied by **5+ years** of professional experience developing embedded software using modern C/C++ in RTOS (e.g., FreeRTOS, Embedded Linux) or bare-metal environments.
- **Low-Level Driver & Automotive Hardware Expertise:** Strong experience writing firmware and device drivers for complex, automotive-grade components, SoCs, and autonomous vehicle sensors (e.g., LiDAR, radar, cameras). This must include hands-on experience with sensor integration, board bring-up, hardware debugging tools (e.g., oscilloscopes, logic analyzers), and familiarity with standard automotive interfaces and vehicle networks (e.g., CAN/CAN-FD, Automotive Ethernet, FPD-Link/GMSL, PCIe, SPI, I2C).
- **Production Lifecycle Execution:** Demonstrated experience contributing to embedded hardware systems from initial prototyping and 3rd-party integration through to production deployment—ideally within the autonomous vehicle, robotics, or mission-critical automotive domains.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- **Hardware-Software Co-Design:** Experience collaborating closely with electrical and hardware engineers on custom board designs, including participation in schematic reviews and component selection for computing platforms.
- **Functional Safety & Automotive Standards:** Understanding of automotive functional safety standards and their application in embedded software development, including experience with ISO 26262 (ASIL B through ASIL D) compliance and adherence to coding guidelines like MISRA C/C++.
- **Advanced Timing & Synchronization:** Experience with distributed system timing and hardware synchronization methodologies critical for sensor fusion. Familiarity with Precision Time Protocol (PTP/gPTP, IEEE 802.1AS) and hardware PPS routing across complex vehicle networks is highly valued.
- **High-Performance AV Compute Platforms:** Experience developing firmware and drivers for modern, high-performance autonomous driving SoCs and compute platforms (e.g., NVIDIA DRIVE, Qualcomm Snapdragon Ride) and optimizing data pipelines utilizing hardware accelerators (GPUs, DSPs, NPUs).
For Sunnyvale, CA-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$202,000 per year - USD$224,000 per year.

title: Senior Mechanical Engineer
description:
**About the Role**
As a Senior Mechanical Engineer on our AV Labs Hardware team, you will own and drive the mechanical structural design and system integration of critical vehicle subsystems, including complex sensor arrays and computation modules. By executing design for manufacturability (DFM) and collaborating closely with cross-functional teams and global manufacturing vendors, you will shepherd complex hardware from the initial proof-of-concept phase through to mass production. Ultimately, your innovative solutions and rigorous validation strategies will directly impact the reliability, safety, and scalable deployment of our next-generation autonomous data collection fleet.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
- **Drive mechanical structural design projects** as the end-to-end owner of a mechanical module, taking full responsibility for on-time delivery of design release and product launch. You will contribute to architecture definition and execute the integration of sensor systems, computation modules, and cabin hardware from the requirement collection phase through to final launch.
- **Build and iterate** on the integration of mechanical structures, enclosures, sensors, harnesses, PCBAs, and thermal modules at both the component level and the overall vehicle module level.
- **Execute mechanical component validation and testing** by drafting comprehensive test plans, customizing testing profiles, building test fixtures, and collaborating closely with external labs.
- **Partner with local and global cross-functional teams**, OEMs, and manufacturing vendors to shepherd projects through design, DFM, DFA, prototyping, iteration builds, and into mass production.
- **Create and maintain essential engineering documentation**—including BOMs, SOPs, and troubleshooting instructions—and provide hands-on training and handovers to technicians.
**Basic Qualifications**
- Bachelor's or Master's degree in Mechanical Engineering alongside **7+ years** of industrial experience in product mechanical design, with a strong preference for backgrounds in autonomous driving, consumer electronics, or semiconductors.
- **Expertise in 3D CAD modeling** (such as Solidworks, CATIA) and GD&T, coupled with hands-on experience integrating complex mechanical structures, sensor systems, compute modules, and thermal components.
- **Proven track record** navigating full-cycle product development from Proof of Concept (POC) to Mass Production (MP), demonstrating deep proficiency in DFM, DFA, FMEA, prototyping, and design validation.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- **Advanced proficiency in structural and thermal simulation tools**, including FEA and CFD (such as ANSYS, Icepack, and Solidworks Simulation), alongside experience utilizing Matlab for data analysis and modeling.
- **Demonstrated project management experience** and the ability to own complex subsystem designs, influence architecture decisions, oversee system integration, and mentor junior engineers.
- **Extensive hands-on background** in mechanical component validation and testing, specifically in drafting test plans, collaborating with external labs, customizing testing profiles, and building custom test fixtures.
- **Strong capability to collaborate** with global cross-functional teams, OEMs, and manufacturing vendors (such as Asia FATP), driving DFM, DOEs, and FACA to successfully resolve complex quality and build issues.

title: Operations Manager, Portugal
description:
**About the Role**
Our Operations teams work where Uber's technology meets the real world. We own country-specific business outcomes and bridge our overall business strategy with local needs.
As the Operations Manager, you will be instrumental in executing an ambitious roadmap designed to meet our business growth and expansion targets.
In this high-impact role, you’ll navigate ambiguity to bridge the gap between strategy and execution, taking critical growth initiatives from initial concept to full implementation. By leading cross-functional projects across supply growth, marketplace reliability, and product innovation, you will directly own the strategies that accelerate our trip volume and revenue.
You will work closely with several operational and functional teams including EMEA central operations teams, Safety, Marketing, Legal, Business Development, Policy & Comms teams as well as other HQ stakeholders across the business.
This is a unique, high-impact opportunity to drive the growth and scale of Uber's mobility business in Portugal.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
- Drive Strategic Growth: Lead and execute supply growth and engagement initiatives to ensure sustainable network expansion. You’ll work closely with local partners to expand fleet coverage and driver adoption.
- Optimize the Marketplace: Identify, analyze, and solve core marketplace challenges, such as dispatch inefficiencies and low acceptance rates, to ensure every rider gets a ride and every driver maximizes their earnings.
- Product Innovation & Launch: Manage the end-to-end launch of new product features. You will serve as the operational voice for Portugal in global product discussions, influencing the roadmap with on-the-ground insights.
- Data-Driven Problem Solving: Rigorously monitor business metrics and marketplace performance. You’ll conduct deep-dive analyses to uncover root causes and translate those insights into proactive, executed solutions.
- End-to-End Ownership: Define and prioritize initiatives using data to improve reliability and increase trip volume, owning the project lifecycle from initial concept to implementation.
**Basic Qualifications:**
- Experience: 3–5 years of experience applying rigorous analysis to solve complex business problems (e.g., consulting, investment banking, finance, tech, or operations);
- Full professional fluency in both English and Portuguese is required;
- Analytical Mindset: Proven data-driven decision-making mentality and sound business judgment;
- Execution & Ownership: A self-starter who can identify opportunities and proactively drive implementation. You are a "doer" who thrives on mobilizing people and resolving problems;
- Adaptability: High adaptability to change, with the resourcefulness to move quickly and embrace continuous iteration and experimentation;
- Stakeholder Management: Ability to manage multiple projects in parallel, working effectively with diverse functions and regional teams;
- Technical Skills: AI aficionado; Excellent proficiency in Excel / Sheets; working knowledge of SQL is preferred.
**Preferred Qualifications:**
- Partnership Management: Experience in Business Development or External Key Account Management (KAM), specifically building trust with local networks.
- Advanced Analytics: Experience with statistical packages or basic programming skills to measure performance and prepare business reviews.
- Project Management Mastery: Exceptional organizational skills with a track record of execution in a constantly changing, complex environment.
- Cross-Functional Influence: Experience engaging with Policy, Communications, Product, Marketing, and Legal to move the needle on local business goals.

description:
**About the Role**
We're looking for a driven Junior Business Development Representative (more generally referred to as "インサイドセールス (Inside Sales) or "内勤営業" in Japanese) to support our Uber for Business sales team. This high impact position will provide sales support to Account Executives through the development of qualified leads that result in achieving our business goals. If you're looking for career growth, this role will serve as a training ground for a pathway into an Account Executive position with demonstrated success.
You will be responsible for outbound prospecting through phone, email, and tools such as LinkedIn Sales Navigator and Sales Marker. A key focus in Japan will be building trust-based relationships and understanding customer needs and decision-making processes. Partnering with Account Executives, you will ensure leads are properly qualified and progress through the sales funnel. This includes accurately updating Salesforce with customer insights and identifying clear next steps for Mid-Market Account Executives to engage prospects.

2. Research and develop our accounts Outbound prospecting via cold calling, email, and marketing campaigns

4. Uncover a prospect's business challenges and identify relevant new business opportunities, while highlighting the Uber for Business value proposition
5. Conduct high-level conversations with senior executives.

Basic Qualifications
1. 2025 University new graduate with 1 years or less experience OR 2026 University new graduate
2. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with business-level English proficiency and the ability to extract critical prospect insights while maintaining a mutually valuable conversation.
3. Have a positive, self-driven team-first mentality with a desire to learn
4. Excellent organization, project management, time-management skills, and ability to take initiative
Preferred Qualifications
1. Experience with email campaigns and Salesforce
2. Salesforce, LinkedIn Sales Navigator and Groove experience
3. A passion for technology and B2B sales

title: 2026 Operations Internship, Milan
description:
## **About the Internship**
Operations are the heart and soul of Uber. These teams are bridging the gap between the virtual and the physical world every day by enabling movement. Movement of people, things, food, and more. We are looking for highly motivated problem-solvers who are passionate about Operations. You will work alongside Operations Managers and partner with teams across our Mobility business.
You will get the chance to work on impactful projects that involve analysing data sets, providing creative solutions and leaning into the execution. You will help support decision making within your teams through your critical thinking, strategic analysis, and data-driven approach.
**What You'll Do**
- **Embark** on a chance to reimagine the way the world moves by focusing on real-time, impactful projects
- **Learn** the vast inner workings of Uber - you will have a direct impact on Riders, Drivers, Consumers, Couriers, Restaurants and/or the Marketplace
- **Solve** key strategic problems through analytical frameworks and insights to advise the team on key decisions
- **Experiment** with new products and efficiencies aiming to improve our operations, spanning pricing, demand building, segmentation, customer operations and much more.
- **Collaborate** and engage with a number of functions across the business - operations, finance, policy, communications, product, marketing, legal, and customer support.
**Basic Qualifications**
- Enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program for the full duration of the internship
- Available to start on 7th September and work full time for 6 months
- Work authorization in Italy - No visa sponsorship is available for internships
- Fluency in Italian and English
- Graduation Timeline: Able to join in a full-time role \[if an opportunity opens up\] shortly after the internship ends
## **Preferred Qualifications**
- **Communication**: Using interpersonal skills and situational awareness to clearly deliver information to a variety of audiences
- **Planning and Organization**: Setting up and maintaining systems to appropriately structure and keep track of tasks and projects
- **Problem Solving**: Effectively resolving issues that involve people, things, and processes by using logic and common sense
- **Stakeholder Management**: Taking time to build meaningful relationships; earning the trust of others within the organization through strong situational awareness and reliability
- **Teamwork**: Demonstrates optimism, respects others, and works well in a team
- **Skills:** Knowledge of data analytics, SQL and experience with statistical packages or basic programming skills (ie Python) is highly regarded.
**Internships @ Uber**
Perks: We offer a competitive monthly internship allowance, Uber credits and much more!
You have the rare chance to create a global impact - we’re moving real people and assets and reinventing transportation and logistics. We offer expansive mentorship from Uber employees to guide you through your internship experience and for you to build personal connections and friendships with hardworking people who share your passion for technology.
**About Uber**
We welcome people from all backgrounds who seek the opportunity to help build a future where everyone and everything can move independently. If you have the curiosity, passion, and collaborative spirit, work with us, and let’s move the world forward, together.

title: Partner Manager, Uber Grocery and Retail, Ireland
description:
**About the Role**
The Retail vertical is a startup within Uber, a team focussed on growth, but with the support and leverage of Uber’s large global delivery and ridesharing businesses. It is the delivery of everything that isn’t restaurant food - think groceries, essentials, alcohol, pharmaceuticals, beauty, electronics, and more... We are creating something brand new!
Many of the problems we face are new for Uber and require creative solutions that cater to different customers. If you have the curiosity, passion, and collaborative spirit, work with us, and let’s move the world forward together.
Uber Ireland is looking for a Partner Manager - a hands-on generalist who can own business relationships - to shape and scale our new Retail vertical. This role calls for a combination of project management, relationship management, operational optimization, data analysis and complex problem-solving.
The role calls for a combination of operational mindset, savvy number-crunching, and outstanding relationship management. You should thrive in an “all hands on deck” environment, love solving problems, thrive in a startup culture, and above all be passionate about our Grocery & Retail partners.

title: Senior Account Executive, UberEats, Honolulu
description:
**About the Role**
Uber Eats is looking for a dynamic and results-driven **Senior Account Executive** to join our team. The Senior Account Executive role is a great opportunity to demonstrate your sales experience to expand the number of restaurants that work with Uber Eats. Your primary focus will be **pitching and onboarding the largest restaurant group partners across the SMB Business** that fit our partnership criteria. You will use your prospecting and negotiation skills through cold-calling, email outreach, and in-person visits to build successful partnerships.
As a **Sr. Account Executive**, you will lead the full sales cycle and sign partnerships with top local restaurants while being a champion for the Uber and Uber Eats brands. Your success in this role is measured by achieving and exceeding your monthly quota and proactively building your pipeline by managing and hitting the weekly metrics (cold calls, emails, and field appointments).
**What You'll Do**
- Own and manage a pipeline of strategic, mid-market restaurant groups to sign new partnerships in your geographic region with a mix of cold-calling (at least 50 dials per day), emails, and in-person meetings.
- Integrate feedback from multiple cross-functional stakeholders to drive adoption of Uber Eats across our highest profile merchant partners in the SMB business.
- Create a great first-impression and act as an ambassador of Uber Eats to develop long-term partnerships and clearly communicate the value of Uber
- Manage contract negotiation to establish the foundation of a strong working relationship with our most sophisticated restaurant partners, ensuring sustainable economic enablement for both parties
- Help us identify and build standard processes and collaborate with the Account Management team to support a seamless onboarding experience for each new restaurant partner
- Proactively maintain communication channels with prospects via phone, email, and face-to-face meetings
**Basic Qualifications**
- 4+ Years of sales experience in a closing role
- Excited to learn and have no fear of picking up the phone - this is a great role to grow or build your career in sales
- Bachelor's Degree or equivalent
- Proven achievement in performance-based role
- Effective communication with colleagues and clients
- Experience multi-tasking work with attention to detail
- Coachability, interest in implementing feedback, and dedication to building your brand
- Spanish-language fluency is a bonus
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Spanish, Mandarin or any additional Languages
- Based in Honolulu, HI (Open to relocation)

description:

The Merchant Onboarding & Compliance Specialist plays a critical role in ensuring that all merchants onboarded through our sales channels are fully compliant with Uber’s documentation, structural, and regulatory requirements.

title: Latam Security Center Manager
description:
## About the Role
Leading at Uber means setting direction in motion, not waiting for perfect conditions. As the LATAM Regional Security Center (LSC) Manager, you won't be managing from a polished playbook; you will carry the weight of real-world decisions that safeguard our people, places, and brand across a highly dynamic and volatile landscape.
The environment is intense, high-stakes, and complex. You will oversee a 24/7 security operations engine that translates massive streams of real-time security data into immediate, decisive intelligence and response. This role demands an owner’s mindset, unshakeable grit, and the adaptability to navigate massive ambiguity under immense pressure. If you are looking for a standard corporate security role where the parameters are fixed, this isn't it. But if you are energized by the challenge of managing operational complexity and have the resilience to lead teams through high-stakes crises, this is where you'll make your mark.
## What You’ll Do
- **Lead and sustain** a high-performing, 24/7 team of security center operators and analysts, driving operational excellence and accountability across real-time employee life safety monitoring.
- **Navigate the messiness** of rapid crisis situations, making quick, thoughtful trade-offs and decisions with imperfect or evolving info to protect physical assets and operations.
- **Unblock communication barriers** by collaborating cross-functionally with threat and intelligence teams to synthesize disjointed security data into clear, actionable intelligence products.
- **Own your region’s operational metrics**, managing tight SLA standards, tracking performance goals, and aligning center workflows with shifting global security policies.
- **Build and bridge** critical external partnerships and internal stakeholder networks, using data-driven insights to influence decisions, manage friction, and maintain deep credibility.
- **Foster an inclusive culture** within a high-pressure team, coaching operators to stay steady, show up with intention daily, and continuously raise the execution bar.
## Basic Qualifications
- **Experience with** threat analysis management, security operations, or emergency response leadership in a 24/7 Watch Center, Fusion Center, or equivalent quick-response environment.
- **Professional fluency** in both written and spoken Spanish and English to successfully communicate across local regions and global executive stakeholders.
## Preferred Qualifications
- **Proven track record** of leading a Security Operations Center (SOC) and guiding complex physical security investigations through to resolution.
- **Strong systems thinking** and the ability to organize and prioritize multiple high-stakes initiatives simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.
- **Excellent business judgment** and stakeholder management skills, with the capability to align multiple partners with conflicting priorities during critical incidents.
- **A collaborative mindset** rooted in integrity, transparency, and accountability—willing to disagree and commit for the greater good of Uber's mission.

title: Director, Executive Compensation
description:
**About the Team**
**As the Director of Executive Compensation, you will join a high-impact, agile Total Rewards organization that acts as a strategic engine for the company’s growth. We operate at the intersection of corporate strategy, finance, and human resources, ensuring our leadership incentive structures directly accelerate our mission to reimagine the way the world moves for the better. This is not a traditional, siloed corporate function. We are a data-driven, collaborative team that thrives on complexity, rapid scaling, and innovative design. You will work alongside world-class peers in broad-based compensation, benefits, equity operations, while serving as a trusted advisor to the highest levels of company leadership.**
**About the Role**
You will lead the creation of Compensation Committee materials and administration of executive pay programs that satisfy both the SEC’s requirements and the high growth expectations of our investors. You will also help with the creation of our annual proxy statement in an attempt to secure a high Say-on-Pay vote. You will ensure the executive team is rewarded for building long-term shareholder value while maintaining compliance.
**What You’ll Do**
- **Compensation Committee Materials:** Act as a technical expert, preparing Board-ready decks for quarterly Compensation Committee materials.
- **Executive Benchmarking:** Partner with the Compensation Committee’s independent consultant on executive compensation benchmarking, peer group changes, and potential impact of emerging trends.
- **Dilution:** Maintain models to assess dilution, burn rate, and stock-based compensation expense.
- **Executive Offers:** Partner with Talent Acquisition on executive offers.
- **Proxy Statement:** Partner with Legal in drafting of the Compensation Discussion and Analysis (CD&A). You will translate complex pay decisions into a narrative that wins over institutional investors and proxy advisors (ISS/Glass Lewis). You will prepare most of the compensation tables included in the proxy statement and partner with management consultants on complicated compensation tables such as the PVP table.
- Partner closely with Legal and Finance to ensure executive compensation programs align with disclosure, governance, and compliance requirements.
- Collaborate cross-functionally within Uber to ensure accurate execution, implementation, and ongoing oversight of executive compensation programs.
- Manage special projects as necessary.
**Basic Qualifications**
- 10+ years of experience in executive compensation, ideally within high-growth and/or large cap technology companies
- Direct experience supporting executive compensation decisions, Compensation Committee processes, benchmarking, disclosures, and governance/compliance requirements, and compensation surveys
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Advanced Excel and/or Google Sheets skills, exceptional written and verbal communication skills, and comfort producing board-level and executive-ready materials
- Exemplary judgment and absolute discretion in managing highly confidential executive compensation data and sensitive leadership matters
**The "Toolkit"**
**Transparency: CD&A Disclosures**
- Technical writing that simplifies SEC Regulation S-K requirements.
**Precision: Section 16 Filings**
- Review filings when necessary; partner with Legal when needed.
**Technology: Reporting Tools**
- Workday/Oracle; Morgan Stanley/Shareworks
**Modeling: LTI Payouts & Dilution Models**
- Partner with a consultant on simulating TSR (Total Shareholder Return) to predict future equity vesting levels.
- Create and maintain dilution models that helps us track equity spend and estimate stock-based compensation
****Critical Performance Metrics (KPIs)****
- **Say-on-Pay Support:** Achieving >90% shareholder approval for executive pay proposals.
- **Reporting Integrity:** Zero SEC comments or restatements related to executive compensation disclosures.
- **Competitiveness:** Maintaining executive pay within the chosen tech peer group to prevent poaching by competitors.
### **Advanced Technical Knowledge Required**
- **Internal Revenue Code:** Deep knowledge of **Section 162(m)** and **Section 409A** to avoid tax penalties for the company and the executives.
- **GAAP Accounting:** Understanding **ASC 718** to collaborate with Finance on the "fair value" of equity grants and their impact on the company's P&L.

title: Manager, Driver Operations
description:
**About the Role**
As the Manager, Driver, UK, you will be a critical part of UK Driver Ops, reporting directly to the UK Head of Driver Operations. You will be responsible for some of the highest priority initiatives for UK mobility supply, which may include driver growth, experience, retention and actioning.
As your initial focus, you will spearhead the rapid expansion of our UK taxi business, with a strategic focus on the iconic London Black Cabs (LBC). This critical project sits at the intersection of supply-side execution, demand generation, marketplace strategy, and high-stakes stakeholder management. LBCs are globally recognized, and while Uber's presence is currently limited, this role presents a massive opportunity for market-shaping growth and scale.
You will assume full end-to-end ownership of Black Cab operations, encompassing driver acquisition, engagement, and retention; driving rider initiatives; launching strategic partnerships; and managing key external stakeholders. You will also lead the dedicated Black Cab pod, consisting of an operations manager, a field sales representative, and a driver specialist.
Success will require close collaboration with numerous local and international teams, including UK Driver, UK Marketplace, UK Rider, UK Regs, U4B, Policy, Legal, Business Development, Hailables, Product, and Tech.
**What You'll Do**
01. Own Uber’s UK taxi business performance, with a focus on London Black Cabs
02. Own other high-priority UK mobility driver projects
03. Collaborate closely with the broader UK Driver Operations team.
04. Drive the strategy for Black Cab driver acquisition and onboarding, utilizing both direct channels (field sales, driver marketing, referrals) and key partners (meter/payment companies, fleets).
05. Manage and execute strategic partnerships
06. Accelerate Black Cab adoption among Uber riders through targeted incentives and innovative marketing campaigns.
07. Shape Black Cab compliance policies by providing well-informed recommendations.
08. Serve as the primary subject matter expert for London Black Cabs and general UK taxi market topics.
09. Support taxi launches across the UK
10. Partner cross-functionally with leaders from UK Marketplace, UK Rider, UK Regs, U4B, Policy, Legal, Business Development, Hailables, Product, and Tech.
11. Cultivate and manage relationships with essential external stakeholders, including partners, meter companies, driver unions, and fleets.
12. Mentor and lead team
**What You'll Need**
1. Minimum of 7 years of professional experience in operations, strategy, consulting, marketplace operations, or a similarly fast-paced, high-growth environment.
2. Demonstrated strong analytical capabilities and proficiency in using data to drive critical decisions.
3. Substantial experience managing complex programs involving multiple stakeholders.
4. Exceptional ownership mindset with a consistent track record as a trusted, respected operational problem solver.
5. Strategic thinker able to work through complex, high-level challenges and recommend both short- and long-term solutions.
6. Strong project management skills, with the ability to prioritise and drive initiatives from start to finish in a changing environment.
7. Comfortable operating in ambiguity, demonstrating proactive initiative and executing rapidly in a fast-paced setting.
8. Resourceful, go-getter mentality - excited to build new processes, improve existing ones, and create scalable value for the business.
9. Strong communication and organisational skills

1. Previous experience in the taxi industry, with specific knowledge of London Black Cabs highly preferred.

title: Senior Product Manager — Grocery Fulfillment Optimization.
location: New York, New York, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
Uber Grocery & Retail is scaling into a daily-use, trust-based delivery platform. Fulfillment Optimization is at the center of that ambition.
As **Senior Product Manager, Grocery Fulfillment Optimization,** you will own the systems and decision logic that determine last-mile order fulfillment and how reliably, efficiently, and cost-effectively it happens when conditions are imperfect.
This role sits at the intersection of **marketplace algorithms, real-world logistics, and customer trust.** You will work deeply with **Marketplace and Data Science** to design matching, batching, and recovery systems that adapt dynamically to supply, demand, store readiness, and inevitable failure modes, without degrading the customer or earner experience.
The work spans both **physical-world execution** (stores, couriers, timing, constraints) and **abstract optimization** (matching, batching, objective functions, cost-per-trip). The outcome directly impacts reliability, unit economics, and long-term platform credibility.
This is a senior IC role. We expect you to own ambiguous, cross-cutting problem spaces end-to-end, set direction for modeling and experimentation across teams, and influence roadmaps and prioritization well beyond your immediate scope.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
You’ll tackle some of Uber’s most complex fulfillment problems, where software decisions meet the realities of grocery stores, couriers, and time-bound customer expectations. In this role, you will:
1. **Own the decision logic** behind grocery fulfillment: how orders are matched, batched, planned, recovered, and completed when real-world conditions change.
2. **Partner closely with Marketplace and Data Science** to define matching, batching, and assignment logic that balances reliability, cost-per-trip, earner experience, and store readiness, making tradeoffs explicit and testable in code, not debated in meetings.
3. **Design resilient fulfillment systems** that treat recovery and graceful degradation as core product surfaces. Build backup fulfillment paths, partial-fulfillment recovery, and risk-based reassignment that materially reduce cancellations, missed deliveries, and partial fulfillment.
4. **Translate operational complexity** into product and modeling requirements, ensuring systems behave predictably across edge cases, degraded states, and at scale.
5. **Shift fulfillment from reactive to proactive** — build systems that anticipate risk (scheduled orders, supply variability, store performance) and adapt before customers feel the impact.
6. **Set direction for modeling and experimentation,** influencing the roadmaps of partner teams (Matching, Pricing & Incentives, Ops Tech) to move the system as a whole.
This role offers a rare opportunity to work simultaneously on logistics systems and optimization models, with direct influence on customer trust and business performance.
**Basic Qualifications**
01. **7+ years of product management experience** delivering successful, durable products, with clear ownership and outcomes you can point to. Your fingerprints are all over what shipped, what scaled, and what materially improved the business.
02. **Strong technical and operational fluency.** You’ve built complex systems directly or worked close enough to real-world operations — last mile supply chain and logistics networks — to understand how theory breaks in practice. You can reason comfortably from high-level system design (distributed systems, event-driven architectures, APIs) down to edge cases, data flows, and how software behaves under real operational constraints and failure modes.
03. **Experience building algorithmic or ML-adjacent products** in close partnership with Data Science. You understand how objective functions, constraints, experimentation, and model tradeoffs translate into real marketplace and fulfillment outcomes.
04. **Hands-on data fluency.** You get the data you need on your own — SQL, dashboards, instrumentation — and turn it into insight that drives decisions, not analysis paralysis.
05. **Exceptional attention to detail.** You obsess over how systems behave in the real world across customers, earners, merchants, and operations, and you notice the gaps, edge cases, and failure modes others miss.
06. **Customer obsession.** You take on messy, high-stakes problems and find elegant, simple solutions so customers and partners don’t have to carry platform complexity.
07. **A strong “driver” mindset.** You are biased toward action, a great collaborator, a master disambiguator and simplifier, and relentless about pushing work to clarity and delivery.
08. **Sound judgment under ambiguity.** You can reason through multi-sided tradeoffs — reliability, cost, customer experience, earner experience, and merchant outcomes — and make principled decisions with imperfect information.
09. **Influence without authority.** You move multiple teams in the same direction by setting clear strategy, raising the bar on quality, and earning trust through judgment and execution.
10. **A consistently high bar.** For your own contributions and for the products you ship.
11. **A never-ending desire to learn and grow.** You seek feedback, adapt quickly, and continuously sharpen your craft.
**Preferred Qualifications**
1. **Direct grocery, last-mile, or supply chain fulfillment experience at scale.** Dispatch, matching, routing, picking, or sortation systems in production, ideally with both system ownership and operations-side exposure.
2. **Ownership of decision systems,** not just diagnostic or detection tooling, the logic that decides what the system actually does when the plan breaks.
3. **Experience launching products side-by-side with Data Science** on optimization models: co-designing objective functions, constraints, and experimentation strategy.
4. **Experience building products from 0→1** in ambiguous problem spaces, especially in marketplaces or multi-sided platforms.
5. **Experience in grocery, retail, CPG, or e-commerce,** especially where physical-world execution and software optimization intersect.

As a **Lead Product Manager for Uber Direct**, you will be at the forefront of Uber’s evolution from an on-demand delivery app to a global logistics powerhouse. You will build a new infrastructure designed to move physical goods across cities with the same ease we move people.
Uber Direct & Logistics sits at the intersection of many teams at Uber. The role will be cross-cutting across all sides of the Uber marketplace (merchant, courier, marketplace, and the end customer, whether business or consumer). You will work closely with technical, as well as non-technical collaborators across the globe, coordinating with business development, sales, operations, data science, design, engineering, and other teammates to set the team’s roadmap and complete it.
**What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points**
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
1. **Define the Future of Uber Logistics:** Lead the product vision for our parcel delivery business, moving beyond food and groceries to create a robust, end-to-end logistics platform for retail and e-commerce.
2. **Set Couriers up for Success:** Launch and scale new courier offerings that support longer-distance and multi-stop parcel routes, ensuring a seamless experience for couriers handling complex delivery sequences.
3. **Innovate on Efficiency:** Collaborate with Data Science and Engineering to build next-generation batching and routing algorithms that maximize density and minimize cost-per-delivery.
4. **Scale a New Line of Business:** Treat the parcel vertical as your own business, owning the key performance metrics and removing every friction point that stands between a merchant's warehouse and a customer's doorstep.

02. Comprehensive existing network of contacts across press and relevant institutions
03. Strong strategic capabilities and proven ability to develop proactive communications strategies that cut through and drive measurable results
04. Experience in issues management and explaining complicated topics to a wide range of audiences
05. Experienced at going beyond media relations to reach our audiences
06. Intellectual curiosity and a willingness to experiment in order to learn and identify creative solutions
07. Natural campaigning mindset; proven ability to shape and promote corporate interests
08. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple projects simultaneously
09. Ability to balance critical thinking with tactical execution and attention to detail, in the face of frequent inbound enquiries and other requests
10. Excellent news (and overall) judgment, as well as exceptional writing and communications skills
**Bonus Points If**
10+ years managing corporate and/or consumer communications for technology or consumer facing brands, or in relevant Government communications departments.

title: Enterprise Account Manager, Uber Eats, France
description:
As the Enterprise Account Manager, you will manage a portfolio of top Enterprise QSR brands, becoming their main point of contact. You’ll be expected to deeply understand their operations, priorities, and challenges to proactively identify opportunities and help them perform better on Uber Eats.
You'll partner with Franchisee Account Managers to help them deliver exceptional value to franchise partners through best-in-class local support.
You will drive growth, operational efficiency, and customer experience, while aligning cross-functional teams to unlock long-term mutual value.
**What You'll Do**
1. Sales Strategy & Execution: Build and implement strategic plans. Exceed revenue targets through upsell and cross-sell initiatives, using data to identify growth levers and engaging internal specialists when needed. Lead and support complex commercial renegotiations, aligning internal stakeholders and partner priorities to drive sustainable, long-term value.
2. Client Success & Retention: Act as the main point of contact for the partners’s HQ, holding C-level relationships and driving high satisfaction through proactive support. Collaborate with Customer Success Managers to define and deliver on joint goals. Lead Joint Business Planning and QBRs, continuously working to enhance performance and value on the platform.
3. Operational Mindset: Go beyond strategy, this role also requires a strong hands-on approach. You’ll need to support operational execution alongside your team, stepping in where needed to troubleshoot partner issues, improve KPIs, and ensure day-to-day excellence.
4. Stakeholder Management: Align with cross-functional stakeholders. Act as a generalist with the agility to collaborate across functions (Specialist teams, Ops, Support) to solve partners problems, adapt to local franchisees needs, and ensure the overall success of the partnerships.
5. Coaching & Development: Support, coach, and mentor Franchisee Account Managers, sharing best practices and helping drive strong execution, partner success, and professional growth. This role offers an excellent opportunity for someone looking to develop or expand their people leadership experience.
**Ideal Profile**
1. 4–6 years of experience in consulting, account management, business development, ideally within a tech company or scale-up.
2. Strong exposure to sales strategy, long-cycle negotiations, and managing complex, high-value partnerships.
3. Comfortable operating with C-level stakeholders and aligning cross-functional teams to deliver business outcomes.
4. Data-driven and analytical, with a strong understanding of KPIs and performance metrics (Excel/GSheets required; SQL a plus).
5. Proactive, solution-oriented mindset with the ability to operate autonomously and make strategic decisions.
6. Fluency in both French and English.
7. Experience supporting, coaching, and developing high-performing individuals, including first-line management experience, is a strong plus.

description:

Are you great with cold outreach, prospecting, qualifying, pitching, and closing new customers? Can you deal with constant rejections from prospects and be persistent in following up until you secure a meeting?

title: Group Product Manager, Grocery & Retail Selection
location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
As the Group Product Manager for Grocery & Retail Selection, you will own the strategy, roadmap, and execution for growing merchant and item selection to be delivered on the Uber Eats platform. This starts with merchant onboarding, is maximized through catalog quality and health, and refined with the addition of new item types.
Catalogs power every consumer purchase decision. When they are inaccurate, poorly structured, or unreliable, we create revenue loss, operational friction, and merchant frustration. This team sits at the critical boundary where merchant data enters Uber, and is accountable for ensuring that data is correctly represented, validated, optimized, and protected before it reaches the marketplace.
This is a high-impact leadership role responsible for three core charters:
1. **Merchant Selection & Onboarding:** How Uber can onboard as much valuable selection as possible
2. **Ingestion Platform, Tooling & Optimization:** How merchant data enters Uber through APIs, POS integrations, feeds, and merchant-facing tools. How merchant intent is structurally represented within Uber’s canonical model at ingestion, and how we enable performance uplift while preserving merchant autonomy.
3. **Catalog Reliability & Protection:** How we detect, prevent, and mitigate catalog-related business risk through intelligent monitoring, regression detection, and protective automation.
You will lead a team of 8 PMs with the opportunity to grow the team. You will be partnering closely with Engineering, Data Science, Merchant Operations, and Consumer teams. Your mandate is to evolve from a data pipeline into an intelligent, self-protecting, growth-enabling system that makes selection and catalog integrity a competitive advantage for Uber Eats.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
1. Experience working with data ingestion systems, APIs, integration platforms, or canonical data models.
2. Experience with marketplace, e-commerce, or catalog/menu systems.
3. Track record of improving system reliability, detection systems, or risk mitigation frameworks.
4. Experience driving strategy for business growth
5. Strong executive communication skills with the ability to influence senior stakeholders.
**Basic Qualifications**
As we scale Uber Eats globally, your mission is to make it effortless for any merchant to get and maintain their catalog on Uber in the most optimized, compliant, and reliable way possible.
Here are some of the areas you’ll work on:
1. Define and drive the multi-year vision and strategy for Selection, spanning ingestion method platform, interpretation, and reliability.
2. Bachelor's degree or equivalent
3. Lead and develop a team of 8 PMs, clarifying ownership across charters and elevating product rigor and execution quality.
4. Reduce integration cost and complexity by standardizing APIs, POS integrations, feed ingestion, and mapping/configuration systems.
5. Improve merchant-facing tooling and workflows to simplify catalog setup, bulk updates, and ongoing maintenance.
6. Establish clear structural representation standards for merchants at ingestion and build proactive configuration and compliance guardrails that prevent invalid, un-orderable, or risky catalog states.
7. Evolve catalog health from reactive detection to predictive, automated protection with intelligent staging, blocking, and rollback systems.
8. Tie reliability signals directly to business impact, improving precision/recall while reducing catalog-related incidents and operational load.
**Preferred Qualifications**
1. 8+ years of product management experience, including ownership of complex, technical platform systems.
2. Proven experience managing and developing product managers.
3. Strong analytical and systems-thinking skills, with the ability to connect technical decisions to business impact.
4. Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional initiatives across engineering, data science, and operations.
5. A bias for action and ownership, you’re comfortable working through ambiguity to get to impactful outcomes

title: Construction Manager, Autonomous Vehicle Infrastructure
location: Dallas, Texas, United States; Phoenix, Arizona, United States; Denver, Colorado, United States; San Diego, California, United States; Las Vegas, Nevada, United States; Houston, Texas, United States; Chicago, Illinois, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
At Uber, we're committed to accelerating the transition to autonomous vehicles (AV). The Global AV Infrastructure team plays a pivotal role in expanding infrastructure access to support Uber's ambitious autonomous and electrification goals. We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Construction Manager to join our team. In this multifaceted role, you will be responsible for leading the construction and commissioning phases of AV charging station and vehicle maintenance projects, ensuring they are delivered on schedule and within budget.
### **What You Will Do**
- **End-to-End Project Management:** Comprehensively manage projects from design handoff through construction completion, strictly maintaining quality, budget, and timelines.
- **Procurement & Vendor Selection:** Conduct RFPs, answer RFIs, level-set proposals, and select general contractors for the construction of both maintenance depots and charging station projects.
- **Pre-Construction Planning:** Complete constructability reviews and construction estimates during the design phase to ensure feasibility and cost-effectiveness.
- **Cross-Functional Collaboration:** Partner closely with internal cross-functional teams, external consultants, and general contractors to drive efficient project execution and compliance with negotiated agreements.
- **Oversight & Compliance:** Provide general contractor oversight and manage project budgets, ensuring strict compliance with Uber’s safety standards, local codes, and regulations.
- **On-Site Supervision:** Conduct on-site inspections to verify work performed, proactively identifying and resolving on-site project challenges to ensure continuous progress.
- **Reporting & Onboarding:** Track project progress and regularly report updates to internal stakeholders, including launch teams and executives. Complete contractor reviews and onboard new contractors or stakeholders when necessary.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- **Experience:** 5+ years of experience in site supervision, construction management, or related fields.
- **Project Track Record:** Proven track record of managing end-to-end construction of infrastructure and/or maintenance facility projects on time and on budget.
- **Technical Skills:** Capable of reading complex design drawings and interpreting them for construction. Experience in utility infrastructure, site civil, and/or facility construction.
- **Software Proficiency:** Proficiency in PDF editing and project management software such as Bluebeam, Procore, Asana, or similar tools.
- **Construction Processes:** Strong understanding of construction processes (RFIs, contracts, schedules, change orders), methods, and codes. Electrical contractor experience is a plus.
- **Project Management:** Exceptional organization skills with a demonstrated ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
### **Preferred Qualifications**
- **Advanced Experience:** 8+ years of experience in construction management or a related field.
- **EV Infrastructure:** Proven track record of managing end-to-end construction of fast-charging electric vehicle (EV) infrastructure projects.
- **Electrical Expertise:** Capable of interpreting and applying AC and DC power systems knowledge, including transformers, circuit protection, and single-line electrical and mechanical diagrams.
- **Work Style:** Self-starter with the ability to work independently in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.
- **Travel:** Willingness to travel **>50%** of the time.
- **Education:** Bachelor’s degree in civil/electrical engineering, construction management, or a related field.

location: Nashville, Tennessee, United States; Minneapolis, Minnesota, United States; Chicago, Illinois, United States
description:
**About the Role**
Uber is seeking a Product Developer to lead the development of our charging and maintenance infrastructure for autonomous vehicles. This role will focus on site acquisition for fast charging stations and maintenance depots in core city centers across the Central US region. From identifying and sourcing real estate opportunities to negotiating leases or purchase agreements and handing projects off to design and construction teams, the Product Developer will be responsible for driving projects from start to finish.
This is a cross-functional role that requires working closely with internal teams—including design, operations, legal, policy, and engineering—as well as external stakeholders such as landlords, utilities, city agencies, contractors, and AV Tech partners. The ideal candidate combines real estate, contract negotiation, and infrastructure development experience with strong organizational and communication skills.
**What You'll Do**
- Develop and manage a pipeline of potential new charging and depot sites in alignment with Uber’s strategy.
- Conduct and refine comprehensive site due diligence, including zoning, utility access, permitting, and financial feasibility.
- Lead contract negotiations with landlords, property owners, and public/private partners.
- Partner with attorneys to draft, negotiate, and execute site agreements (lease or purchase).
- Coordinate with design and engineering teams to evaluate installation costs, optimize layouts, and manage project hand-off.
- Accurately forecast milestones and maintain documentation of project progress.
- Build and maintain strong relationships with internal stakeholders and external partners.
- Anticipate risks and proactively resolve challenges that could impact project timelines or costs.
**Basic Qualifications**
- 5+ years of experience in real estate development, infrastructure deployment, project management, or related fields.
- Proven track record in site acquisition, contract negotiation, and lease/purchase agreements.
- Strong understanding of permitting, utility planning, and real estate development processes.
- Experience managing 20+ concurrent projects with complex stakeholders.
- Excellent communication skills; able to engage confidently with executives, landlords, utility partners, city officials, and contractors.
- Highly organized, self-motivated, and able to work autonomously in a fast-paced, evolving environment.
- Ability to travel frequently (up to 50% within the Central US region).
- Valid driver’s license and clean driving record.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Familiarity with charging infrastructure, EV policy, utilities, or autonomous vehicle technologies.
- Experience working cross-functionally in a large, complex organization.
- Prior exposure to site development for mobility or transportation infrastructure.

title: Consumer Operations Manager (Product & Experience)
description:
**About the Role**
As the **Consumer Operations Manager (Product & Experience),** you'll collaborate with a world-class product development and go-to-market team, playing a pivotal role in propelling our product to success. It's a significant opportunity to make a lasting impact.
Your primary goal would be to understand the unique dynamics of the local market, identifying consumer needs as well as opportunities to engage our users and advocating for products and experiences that resonate with the local market. Your analytical skills will be put to good use as you delve into the user journey, gathering qualitative and quantitative insights to enhance the customer experience and address pain points.
In addition to market insights, you will collaborate closely with cross-market and cross-functional teams to operationalize new products and features, further enhancing the consumer experience.
We're seeking an individual who is not only analytical but also passionate about delivering excellence. Strong project management skills are a must, and you should be ready to take on challenges as they arise. If you're passionate about making a meaningful mark and aspire to be a driving force in Uber Eats' success story, we invite you to seize this opportunity and join us.
**Your Impact in Role**
In this role, you will be a vital contributor to our success in Taiwan, utilizing your expertise to enhance our products, improve user experiences, and drive innovation across the board.
- **User Advocacy:** To gather and share insights necessary for product enhancements and new feature development, collaborate with domain experts across various fields, and become the go-to authority on our products and users in Taiwan. You will advocate for features and experiences that align seamlessly with the preferences of the local audience as well as the business and growth strategies
- **Product Launch and Optimization:** Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams including Product, Legal, Tax, Community Operations, Sales, and more to successfully launch and operationalize new consumer-facing product features
- **Cross-line-of-business synergy:** Drive cross-line-of-business coordination to unlock synergies across the Uber ecosystem, increasing user engagement, LTV, and platform stickiness
- **Data-Driven Insights:** Analyze consumer and business data alongside competitive market observations to derive actionable insights and provide valuable recommendations
- **Consumer Research:** Work with internal research team or external agency to plan, design, and execute qualitative and quantitative research in alignment with the consumer growth strategies
- **Customer Satisfaction:** Collaborate with the customer service team, identify pain points and consumer needs to consistently improve customer satisfaction levels
- **Process Efficiency:** Systematically identify and improve process inefficiencies within the adjacent teams, developing programs that enhance execution efficiency and deliver significant business value
- **Fraud Management:** Work with the Risk team, assist in the investigation and management of fraud-related issues, carefully manage the balance between risk control and user experience, and ensure the security and integrity of our operations
**The Experience You'll Bring**
- **Educational Background:** MBA, BA, or BS degree
- **Language Proficiency:** Fluent in English and Mandarin, both written and spoken, is a must.
- **Communication Skills:** Excellent verbal/written communication and presentation skills
- **Detail-Oriented:** Exceptional attention to detail, with the ability to handle multiple projects simultaneously without compromising the quality of deliverables. A knack for bringing innovative ideas to your work
- **Self-Starter:** Self-motivated and capable of working independently while maintaining a collaborative approach within a fast-paced environment. Approach work with a positive, proactive, and energetic attitude
- **Professional Experience:** A minimum of 3+ years of experience as a Product Owner/Product Manager or Product GTM roles for B2C platforms
**Preferred Qualifications**
- **Analytical Background:** Possess 3+ years of experience in consultancy, market research, or business/data analytical roles.
- **Data Proficiency:** Ability to proficiently analyze data using SQL and advanced Excel (Google Sheets) with a strong analytical mindset
- **Project Management:** Demonstrated skills in organization, planning, stakeholder and time management.
- **Additional Skills:** Familiarity with Tableau/Power BI and product tracking tools like App Annie, Mixpanel, and AI tools like Cortana is a valuable plus

description:
**About the Role**
We’re looking for a highly analytical, proactive, and collaborative operator to join the 3P Demand team as Sr Strategy & Operations Manager. This role is central to advancing Uber’s priority of capturing demand outside our native apps by integrating Uber’s Mobility and Delivery services into third-party environments like Instacart, WeChat, and other platforms.
Sitting within the global 3P Demand Operations organization, you will support in building the operational foundations of our 3P Demand strategy—turning external and internal data into actionable insights, supporting tech prioritization, and enabling smarter investment decisions across our global roadmap.
In this role, you’ll own the performance management, manage budget tracking, and support the governance of recurring business reviews. You will conduct deep dives into partnership incrementality, efficiency, and strategic fit to ensure our 3P integrations are driving high-value growth. Partnering closely with Regional Ops, Product, Tech, Marketing, and Data Science, you will provide the clarity and transparency needed to scale Uber’s presence across the third-party ecosystem.
**What You’ll Do**
- Build 3P Demand Strategy: Help shape the 2026 global roadmap and beyond by crystallizing strategic direction, and identifying high-growth opportunities
- Generate Insights & Deep Dives: Conduct recurring and ad-hoc analyses on 3P partnership performance, focusing on incrementality and consumer lifetime value to inform the 2026 global roadmap
- Operationalize 3P Governance: Support the day-to-day execution of the 3P operating model, including tracking progress against roadmap milestones and ensuring cross-functional alignment
- Develop Prioritization Frameworks: Partner with cross functional teams, developing strategic frameworks to evaluate business & technical trade-offs to prioritize against limited resources
- Support Budgeting & Reporting Cadence: Manage the governance for budget tracking and goal setting, ensuring accurate reporting for monthly and quarterly business reviews
- Enable Efficiency Decisioning: Partner with Data Science and Finance to determine efficiency metrics, ensuring that every 3P integration meets Uber’s standards for profitable growth
- Drive Cross-Functional Clarity: Act as the connective tissue between Ops, Tech, and Marketing by standardizing performance inputs and surfacing insights that inspire teams to scale the 3P Demand space
**Basic Qualifications**
- 4+ years of experience in strategy, operations, analytics, consulting, or a similar role
- Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative, business, technical, or related field
- Proficiency in SQL and Excel/Google Sheets; experience building dashboards and reports
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; ability to distill data into insights and recommendations
- Experience with lifecycle segmentation, customer strategy, or allocation frameworks
- Familiarity with BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker) and large data sets
- Demonstrated ability to support executive-level reporting cadences (MBRs/QBRs)
- Strong communicator and collaborator; able to work effectively with cross-functional partners
- Bias for action and ownership; comfortable managing recurring processes with high visibility

You will play a pivotal role in owning Taiwan’s Offers performance and growth, overseeing the co-marketing deal desk, and managing co-fund planning and governance. This role requires a blend of strategic thinking, commercial acumen, and strong cross-functional communication skills.
This is not a quiet role. The Co-marketing and Affordability workstream sits at the intersection of merchant revenue, consumer promotions, and P&L governance — with high stakeholder complexity (AM team, Consumer Ops, COps, Finance) and real commercial accountability. If you are looking for a role with clear playbooks and low ambiguity, this is not it. If you want to own something meaningful, build your commercial muscle, and operate with high visibility, this is one of the best.

title: Staff Software Engineer, Production Engineering
location: San Francisco, California, United States; Sunnyvale, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
Engineering at Uber means building for real-world impact under real-world constraints. The problems are complex, the systems are massive, and the pace is fast. You'll need to make smart decisions with imperfect information, and own them. If you think in systems, thrive in ambiguous, high-stakes environments, and care about building things that actually work, this is where you'll grow.
Core Services Production Engineering is a software engineering team, not a traditional operations or sysadmin team. We solve reliability problems by writing code: chaos testing tooling, traffic engineering and dependency safety frameworks, automation tooling and observability primitives. As a Production Engineer, you will blend multiple domains of software engineering to ensure Uber's services run reliably at massive scale, while improving compute efficiency and accelerating developer productivity for a platform that serves millions of users around the world.
Our main focus areas:
1. Chaos testing tooling and applications
2. Dependency Safety frameworks and tooling
3. Observability and alerting platforms
4. Graceful degradation
If you've worked in an SRE/Production Eng/Reliability role with a heavy software engineering focus, or are energized by the challenge of solving complex engineering issues and building tools that empower an entire engineering organization, you belong on this team.
**What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points**
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
1. Design, build, and maintain software to increase the reliability, scalability, and efficiency of thousands of stateless and stateful production services spread across multiple datacenter zones and regions.
2. Lead initiatives end-to-end within the team, the Production Engineering org, and across engineering at large to increase reliability through automation, setting standards, developer tooling, and reusable frameworks.
3. Work with other engineers to deeply understand their services and guide them towards practical and reliable architecture and implementation.
4. Apply SRE concepts such as observability, integration/load/chaos testing, on-call, incident management, failovers, and disaster recovery to improve mean time between failures (MTBF), time to detection (TTD), and time to mitigation (TTM) of incidents.
5. Participate in on-call rotations, responding to and leading mitigation of production incidents, and driving post-incident improvements.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. Staff Engineer L5B 8+ years of experience in Go, Java, Python, or similar language
2. Experience in delivering solutions end-to-end from defining problems to generating architecture plans, implementation, testing, and delivery.
3. Writes clear technical proposals and RFCs; able to drive engineering alignment across teams through written design docs and verbal discussion.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Experience in various parts of SRE / reliability engineering / incident management at a large-scale company.
2. Experience in platform/infrastructure engineering in related disciplines such as compute platform, software networking, online storage, developer platform, and observability.
3. Experience mentoring and leading teams on projects while remaining hands-on and technical.
