title: Consumer Strategy and Operations Manager
location: Sydney, New South Wales, Australia; Melbourne, Victoria, Australia
description:
##### **About the Role**
##### Uber Eats Australia and New Zealand have been extremely successful markets for our global business and we are looking for a Consumer Strategy and Operations Manager to help drive our next phase of growth. The successful candidate will be a Consumer XLOB (Cross-Line-of-Business) growth coordinator championing the Eats XLOB workstream across ANZ, acting as the connective tissue between our Mobility and Delivery businesses, identifying opportunities to grow cross-platform adoption, deepening our understanding of multi-product consumers, and unlock incremental users and trips by leveraging the full breadth of the Uber platform.
##### **What You’ll Do**
- Own the Eats XLOB workstream: Act as the primary champion for cross-platform growth initiatives, ensuring alignment with Mobility counterparts and driving execution end-to-end.
- Identify and size XLOB growth opportunities: Use data to understand how consumers move (or don't move) between Eats and Rides. Map the cross-platform journey, identify conversion gaps, and quantify the incremental value of multi-product adoption.
- Drive strategic recommendations to leadership: Translate complex cross-platform data into clear, actionable strategies. Build business cases for investment in XLOB initiatives and secure buy-in from senior stakeholders across both lines of business.
- Continuously evolve the XLOB playbook: Test, learn, and iterate on cross-platform growth levers from in-app cross-promotion to bundled value propositions. Play a part in deploying consumer campaigns into the ANZ market, and scale what works into repeatable strategies.
- Advocate for the ANZ consumer experience: identify pain points and collaborate with cross-functional teams to enhance ease of use, reliability, and satisfaction to make using Uber Eats a delight.
##### **What We’re Looking For**
- ##### **Analytical Mindset and Execution Power**: A strong problem solver with exceptional analytical and data-driven decision-making skills. You thrive in tackling multiple emerging issues simultaneously, dealing with ambiguity, and excel at finding innovative, effective solutions.
- ##### **Meticulous Attention to Detail:** You will have a large amount of data available, so you will need to understand the nuances of each metric in order to seamlessly bring it into the broader analysis. It is second nature to you to double check and document, and you go to extensive lengths to make sure things do not fall through the cracks.
- ##### **Exceptional Strategic Thinking Skills:** A proven track record of developing and executing forward-looking strategies in dynamic, high-growth environments. You will be taking strategic workstreams from ideation to execution and will require consideration of a wide range of factors including macro-economic and competitive environments, investment appetite, and operational capabilities.
- **Adaptability and Innovation:** A proactive mindset that thrives in a fast-paced environment, with the ability to swiftly adapt to changing priorities and market conditions. You continuously seek out opportunities to innovate and improve our consumer experience and value proposition.
##### **What You’ll Need**
- 3+ years of demonstrated high-performing work experience in consulting, strategy, analytics, or operations, ideally spanning multiple product lines or business units.
- Consistent track record of using data-driven insights to solve complex, open ended problems, which you will have built by leading projects including launching and optimising processes in a fast-paced environment.
- Strong verbal and written communication. You can craft a compelling business case, and pitch it to senior business leaders, confidently telling a data backed story that helps secure buy in.
- Strong analytical capabilities and data acumen. You are a natural at sourcing qualitative and quantitative data to help define problems and solutions clearly, with experience dealing with complex data sets using excel (or Google sheets) to derive insights.

title: Sr Strategy Associate, Commercial Planning & Merchant Selection
location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
As a Senior Associate for Commercial Strategy and Planning at Uber Eats, you will play a critical role in architecting our merchant ecosystem and designing the economic frameworks that drive merchant success and Uber’s overall business performance.
This role sits at the intersection of marketplace strategy, advanced analytics, and commercial execution. You will lead the design and implementation of data-driven strategies that balance selection density with commercial sustainability. You will be a key architect of our merchant portfolio, ensuring we maintain a competitive, high-quality selection that drives eater frequency while optimizing business economics. This is a high-visibility role where you will provide the analytical horsepower and commercial judgment required to advise senior stakeholders on complex deal structures and long-term portfolio health.
**What You'll Do:**
- **Develop Selection Strategy:** Create the strategic framework for merchant acquisition and retention; identify "must-have" selection gaps via geospatial analysis and design the commercial terms needed to win them
- **Optimize Marketplace Economics:** Analyze market dynamics to determine the optimal balance of merchant investments and develop sophisticated pricing models that reflect the value each merchant brings to the platform, including the design and ROI modeling of merchant incentives
- **Lead Commercial Advisory:** Manage the review and advisory process for complex merchant deals, ensuring they adhere to internal guardrails for pricing, ROI, and long-term profitability
- **Advance Data-Driven Insights:** Build and own the modeling for merchant performance trends, including price sensitivity and investment impact, to surface actionable insights for the Commercial and Operations teams.
- **Drive Process Excellence:** Design and implement standardized frameworks, playbooks and AI-based commercial tools that enable faster, more consistent commercial decisions across the merchant lifecycle
- **Cross-Functional Leadership:** Collaborate with Commercial, Finance, and Legal to operationalize pricing and selection decisions, ensuring our value proposition remains competitive and sustainable
- **Governance, AI & Reporting:** Build and maintain automated dashboards to track selection health and pricing performance to guide executive-level conversations
**Basic Qualifications:**
- 4+ years of professional experience in Strategy & Ops, Commercial Strategy, Finance, Consulting or similar roles within a high-growth marketplace or platform
- Proficiency in SQL and Excel/Google Sheets: experience building complex financial or scenario models from scratch and explaining assumptions to non-technical audiences
- Bachelor's degree, preferably in a quantitative field such as Engineering, Economics, Math, or Finance
**Preferred Qualifications:**
- Communication and stakeholder management: Excellent oral and written communication skills, particularly with senior leadership and commercial stakeholders
- Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret large datasets and translate them into clear pricing strategies that balance short- and long-term impact
- Experience advising senior stakeholders on commercial decisions and the ability to build data-backed processes that improve operational efficiency
- Understanding of restaurant economics, marketplace facilitation, and how pricing influences customer conversion and retention

title: APAC Senior Operations & Strategy Manager, Uber Mobility
description:
**About the Role**
APAC is one of our most under-penetrated and exciting mega-regions, with large consumer pools, a rich and diverse product portfolio, and agile and innovative competitors. APAC Mobility Central Ops is a newly formed unit with an objective to accelerate growth for our mobility business in the region by scaling local and global best practices and deploying subject matter expertise across countries, in close collaboration with the country teams and global partners. The Marketplace team will be responsible for enabling country teams to grow our business and improve efficiencies of our spends via scaled solutions, tech and deploying global best practices.
Key Expectations from the role: Own Core Pricing & Fares across APAC to optimise market performance. Build deep expertise in Uber’s pricing tech and configurations, partner with Local Ops on pricing decisions, and lead the Pricing for Growth agenda. Manage internal pricing tools to ensure accurate, compliant fare updates. Ideal candidate should have strong analytical and problem-solving skills, experience working cross-functionally with global teams, proven ability to drive end-to-end execution, knowledge of pricing strategy & data/analytics.
**Your Impact in Role**
1. Lead Core Pricing recommendations across APAC by scoping, designing, and executing pricing changes that drive long-term growth.
2. Monitor and analyze performance through leading and lagging indicators, identify areas of risk or opportunity, and implement corrective actions as needed.
3. Partner with country teams to ensure markets adopt optimal pricing strategies that balance rider affordability, driver earnings, and business sustainability
4. Manage internal pricing tools to ensure accurate fare updates and compliance across core markets
5. Collaborate with global Product teams to influence the design of new pricing products and ensure alignment with on-the-ground market needs.
6. Communicate insights effectively to leadership through clear, data-driven reporting
**What You Will Need**
1. Extensive experience in operations, strategy, or business leadership, ideally with exposure to mobility, fleets and/or logistics
2. Exceptional problem-solving skills and the ability to think strategically with a comprehensive understanding of the operating environment.
3. Strong analytical and data-driven mindset, with the ability to derive insights and drive decisions.
4. Exceptional stakeholder management and collaboration skills with cross-functional and global teams.
5. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills; ability to build trusted relationships with internal teams and cross-functional teams, be a good listener and genuinely seek to incorporate their views.
6. You have the GoGetIt mindset: you’re willing to embrace the grind, overcome adversity and play to win for the people we serve, seizing opportunity to push the envelope.
7. Advanced analytical skills and prior experiences with SQL, Python or other analytical tools are a plus.
8. Prior experiences with pricing and financial modelling are a plus.

description:

Sales Representatives at Uber Eats bring the best local and regional small and medium restaurants to the Uber Eats platform. Who are we looking for? Someone who is natural at selling and knows the best local restaurants, passionate about food and Uber, and enjoys negotiating.

- Experience with Salesforce and Google Suite

- Comfortable thinking on your feet, genuinely curious and like to ask questions
- Excited to learn - This is a great role to grow or build your career in sales

title: Account Manager
description:
**About the Role**
Community Operations (CommOps) leads how we design and deliver support to users around the world. Uber’s Center of Excellence (COE) is a contact management center that provides world-class customer support to our community. The COE handles complex and high-impact issues while also playing a critical role in launching support models for new products and testing new operational processes and service approaches.
The COE Specialist II role focuses on account management for taxi fleet partners within the Mobility business in Japan. This role goes beyond reactive inquiry handling. Through data-driven consulting, you will support fleet partners in achieving their KPIs and drive mid- to long-term growth across revenue and key performance metrics.
You will proactively lead the cycle of analysis, dialogue, hypothesis development, action execution, and continuous improvement to maximize partner outcomes. In addition, you will contribute to operational improvements and knowledge sharing to enhance overall team productivity, while also serving as a sub-leader supporting the Team Lead.
--
Community Operations（CommOps）は、世界中のユーザーへのサポートのあり方をリードしています。
UberのCOE（Center of Excellence）は、ユーザーに対して世界水準のカスタマーサポートを提供するコンタクトマネジメントセンターです。Uberは、コミュニティにおける複雑かつ高難度な問い合わせ対応を担うと同時に、新しいプロダクトのサポート体制構築や、新たなオペレーション・プロセスの検証も担う重要な役割を果たしています。
本ポジション（COE Specialist II）は、Mobility領域におけるタクシーフリートパートナーのアカウントマネジメントを担うポジションです。本ポジションは、問い合わせ対応にとどまらず、データ分析に基づくコンサルティングを通じてフリートパートナーのKPI達成を支援し、売上および各種Metricsの中長期的な成長を推進します。
「分析、対話、仮説立案、アクション実行、改善」のサイクルを主体的に回し、パートナーの事業成果最大化をリードするとともに、オペレーション改善やナレッジ共有を通じてチーム全体の生産性向上にも貢献します。また、チームリーダーを支えるサブリーダーとしての役割も担います。
**What the Candidate Will Do**
Fleet Partner Account Management: Serve as the primary point of contact for Mobility fleet partners. Manage inquiries end-to-end, resolve issues, and build and strengthen strong, trust-based relationships.
1. Data-Driven KPI Improvement: Analyze revenue and key metrics, identify issues, develop hypotheses, design action plans, and validate impact. Drive sustainable mid- to long-term performance improvements.
2. Consulting and Strategic Recommendations: Lead regular business reviews and reporting sessions. Develop and execute account strategies and improvement initiatives aligned with KPI achievement and business growth.
3. Escalation Management and Cross-Functional Collaboration: Identify root causes of bugs, errors, and complaints. Partner with internal and external stakeholders to drive structured and timely resolutions.
4. Operational Optimization and Process Improvement: Improve workflows, implement preventive measures, and systematize best practices to build scalable and sustainable operational foundations.
5. Team Contribution and Sub-Leadership: Contribute to overall team productivity and quality through knowledge sharing and peer support. Act as a sub-leader and support the Team Lead when needed.
--
1. フリートパートナーのアカウントマネジメント全般: Mobilityフリートパートナーの主要窓口として、問い合わせ対応から課題解決までを一貫して担い、信頼関係を構築・強化する。
2. データドリブンなKPI改善推進: 売上および各種Metricsを分析し、課題特定・仮説立案・アクション設計・効果検証までをリードし、中長期的なパフォーマンス向上を実現する。
3. コンサルティングおよび戦略的提案: 定例ミーティングやレポーティングを通じて、KPI達成および事業成長に向けた改善提案・アカウント戦略を立案・実行する。
4. エスカレーションおよびクロスファンクショナル連携: バグ・エラー・クレームの根本原因を分析し、社内外ステークホルダーと連携しながら迅速かつ構造的な解決を推進する。
5. オペレーション最適化・プロセス改善: 業務フローの改善、再発防止策の構築、ベストプラクティスの体系化を通じて、持続可能かつスケーラブルな運用基盤を整備する。
6. チーム貢献およびサブリーダーシップ: ナレッジ共有やメンバー支援を通じてチーム全体の生産性・品質向上に貢献し、チームリードを補佐する。
**What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points**
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. Basic business-level English communication skills
2. Experience leading KPI improvement initiatives
3. Experience in operations improvement or process design
4. Strategic thinking with the ability to balance short-term results and long-term scalability
5. Ability to proactively tackle ambiguous challenges and deliver creative solutions in collaboration with stakeholders
6. Experience as a Team Lead or Sub-Leader
--
1. 英語での基本的なコミュニケーション能力
2. KPI改善プロジェクトの推進経験
3. オペレーション改善やプロセス構築の経験
4. 短期的成果と長期的スケーラビリティを両立できる戦略的思考力
5. 前例のない課題に対しても主体的に取り組み、社内外ステークホルダーと協働しながら創造的な解決策を生み出せる実行力
6. チームリーダーまたはサブリーダー経験
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. More than 0.5 years of experience in account management or customer success, or experience in a leadership-equivalent role within the customer support domain.
2. Native or business-level fluency in Japanese (written and verbal communication)
3. Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with internal and external stakeholders
4. Proven ability to build and maintain client relationships
5. Hands-on data analysis experience using Google Sheets and/or Excel
6. Strong logical thinking and structured problem-solving skills
7. Experience working in complex, cross-functional environments
8. Self-driven with the ability to work autonomously
9. Collaborative mindset
--
1. 0.5年以上のアカウントマネジメント、カスタマーサクセス。またはカスタマーサポート領域でのリーダー相当の経験
2. 日本語ネイティブレベル、もしくはビジネス上支障のない高い日本語力（口頭・文書）
3. 社内外ステークホルダーとの円滑なコミュニケーション能力
4. クライアントとの関係構築能力
5. データ分析スキルおよびGoogle Sheets／Excelの実務経験
6. 論理的思考力および問題解決能力
7. 複雑なクロスファンクショナル環境での業務経験
8. 自律的に業務を推進できる主体性
9. 協調性のあるマインドセット

title: SMB Account Manager, Uber Eats, Poland
description:
### About the Role
Sales at Uber means navigating ambiguity, earning trust fast, and solving problems that don’t come with a script. As an Account Manager for our SMB (Small and Medium Business) restaurant partners in Poland, you won’t just be a point of contact—you’ll be a strategic growth partner in a market that moves with relentless speed.
This role is about more than just maintaining accounts; it’s about ownership and grit. You will be responsible for the success of our highest-value partners, requiring you to stay calm under pressure and make smart decisions with imperfect information. The work is messy, the stakes for our partners are high, and the pace is fast. You will need to balance operational precision with aggressive revenue growth, often navigating competing priorities across different teams. If you are a resourceful problem-solver who is energized by the challenge of building long-term partnerships in a fast-changing environment, this is where you’ll grow.
### What You’ll Do
- Build and scale long-term partnerships with Poland’s SMB merchants, moving beyond transactional support to become a trusted advisor for their business.
- Navigate ambiguity and internal complexity to unlock value for both our partners and Uber, ensuring they thrive on our platform.
- Identify and execute growth opportunities by upselling marketing solutions (Ads, promotions) that align with the specific needs of your restaurant partners.
- Own your portfolio end-to-end, monitoring merchant KPIs and conducting business reviews to provide data-driven recommendations that prevent churn.
- Collaborate across disciplines—including Operations, Marketing, and Product—to resolve blockers and shape the merchant experience at scale.
- Lead through tension, managing difficult conversations with partners and negotiating outcomes that create mutual value.
- Drive with urgency and discipline, managing your pipeline and portfolio with a constant focus on measurable impact and excellence.
### Basic Qualifications
- Experience with account management, sales, or similar partner-facing functions (typically achieved through 3+ years of professional experience).
- Native or business fluency in Polish and English.
- Proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets, with the ability to use data to inform business judgment.
- Experience managing high-value accounts and meeting or exceeding business targets or quotas.
### Preferred Qualifications
- Prior domain knowledge working within the technology, food delivery, or e-commerce sectors in the Polish market.
- Demonstrated track record of customer centricity, value creation, and managing multiple stakeholders with conflicting priorities.
- Strong systems thinking and the ability to build robust frameworks for solving recurring partner challenges in high-ambiguity environments.
### Let’s ride together
Ready to ride? Uber's mission is to reimagine the way the world moves for the better. Here, bold ideas create real-world impact, challenges drive growth, and speed fuels progress. This isn’t the kind of place where you follow a playbook — it’s where you help write one. If you’re driven by impact, energized by challenge, and ready to shape how the world moves, this is the place for you. What moves us, moves the world — let’s move it forward, together.

title: Groceries & Retail Growth Operations Manager
description:
**About the Role**
This role is a unique opportunity to shape the future of Uber Eats growth across some of our most dynamic and diverse markets — the Andean, Caribbean, and Central America (ACAC) region.
As a Consumer Growth Manager, you will define and lead the strategy for eater acquisition, retention, and engagement, identifying key opportunities to expand our user base and deepen customer loyalty in the Retail business. You’ll partner with local country teams, as well as regional and global stakeholders, to design initiatives that drive measurable business impact.
This opportunity combines analytical expertise, strategic thinking, and strong execution to accelerate growth across markets with different maturity levels. This is a high-impact role at the intersection of data, strategy, and operations — ideal for a leader who can turn insights into action and scale winning ideas regionally.
**What the Selected Candidate Will Do**
Strategy & Execution
1. Develop and execute the regional consumer growth strategy to drive acquisition, retention, and engagement across ACAC.
2. Design, prioritize, and roll out high-impact initiatives tailored to local market dynamics and business priorities.
3. Coordinate and optimize growth and promotional budgets, ensuring efficient investment and measurable ROI.
Performance & Insights
1. Analyze consumer metrics and marketplace dynamics to identify opportunities and bottlenecks.
2. Translate insights into actionable plans to improve user conversion, engagement, and frequency.
3. Establish performance dashboards and frameworks to monitor growth impact and efficiency.
Cross-Functional Leadership
1. Collaborate with teams across Marketing, Product, Operations, Policy, Communications, and Central Strategy to accelerate regional growth initiatives.
2. Advocate for localized product improvements and support prioritization of features that enhance user experience.
**Basic Requirements**
- Immediate availability to work in a hybrid environment from our offices in Santiago, Chile
- Strong communication skills in Spanish and English, with the ability to influence and align stakeholders across multiple countries.
- Advanced Excel / Google Sheets skills.
- 3+ years of experience in strategy, operations, business consulting, investment banking, or a high-growth tech environment.
- Proven ability to analyze data, develop strategic recommendations, and execute effectively in complex, fast-paced settings.
- Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple workstreams simultaneously.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience using Python for data analysis or automation.
- Strong understanding of budget management and performance optimization.
- Hands-on experience with SQL for data analysis and decision-making.
- Experience with AI tools

title: Account Partner
description:
About the Role--
The Account Partner role is an exciting opportunity to showcase your relationship management, sales, and strategic account growth skills. This role focuses on post-sales engagement with small-to-medium-sized businesses, including restaurants and other local merchants.
As an Account Partner, you will play a critical role in retaining and growing our highest-value accounts, ensuring that new partners are set up for success, and identifying opportunities for expansion and increased revenue. Your ability to consult, strategize, and drive long-term value will be key to making Uber Eats the preferred platform for our partners.
What You Will Do ----
1. Own and manage a portfolio of merchants, serving as their primary point of contact and trusted advisor on Uber Eats.
2. Drive revenue growth through upselling and cross-selling Uber products and services (e.g., marketing solutions, Ads, membership, new products), while protecting and improving retention.
3. Analyze account performance using internal dashboards and data tools; translate insights into clear, actionable recommendations and joint plans with partners.
4. Build and execute regular business reviews (e.g., monthly/quarterly) to align on performance, goals, and growth opportunities.
5. Partner cross-functionally (e.g., Sales, Operations, Marketing, Analytics) to solve merchant pain points, improve operational reliability, and unlock new initiatives.
6. Collaborate on and support the execution of promotions, campaigns, and merchandising strategies that drive incremental demand for partners.
7. Monitor key operational metrics (e.g., availability, preparation times, cancellations, quality issues) and work with partners and internal teams to improve end-to-end experience.
8. Maintain accurate account documentation, pipeline, and activities in CRM and internal tools.
9. Represent the voice of the customer internally, influencing product, process, and policy improvements to better serve partners over time.
Basic Qualifications ----
1. 1+ years of experience in Account Management, Sales, Customer Success, Partnerships, or other customer-facing roles.
2. Demonstrated experience building and managing external client relationships, preferably with SMB or mid-market customers.
3. Strong verbal and written communication skills; able to influence a range of stakeholders internally and externally.
4. Comfort working with data (e.g., spreadsheets, dashboards) to diagnose performance, identify trends, and support recommendations.
5. Proven ability to prioritize, organize, and manage a diverse book of business in a fast-paced, constantly evolving environment.
Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Experience in food delivery, e-commerce, retail, SaaS, or marketplace businesses.
2. Track record of achieving or exceeding commercial targets (growth, retention, or revenue) in a quota or goal-based role.
3. Experience using CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce) and working with cross-functional teams to execute joint plans.
4. Strong problem-solving skills; able to move from insight to action with limited direction.
5. Data-oriented mindset; comfortable using data to tell a story, build narratives, and drive decisions with partners.
6. Entrepreneurial, ownership-driven attitude with a bias toward action and continuous improvement.

description:
## About the Role
As a Strategic Agency Partnerships Lead, you will be responsible for expanding Uber Advertising’s position as an indispensable partner to priority agency holding companies. You will sit at the intersection of commercial strategy and relationship management, building deep, multi-level connections across agency investment, strategy, programmatic, and leadership teams.
This role requires a blend of commercial acumen, cross-functional leadership, and a hustle-oriented mindset to drive in-quarter revenue while building long-term, scalable joint business plans.
## What You'll Do
- Agency Partnership & Account Leadership: Build, maintain, and scale relationships across major agency stakeholders. Own the development of joint business plans (JBPs) in partnership with Global and regional leadership, acting as the bridge between global strategy and local execution.
- Commercial Growth: Drive revenue performance while identifying new, scalable commercial opportunities across agency portfolios. Contribute to pipeline development, forecasting, and the operationalization of agency incentive programs.
- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Sales, Product, Marketing, Measurement, and Operations to deliver cohesive agency strategies. Bring structured marketplace feedback back to internal teams to directly shape our product roadmap and go-to-market narratives.
- Enablement & Adoption: Educate agencies on Uber Advertising’s evolving product suite. Build playbooks and workflows that accelerate platform adoption, while empowering internal sales teams with strategic partnership insights.
## Basic Qualifications
- 10+ years of experience
- Experience in media, digital advertising, or platform partnerships.
- Agency-Facing Expertise: A proven track record of managing and growing multi-million dollar advertising portfolios specifically with global agency holding companies or large agency networks.
- Matrixed Environment Experience: Demonstrated success working cross-functionally and globally in a fast-paced, highly matrixed organization.
## Preferred Qualifications
- The Uber Hustle: Speed, resourcefulness, and a go-getter mentality. You thrive in ambiguity and love navigating fast-paced, evolving environments.
- Data-Driven Storytelling: A creative storyteller who leverages data to quantify massive opportunities and influence senior decision-makers.
- AdTech & Data Savvy: Comprehensive understanding of full-funnel measurement solutions (lift vs. attribution) and experience establishing data partnerships or 1P data solutions.
- Trust & Leadership: A proven trust-builder who leads with respect, excels at motivating others, and is a champion for diversity, equity, and inclusion in the advertising industry.

Are you seeking an opportunity to join a world class team, have impact, innovate through your problem solving, and grow professionally? We are currently looking for motivated sales professionals to join our growing SMB team in the U4B organization, based in our Chicago Hub! In this role you will take ownership of building new business pipeline, forecasting opportunities, and constructing account plans to exceed your new business quota.
**_Our team collaborates in-person out of our incredible office in Chicago on Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. We encourage our employees to work from our office on additional days if they desire to do so._**
## **What You'll Do**
- Prospect and build a pipeline to create revenue generating partnerships with new prospects for our business
- Communicate the value of U4B products (Travel, Eats, Delivery) to prospects with a consultative and solution oriented approach
- Achieve and consistently exceed sales goals while also keeping a keen eye on profitability for our business
- Partner cross-functionally with internal departments to ensure a successful onboarding, launch, and long-term success of our clients programs
- Lead and coordinate end to end sales motion for SMB customers including qualification, education, pricing/quoting, contract negotiation to close deals.
## **Basic Qualifications**
- 12+ months of full cycle B2B sales experience OR 24+ months of BDR experience OR 12+ months of BDR experience + 6 months+ of full cycle sales experience
- Experience developing an account prioritization framework, contact identification and outbound narratives
- Proven achievement in a performance-based sales role (meeting sales quota targets) with an emphasis on utilizing a consultative sales approach and customer follow up
- Have a proven track record that shows the ability to search for and identify prospective business opportunities that are aligned with business strategy and criteria.
- Have the ability to adapt your sales style to each unique situation or task to meet the needs of the customer or stakeholder.
## **Preferred Qualifications**
- 1+ years BDR/SDR experience (Lead Generation Experience)
- 1+ years Account Representative experience (Acquisition Experience)
- Experience in Anything-as-a-Service (Tech Based), Tech that is consumption based, Anything for Business (Preferably Tech , Companies/Prospects must sell in to multiple verticals
- Work experience in one or more of the following industries: Internet, IT Services, Computer Software, Leisure and Hospitality (Corporate Level or Enterprise/MM Experience)
- Experience working cross-functionally with internal/customer teams (e.g., Legal, Marketing, Operations)

title: Regulatory Support Specialist
description:
What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
Executional Excellence
1. Deliver outstanding operational outcomes in line with agreed service level agreements, while respecting our regulatory obligations
2. Maintain a high quality of output and attention to detail to ensure that tasks are accurate and complete on time
Data & Case Handling
1. Own monthly and periodic data provisions to regulators, ensuring data is accurate, timely, and aligned to agreed templates and automation flows.
2. Pull, reconcile, and quality-check data needed for audits and RFIs using internal data tools such as QueryBuilder, dashboards, and trackers.
3. Prepare first-draft written responses and supporting attachments for routine audits and requests
4. Support on-site or virtual audits by organising data packs, walking through operational flows, and capturing follow-up actions, with L4 or Legal acting as primary external lead
Subject Matter Expertise
1. Demonstrates a deep expertise in our regulatory and reporting requirements in Taiwan; has the ability to address agent inquiries and handle escalations to ensure regulatory compliance and meet SLA.
Operational Excellence
1. As the team’s scope increases across the APAC region, you will play a key role in ensuring continuous improvement and seamless operations as the team scales
2. Drive improvements to efficiency, accuracy, scalability and robustness by proactively identifying areas for improvement
3. Propose, build and launch innovative and AI-fueled solutions to address process gaps and concerns
Governance
1. Maintain and improve regulatory SOPs, knowledge bases and checklists so requests, audits, and regular reporting can be executed consistently by the broader team and scaled effectively in future
2. Identify recurring pain points or manual steps and propose pragmatic simplifications or automation opportunities, and build / launch them where required
3. Help ensure records are complete, accurate, and auditable, and supporting periodic self-checks or governance reviews.
4. Keep single sources of truth (SSOT) such as knowledge bases, master repositories, and risk / reg trackers up to date so that the team remains audit-ready and can quickly respond to new enquiries.
Stakeholder management & Communication
1. Coordinate closely with cross-functional stakeholders (legal, safety, operations, policy) to collect input for audits and requests for information, and ensure responses reflect Uber’s agreed-upon position
2. Liaise closely with other Community Operations teams to clarify compliance requirements, and monitor and surface any handling issues which may have regulatory impact
3. Display excellent communication skills in engagements with regulators and internal stakeholders
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. Stakeholder management: demonstrated experience in a customer support, operations, regulatory, risk, or analytics role where you regularly worked with multiple stakeholders in different teams and with different perspectives
2. Strong data literacy and comfort working with spreadsheets and querying tools to pull, join, and sanity-check datasets for internal and external presentation
3. Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to translate technical and operational details into clear narratives that regulators and non-technical partners can understand.
4. High attention to detail and a strong sense of ownership for data quality, documentation hygiene, and meeting deadlines, especially when responding to formal government requests.
5. Comfort working in a fast-paced occasionally ambiguous environment, balancing multiple requests and priorities while following structured processes and escalation paths.
6. Continuous improvement mindset: You proactively look for opportunities to Ability to thrive under pressure in a fast-paced, unstructured environment
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Prior experience with regulatory reporting, safety operations, or government-facing compliance work in Taiwan, ANZ, or other regulated markets.
2. Working proficiency in Mandarin and English. Note that you will be required to review Chinese-language regulatory correspondence and draft or quality-check responses, especially for Taiwan regulators. You will be expected to engage with internal stakeholders in both Mandarin and English.
3. SQL proficiency or experience partnering with data/engineering teams to design and maintain data pipelines for operational use
4. Exceptional written and verbal communication skills across multiple functions and teams - a natural storyteller and consensus builder

title: Senior Manager, Japan Business Development
description:
**About the Role**
The Japan Business Development team drives high-impact growth across Uber’s **Delivery and Mobility** platforms by building strategic partnerships that expand vehicle and courier supply, merchant selection and unlock new demand to create long-term value for users, partners and Uber. In this role, you will lead complex, high-value partnerships with leading and emerging players across a range of industries, identifying opportunities that can meaningfully accelerate Uber’s growth in Japan. You will build the business case, negotiate commercial agreements, and mobilize cross-functional teams to turn partnerships into measurable business impact.
**Your Impact in Role**
- Lead the identification, evaluation, negotiation, and execution of strategic partnerships that grow supply and demand across Uber’s Delivery and Mobility platforms.
- Build business cases, shape deal strategies, and influence senior internal and external stakeholders to align and execute on priority opportunities.
- Work cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Operations, Legal, Finance, Policy, and Marketing to move partnerships from concept to launch and measurable impact.
- Partner with leadership and cross-functional teams to shape Uber Japan’s long-term partnership strategy as Uber continues to mature as a key enabler of Japanese society, commerce, and the broader economy.
**What You Will Need**
- Extensive experience in business development, strategic partnerships, corporate development, consulting, strategy, commercial development, or a related field, with a proven track record of leading complex, cross-functional initiatives from strategy through execution.
- Direct experience leading commercial negotiations, including business case development, financial analysis, term sheet creation, contract execution, and senior stakeholder alignment.
- Demonstrated ability to build and manage senior internal and external relationships, including C-level or executive stakeholders at large enterprise partners.
- Professional-level fluency in both Japanese and English, with the ability to negotiate with external Japanese partners and communicate effectively with global internal stakeholders.
- Experience in technology, delivery, mobility, merchant technology, supply acquisition, fleet operations, transportation, payments, loyalty, consumer platforms, travel, or marketplace businesses.
- Strong strategic, analytical, and commercial judgment, including the ability to evaluate tradeoffs across growth, profitability, partner economics, operational complexity, and long-term strategic value.
- Experience working on platform, ecosystem, or API-enabled partnerships that require close collaboration with Product, Engineering, Operations, Legal, Finance, Policy, and Marketing teams.
- High degree of ownership, curiosity, resilience, and comfort operating in ambiguous, fast-moving environments.

title: Senior Partner Manager, Global Delivery Partnerships, Rest of EMEA (RoEMEA)
description:
**About the Role**
As a Customer Success Manager (CSM), you will play a critical role in ensuring merchant success. This role is 70% focused on optimizing operational health (including a combination of operational efficiency, customer experience, and financial health), and 30% on enabling and driving revenue growth by supporting upselling and cross-selling efforts (identifying and proposing growth opportunities) of our GDP Accounts & Account Managers. Global Delivery Partnerships teams manage our largest global restaurant partners such as Starbucks, RBI, Subway, and Domino’s. In this role you will collaborate closely with GDP Account Managers across RoEMEA, to analyze merchant performance, provide financial modeling support for negotiations, identify operational pain points, and scale best practices across the region (14 countries). You will also work closely with internal partners across Product, Engineering, Marketing, Operations, Legal and Finance.
**What You'll Do**
- **Bring your analytical A-game.** Build business models and conduct analyses with Ops, Data Science, and Finance to uncover trends, generate insights, and measure impact.
- **Develop and execute partnership strategy.** Hold internal teams accountable to agreed-upon KPIs and targets.
- **Expand product adoption.** Communicate the value of our newest products creatively to differentiate our offerings.
- **Drive operational initiatives.** Lead key workstreams in collaboration with cross functional teams to drive growth, operational improvements, and increased merchant satisfaction.
- **Collaborate cross-functionally.** Act as the voice of the customer while effectively navigating internal processes alongside Product, Marketing, Operations, Legal, Finance, and other teams.
- **Balance big-picture thinking with attention to detail.** Stay organized and timely to ensure nothing is missed, while also contributing strategically to the growth of our partnerships.
- **Think critically and solve problems creatively.** Navigate negotiations, challenging partner conversations, and internal roadblocks with poise and adaptability.
- **Stay informed.** Remain up to date on industry trends and become a true student of the GDP partners.
**Basic Qualifications**
- Minimum of 5-6 years full-time professional experience
- Experience in a client-facing role working with large enterprises & Global partnerships
- Bachelor's Degree
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience in food delivery and/or third-party marketplaces at a high-growth tech company
- Ability to build and implement joint business plans, prioritizing initiatives that will drive strategic value for the partnership
- Ability to flex your analytical skills and use data to influence decision makers and quantify opportunities
- Speed, resourcefulness, and go-getter mentality. You are comfortable working in a fast-paced environment, navigating ambiguity, and solving problems quickly
- Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
- Excellent listening, communication, negotiation, and organizational skills
- Coachability, interest in implementing feedback, and dedication to the improvement of your craft and our team's development

**About the Role**
The primary responsibility of the Senior Community Operations Manager in ANZ Regulatory Support is to develop, operate, scale, and enhance our regulatory compliance operations for the ANZ region.
This role involves leading teams to achieve compliant outcomes and continuously improve processes, mitigating risks associated with operating within a regulated environment, passionately advocating for our community to influence the overall customer experience, and managing stakeholder relationships across the broader Uber organisation.
**What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points**
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
**Executional Excellence**
1. Lead a team to deliver outstanding operational outcomes in line with agreed service level agreements, while respecting our regulatory obligations and ensuring that our team operates as efficiently as possible without compromising on quality.
2. Ensure that the team maintains accurate records and is audit-proof. This will involve six-monthly checks of all records in keeping with regulatory obligations.
3. Cascade regulatory insights and focus across teams in forums such as fortnightly sync and monthly business reviews.
**Teamwork**
1. Continue to build the team as our scope increases across the ANZ region. As we transform a key part of our operations from an emerging team to a more mature organisation, you'll take the lead in ensuring continuous improvement as the team scales.
**Customer Centricity**
1. Advocate passionately for all members of Uber's community and listen attentively to their feedback to positively influence interactions and provide actionable insights to the rest of the company that directly impact how Uber operates.
**Stakeholder Management**
1. Engage strategically with stakeholders, whether it's mega-regional general managers (GMs) and function leads to influence business goals and priorities, central global teams to improve internal tools and the overall user experience, or local teams to enhance how we operate in cities in the ANZ markets.
2. Lead Regulatory audits, such as Point to Point and Work Health and Safety, liaising with key stakeholders, including legal, Safety Ops, Operations, and GMs, as well as external stakeholders like regulatory bodies.
3. Be the main POC and Subject Matter Expert (SME) with regulators when liaising on notifiables or RFIs, able to communicate with regulatory bodies, and answer ad hoc questions regarding operations at Uber.
**Problem Solving**
1. Coordinate XFN on policy and process RFIs from regulators, including risk mitigation, safety management systems, and ad-hoc rideshare regulatory asks. Communicate this compliance with regulators using data, logic, and procedural handling.
2. Out-of-hours contact may be required on weekends and public holidays for the submission of time-critical notifiables to regulators. Furthermore, the ability to address agent inquiries and handle escalations is expected to ensure regulatory compliance and meet SLA metrics.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. **Project management:** handling sophisticated, multifaceted projects from beginning to end. Has experience running cross-functional projects and a large, complex web of partners, powered by analytical insights.
2. **People management:** A proven track record of strong team leadership and people management
3. **Stakeholder management & communication:** skills across an array of both internal and external stakeholders; exceptional written and verbal communication skills across multiple functions and teams - a natural storyteller and consensus builder
4. **Go get it attitude:** whether in a startup, bank, consultancy, or corporate setting, someone who loves inspiring change in a fast-paced environment.
5. **Regulatory literacy:** Demonstrated ability to navigate complex regulatory environments and conversations
6. **Time management:** Demonstrated ability to balance competing priorities

title: Financial Controller, Dantaxi
description:
**About the Role**
As a Financial Controller at Dantaxi, you will be part of the Finance department and play an important role in ensuring high quality, transparency, and credibility in our financial reporting. You will act as the primary financial contact for Uber, as well as for external advisors and auditors, and work closely with the Finance Manager and CFO.
The role does not include direct responsibility for the monthly closing process, but you will actively contribute to reconciliations, quality assurance, analyses, and explanations, serving as a key sparring partner within the Finance team.
**What You'll Do**
- Ongoing financial reporting to Uber in accordance with agreed requirements and deadlines
- Primary contact for Uber’s finance teams
- Responding to and handling inquiries regarding:
 - Accounting and financial reporting
 - Explanation of variances
 - Revenue, costs, and payroll
 - VAT, tax, and compliance
 - Transfer pricing matters
- Coordinating data, analyses, and responses internally within Finance and with relevant stakeholders
- Primary contact for Danish external advisors and external auditors
- Coordinating local transfer pricing documentation
- Contributing to controlling, financial analyses, and management reporting
- Participating in budgeting and forecasting processes
- Supporting improvements and standardization of financial processes
**What You'll Need**
- Have a relevant degree in finance, accounting, or a related field
- Have solid experience in controlling, accounting, and financial reporting
- Are comfortable communicating with external stakeholders and can do so clearly and professionally—in Danish and English
- Have a strong understanding of the link between accounting, operations, and business
- Possess strong Excel skills and experience working with large and complex financial datasets
- Work in a structured, analytical, and independent manner
- Thrive in a role with both professional responsibility and many touchpoints
**About Dantaxi**
We’ve been providing safe, personal, and reliable transportation since 1937. Today, our approximately 1,900 connected vehicles operate across 75 of Denmark’s 98 municipalities, serving thousands of customers every day.
As part of Uber, we bring global technology and innovation into a local context-creating seamless experiences for customers who want to get where they need to go, when they need it. At the same time, we support drivers by connecting them with more customers through Uber’s platform and providing technology that improves efficiency and the overall experience for both drivers and passengers.
We take responsibility seriously. Dantaxi is a pioneer in the green transition of the taxi industry Sustainability is not just an ambition-it’s something we actively work on every day. Safety is also a core priority, and all our vehicles are affiliated with the industry code “tryg.taxi.”
We believe diversity makes us stronger. That’s why we promote equality and inclusion and encourage all qualified candidates-regardless of ethnic background, gender, sexual orientation, disability, religion, or age - to apply.
Join Dantaxi and help shape the future of mobility in Denmark.

title: Machine Learning Engineer II - AV Foundation, AV Labs
description:
**About the Role**
Uber is launching AV Labs to accelerate the autonomous technology ecosystem. We’re building out a high-velocity team of multi-disciplinary experts to turn real-world operations into high-quality data for our autonomous partners. This team will be focused on the hardest problem in AV today: unlocking real-world, long-tail driving data. Autonomy is now a data race–and Uber has an edge: We collect rare, real-world driving data at a scale and capital efficiency no one else can match (millions of Uber trips every hour across cities, conditions, and edge cases create the data autonomy has been missing). We will build platforms that harness scale and real-world complexity to reimagine how the world moves.
You will be an ML engineer in AV Labs conducting frontier research for autonomous vehicles. The ideal candidate will formulate research problems, design and evaluate novel modeling approaches, and influence the direction of autonomous vehicles research through strong scientific judgment and execution. You will work cross-functionally with engineers from AV Labs and partner engineering teams.
**What You Will Do**
- Design and develop cutting-edge techniques for autonomous vehicle research.
- Collaborate closely with engineering teams and product teams across AV Labs.
- Publish and present original research at top-tier CV and ML conferences.
**Basic Qualifications**
- PhD or MS with equivalent industry experience in Computer Science,  Robotics, or a related field.
- Relevant experience in the field of autonomous vehicles or computer vision, with a strong understanding of how research ideas connect to autonomous vehicles.
- Demonstrated technical contributions, including publications at leading ML and CV conferences such as CVPR, ICCV, ICLR, NeurIPS, or similar venues.
- Proficiency in Python and modern deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow.
- Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with researchers and engineers across teams.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience with autonomous vehicle research.
- Hands-on experience in training multimodal LLMs for autonomous vehicles or relevant computer vision systems.
- Recent publications in one or more of the following areas: 3D perception & scene understanding, multimodal embedding, diffusion models, gaussian splatting, vision language action models, and world models.

title: Lead Product Manager, Seller Tech
location: New York, New York, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Sunnyvale, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
Uber's Seller Tech team owns the end-to-end commercial platform that powers Uber's global revenue motion across every line of business and region. Our scope is product-to-cash across the full revenue lifecycle. The team is the connective tissue between Uber's growth objectives and the systems, workflows, and data that make them executable at scale, anchored on Salesforce and the broader commercial tech stack that connects to it.
We are hiring a Lead Product Manager to own a multi-product domain within this scope and lead the PMs delivering against it. The role sits at the intersection of business strategy, system design, and cross-functional execution. You will own the "what" and "why" for major platform investments, translating ambiguous business goals into structured product strategy, well-scoped requirements, and a defensible roadmap.
You will manage a small team of PMs while staying close to the technical and architectural detail, partnering with engineering, operations, and adjacent teams to ensure platform decisions are durable, instrumented, and architecturally sound. This role suits a senior product leader with the vision to shape long-term direction, the judgment to operate in complex cross-functional environments, and the experience to lead a team of PMs.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
- Own product strategy, roadmap, and execution for a multi-product domain within the revenue lifecycle.
- Manage and develop a team of PMs. Raise the bar on durable product ownership, KPI-driven decisions, and shipping outcomes that matter.
- Build product strategy and roadmap that absorb the complexity of multi-LOB, multi-region sales motions without losing coherence or velocity.
- Lead the design and integration of AI and agentic capabilities into the product surface, including Agentforce and AI-augmented seller workflows. Decide what gets built, what gets integrated, and how the agentic layer interlocks with the platform foundation.
- Partner with engineering, operations, and adjacent product teams to shape solutions, sequence dependencies, and ensure platform changes are scalable, instrumented, and aligned to long-term architecture.
- Define and own KRs and KPIs. Drive measurement discipline so impact can be tied to data, not narrative.
- Represent the roadmap in executive reviews, cross-team forums. Hold the boundary between product strategy and execution, between platform and adjacent systems.
**Basic Qualifications**
- 7+ years experience working at a Product Management, with meaningful time spent on enterprise tech products which Sales, Operations and/or Finance teams use daily.
- 3+ years experience directly managing Product Managers.
- Bachelors degree in Computer Science or similar.
- Domain expertise in one or more of: lead-to-cash, account hierarchy and segmentation, CPQ/CLM, lead management, AM/CS tooling, merchant activation.
- Track record of shipping enterprise CRM or commercial platform products to real users in production, not just leading transformation programs.
- Experience designing or shipping AI and agentic features in enterprise products.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Deep hands-on product experience with the Salesforce ecosystem (CPQ, Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, custom builds) and the integration points to adjacent revenue systems.
- Demonstrated ability to independently own and drive complex product areas from strategy through execution.
- Demonstrated ability to take ambiguous, contested business problems and produce product roadmaps the org can actually execute against.
- Experience operating inside marketplaces, multi-sided platforms, or other businesses where commercial systems have to flex across many seller motions and merchant types.
- Strong instinct for where commercial products break at the seams (between systems, between teams, between regions) and the ability to design product solutions that hold up under those tensions.
- Experience owning products that span multiple regions and lines of business, with the judgment to balance global consistency against legitimate regional variation.
- Demonstrated track record of owning and delivering against a multi-product domain, including managing PMs, and shipping against measurable business outcomes.

title: Uber Health Implementation Manager - Providers and Strategic Partners
location: New York, New York, United States; Washington, District of Columbia, United States
description:
Uber Health is one of the fastest-growing segments within Uber for Business poised to become the leading global platform for health transportation. In the US alone, almost [6 million](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32298170/) people annually miss or delay healthcare appointments due to transportation issues. Breaking down logistical barriers to care and closing social determinants of health gaps will make healthcare more efficient, curb downstream costs through stronger healthcare appointment adherence, and help patients live healthier lives. We do this by leveraging Uber's unique assets to provide healthcare ecosystem partners and consumers with HIPAA-compliant on-demand transportation - at scale.
The Implementation Manager owns the end to end client launch experience, and plays a pivotal role in driving revenue and product performance. This role requires strong discovery, executive-level communication, and the ability to navigate multi-stakeholder cross-functional groups within our client base to catalyze action and sustained adoption of Uber Health. You will lead large, high value accounts post sale to ensure strong adoption and performance of the Uber Health product suite.
Working under the Head of Implementation for Uber Health, the primary focus will be designing and streamlining high touch launches for large providers and strategic partners, as we invest in further segment growth within strategic accounts and sub-segments of the provider landscape. This requires a combination of consultative solutioning and project management to drive adoption of product features specific to client use cases and needs, inclusive of API integrations. Implementation Managers create alignment across internal and external functional teams and fully understand the levers that drive key performance metrics. They are expected to understand the healthcare industry and the client's business models to create operational efficiency and ensure the shortest time from sale to high quality execution.
Up to 35% travel may be required for onsite relationship building and launch management.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
1. Drive comprehensive discovery with partners to understand and align the Uber Health product suite and best practices to customer specific operational workflows
2. Manage the technical configuration and activation of client accounts working with external & internal stakeholders to optimize for customer experience and impact
3. Develop and deliver comprehensive program-specific resources, including training decks, coordinator overviews, and client-facing communications to ensure program readiness and minimize time to launch.
4. Proactively monitor post-launch adoption rates and utilization metrics against initial forecasts, escalating utilization barriers to relevant stakeholders to ensure revenue targets are met.
5. Derive qualitative and quantitative insights, leveraging direct customer stories, user feedback and data trends to inform near and long term opportunities to increase client and product impact
6. Represent the voice of customer to make informed recommendations to Product, Sales, Marketing, Operations and Support on how to improve the customer experience using Uber Health products
7. Achieve operational excellence by iterating and improving on playbooks, internal tools and resources, and cross-functional & internal processes
**Basic Qualifications**
1. Bachelor’s Degree
2. Minimum 6 years of relevant work experience in B2B US Healthcare
3. Deep experience in healthcare and technology, specifically accountable care organizations, or national provider groups
4. Demonstrated ability to lead complex B2B implementations, specifically managing cross-functional dependencies across client teams (e.g., Finance, IT, Operations) and internal teams (e.g., Product, Sales, Support).
5. Project Management, Program Management, or Learning & Development Experience
6. Data fluency
**Preferred Qualifications**
1. Deep experience in healthcare and technology, specifically with health systems, accountable care organizations, or national provider groups
2. Strong use of insights and confidence around data-driven decision making
3. Proficiency in tech platform configurations, and willingness to learn technical API integrations to drive customer adoption at scale
4. Proven track record of operational excellence, developing strategies and implementing them effectively
5. Ability to problem solve, adapt, and grow quickly
6. Excellent written & verbal communication and interpersonal skills
7. Excellent organization, project management, and time management skills.
8. Builder mentality

description:

Uber's rapid global expansion and diverse business models demand a sophisticated and highly scalable revenue accounting and reporting function.
As a Senior Accounting Manager in the Revenue Accounting Operations team, you will be a strategic and hands-on accounting leader responsible for either the Global Mobility revenue team or the Global Ads and Acquired Entities team. You will ensure the integrity of our financial statements by overseeing revenue recognition for diverse business lines—including Mobility, Ads Products, and M&A activity—while driving scalable automation and process improvements to support our aggressive growth trajectory and expanding global footprint. .

title: Senior Systems Engineer, AM&D Services & Products
location: New York, New York, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
As a Senior Systems Engineer in our Autonomous Mobility & Delivery (AM&D) group, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of autonomous transportation at Uber.
You will be responsible for the safety of the technical frameworks that allow Uber to integrate seamlessly with various autonomous vehicle (AV) partners while developing our own internal autonomous offerings. This role sits at the intersection of cutting-edge robotics, safety-critical systems, and complex platform integration. This role is on Uber’s Autonomous Mobility and Delivery Safety team and reports to the Senior Manager, Systems Engineering, Autonomous Mobility & Delivery Autonomous Mobility & Delivery.
**What You Will Need / Bonus Points**
\-\-\-\- What You Will Do ----
1. **Define partner-agnostic solutions:** Develop and standardize technical safety solutions and frameworks that expedite the go-to-market process for diverse autonomous vehicle tech providers and AV fleet operators on the Uber platform.
2. **Define safety requirements for Uber-built autonomous offerings:** Lead the elicitation and documentation of high-level and detailed system safety requirements for Uber’s proprietary autonomous technology and platform services.
3. **Develop V&V strategies:** Design comprehensive Verification and Validation (V&V) strategies for both internal Uber solutions and the complex technical interactions between Uber’s platform and external AV Partner systems to ensure solutions meet Uber’s safety bar.
4. **Safety Risk Assessment:** Lead technical safety risk assessments to ensure that all AV partner integrations meet Uber’s rigorous safety and performance standards.
5. **Influence Strategic Decisions:** Act as a technical subject matter expert to influence long-term product roadmaps and strategic partnerships, ensuring technical feasibility and safety are at the forefront of business decisions.
**Apply AI responsibly:** Evaluate and integrate AI/ML-driven approaches to enhance safety validation and risk identification, while ensuring appropriate oversight, traceability, and compliance with safety standards.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. Bachelor’s degree in Robotics, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, Systems Engineering, Computer Science, or a related technical field.
2. 7+ years of experience in systems engineering, with a significant focus on complex hardware/software integrations.
3. Proven track record in the Autonomous Vehicle industry, specifically within safety-critical systems.
4. Experience with V&V methodologies (e.g., ISO 26262, SOTIF/ISO 21448).
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in a relevant engineering discipline.
2. Experience defining cross-functional system architectures for large-scale distributed systems.
3. Strong ability to communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders and executive leadership.
4. Experience leveraging AI/ML tools in engineering workflows or safety analysis

title: Staff Systems Engineer, AM&D Partner Integration
location: New York, New York, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
In this role, you will be the primary technical lead responsible for evaluating the safety and maturity of Autonomous Vehicle (AV) partners looking to launch on the Uber platform.
Your mission is to conduct rigorous technical safety due diligence to ensure that any third-party AV technology meets Uber's safety standards. You will serve as the technical bridge between Uber and our partners, requiring a unique blend of deep systems engineering expertise and world-class external communication skills. This role is on Uber’s Autonomous Mobility and Delivery Safety team and reports to the Senior Manager, Systems Engineering, Autonomous Mobility & Delivery Autonomous Mobility & Delivery.
**What You Will Do**
\-\-\-\- What You Will Do ----
1. **Determine Partner Assessment Criteria:** Define and maintain the technical rubrics and safety benchmarks used to evaluate diverse AV stacks. You will tailor these criteria to account for different operational design domains (ODDs) and technology architectures.
2. **Partner Integration Leadership:** Work hand-in-hand with external partner engineering teams and internal engineering teams to ensure their system aligns with our safety expectations.
3. **Safety Risk Assessments (SRAs):** Conduct deep-dive technical reviews of partner systems. You will be responsible for authoring comprehensive Safety Risk Assessments that synthesize complex technical data into clear, actionable feedback for both the partner and Uber’s leadership.
4. **Regulatory compliance & system impact:** Partner with policy and regional teams to translate local regulatory requirements into engineering solutions, and perform rigorous safety analyses to evaluate their impact on vehicle systems, user behavior, and operational risk.
5. **Process Improvement:** Continuously evolve Uber’s partner onboarding and assessment frameworks. You will identify bottlenecks in the current verification and validation (V&V) pipeline and implement data-driven improvements to make the process more scalable and robust.
6. **Strategic Communication:** Act as the face of Uber’s safety engineering team to external partners. You will navigate complex technical negotiations and provide high-stakes verbal and written briefings to cross-functional stakeholders, including Product, Legal, and Policy teams.
7. **Apply AI responsibly:** Evaluate and integrate AI/ML-driven approaches to enhance safety validation and risk identification, while ensuring appropriate oversight, traceability, and compliance with safety standards.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. Bachelor’s degree in Robotics, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, Computer Science, or a related technical field.
2. 7+ years of experience in systems engineering, with a significant focus on complex hardware/software integrations.
3. Proven track record in the Autonomous Vehicle industry, specifically within safety-critical systems.
4. Experience with V&V methodologies and AV industry standards and best practices (e.g., ISO 26262, SOTIF/ISO 21448).
5. Proven experience performing Safety Risk Assessments (SRA) or similar technical due diligence.
6. Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with a track record of managing high-stakes external technical relationships.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Experience with ISO 26262, SOTIF (ISO 21448), and ANSI/UL 4600 standards.
2. Demonstrated ability to navigate global regulatory landscapes and work with internationally distributed teams.
3. Ability to influence cross-functional teams without direct authority.
4. Strong analytical skills with the ability to distill complex technical risks into business impact.
5. Ability / experience with AI

description:
**About the Role**
As part of the Strategic Finance team, you will help scale the financial reporting, analytics, and planning capabilities and empower decision makers across our different businesses and functions. You will play a key role in all aspects of financial reporting and make significant contributions to high-profile strategic and operational initiatives. Your work and the insights you create will drive real time business decisions and help Uber to continue to grow, scale and become more profitable, while also creating more opportunities for the many thousands of courier, restaurants and drivers that use our platform daily. By applying to this position, your application is automatically submitted to the following Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) areas based on role availability: Mobility, Delivery, Commercial, Technical and Corporate Finance. So if you're ready to dive into a rewarding set of challenges and you've got the proven financial skills, experience, passion, and strong team mentality - read on and get in touch!
**What the Candidate Will Do**
- Financial & Operational Planning: Partner with company leaders to develop financial forecasts and operational goals
- Partner with the business: To apply business judgment and financial insights to assist with decision-making related to new initiatives, products, and business opportunities
- Sophisticated financial modeling: Break complex business problems down into components, and build models from scratch as well as improve existing models
- Strategic Thinking & Execution Excellence: Partner with your team to execute projects and operationalize our insights
- Be creative, adaptive, and eager to learn new technical skill sets
**Basic Qualifications**
- BS/BA in Finance, Accounting, Economics or other relevant subject
- 4+ years of proven experience
- Experience with Excel/Google Sheets
- Prior financial and/or analytical experience including FP&A, investment banking, management consulting, private equity, or operations
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience with SQL and AI tools
- Outstanding analytical skills - standard methodologies are second-nature, your models are efficient, scalable, and intuitive
- Excellent communication and people skills - numbers are key, but a business is built with people
- Self-starter - you will take initiative, be dedicated, set and drive toward goals
- Ability to prioritize - we are consistently working on multiple projects at any given time, so we are looking for someone who is comfortable working in an ambiguous environment and has the ability to optimally multitask
- Standout colleague - this is a highly collaborative and encouraging team environment
- Attention to detail and ability to execute in a fast paced environment
- Basic knowledge of GAAP

title: Senior Program Manager, Service Operations, AV Fleets
location: Los Angeles, California, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
As the **Service Operations Program Manager**, you will be the primary architect of the operational systems that keep Uber’s autonomous vehicle (AV) fleets moving. You aren't just managing a steady state; you are building the "Service Engine" from the ground up. You will define how we intake, triage, and repair the world’s most advanced vehicle technology across a global network of AV partners and fleet operators.
Our ideal candidate is a **technical operator**—someone who can pivot from high-level strategy (defining a global operating model) to granular execution (ensuring a technician captures the right photo for a warranty claim). You are a builder who thrives in the "gray space" of emerging technology, possessing the entrepreneurial spirit to scale processes and the professional rigor to hold partners accountable to world-class SLAs.
**What You'll Do**
#### **1\. Service Operating Model & Governance**
- **Define the E2E Service Lifecycle:** Establish the end-to-end service journey, including intake, triage, dispatch, repair execution, QA/closeout, billing, and reporting.
- **Establish Governance:** Build the framework for success by defining RACIs, SLAs, escalation paths, and operational readiness gates for new releases.
- **Playbook Development:** Standardize SOPs for high-frequency and high-severity workflows, such as vehicle grounding, safety holds, and tooling outages.
#### **2\. Tooling & System Integration**
- **Lead the Toolchain:** Drive the configuration and global rollout of service management tools in collaboration with Uber’s technical teams and third-party providers.
- **Data Integrity:** Partner with Product and OEM teams to ingest service catalogs and work instructions, ensuring technicians have the right data at their fingertips.
#### **3\. Warranty & Claim Management**
- **Warranty Strategy:** Stand up a comprehensive warranty operating model across OEM and AV components.
- **Recoverability:** Ensure workflows capture critical data (DTCs, logs, failed-part traceability) to maximize warranty recovery.
- **Audit & Optimization:** Build reporting to identify "warranty leakage" or repeat failure patterns, driving corrective actions with providers to reduce costs.
#### **4\. Parts & Supply Chain Operations**
- **Operating Model:** Define stocking strategies, replenishment triggers, and min/max levels to ensure high vehicle uptime.
- **Traceability & Compliance:** Establish standards for serialized parts, lot tracking, and chain-of-custody.
- **Vendor Management:** Partner with suppliers to improve fill rates, reduce backorders, and standardize nomenclature across disparate systems.
#### **5\. Scaling & Partner Onboarding**
- **Partner Readiness:** Collaborate with Launch and Central Ops teams to lead training and onboarding for new fleet partners.
**Basic Qualifications**
- 6+ years of experience in Program Management or Operations (Service Ops, Fleet Ops, Tech Ops, or Supply Chain).
- Proven track record of standing up and scaling operational programs involving multiple external partners/vendors.
- Ability to drive requirements across tooling, data, and process while "holding the line" on global standards.
- Analytical Mindset: Comfortable using data to identify failure patterns and drive partner accountability.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience with fleet maintenance/Work Order systems and inspection tooling
- Familiarity with OTA (Over-the-Air) release management, gating, and incident response for connected vehicles.
- Experience operationalizing partner contracts, specifically defining and enforcing service-level metrics.
- Experience managing operations across multiple geographic markets or regulatory environments.
