title: Localization Program Manager
description:
**About the Role**
The Localization team is seeking an experienced Localization Program Manager to help solve Uber’s growing localization needs. In this role, you'll work closely in a cross functional scope, partnering with stakeholders from Product, Design, Content, Engineering, Marketing, Legal, Regional Operation teams and others, to ensure on-time & high linguistic quality for global & regional launches. This is a hybrid position requiring to be in office 3 days a week.
**What you'll do:**
1. Manage end-to-end localization of Uber products and external clients assets across all supported languages including initial planning & consultation, delivery of localized content, LQAs, and bug routing;
2. Review and influence product requirement documents (PRDs/RFCs) related to new products/features as well as tools and systems that can improve globalization (g11n) across Uber;
3. Manage localization of product, legal and marketing related comms with various assets (surveys, website, contracts, dashboards, etc) across all regions
4. Proactively support or suggest internal and cross-functional initiatives to identify areas for process improvements across different teams;
5. Identify Localization tool & process gaps and work with Localization Technical Operations to enable efficiencies;
6. Work closely with localization vendors and providers, following up during business hours in timezone of regional location, to ensure smooth execution of tasks and on-time deliveries
**Basic Qualifications**
01. BA/BS degree or equivalent practical experience
02. 3 years minimum of localization project/program management experience within the high-tech industry on the client side
03. Familiarity with latest technologies (MT, LLM, GenAI) and their application to localization workflows
04. Strong vendor side experience working with fortune 100 companies with enterprise level localization teams.
05. Experience with managing budgets and creating estimates.
06. Self-directed - ability to take initiative in identifying and solving large problems
07. Ability to navigate ambiguity, work independently and be able to chart direction of projects/program, especially while operating in a different region
08. Ability to leverage available data to analyze status and make recommendations
09. Very detail-oriented, quality-focused and proactive
10. Excellent written and verbal communication skills and the ability to manage and present to multiple and diverse stakeholders across businesses and leadership levels
11. Ability to learn new tools/systems quickly
**Preferred Qualifications**
1. Great understanding of localization and internationalization issues
2. Experience using CAT tools to manage localization pipeline & workflows (e.g. Idiom WorldServer, Phrase, and Trados)
3. Experience managing localization of various content types such as but not limited to UI, HC, Marketing, & Legal

title: Strategic Operations Manager Germany - Growth Bets, Venues & Partnerships
description:
## Strategic Operations Manager: Growth Bets, Venues & Partnerships
**Uber Mobility \| Germany** **Location: Berlin, Germany (Hybrid)**
### **About the Role**
Uber is looking for a **Strategic Operations Manager** to join our Germany Country Team, a group dedicated to shaping the future of mobility in one of our most critical "Star" markets.
In this role, you won't just be managing spreadsheets; you’ll be on the front lines of innovation. You will identify the next "big bets" for growth, manage high-profile relationships with iconic German venues (like the Uber Arena), and bridge the gap between abstract strategy and on-the-ground execution. This is a cross-functional powerhouse role where you’ll collaborate with Product, Marketing, and City Ops to ensure that every rider and driver experience is seamless, whether they are leaving a sold-out stadium or discovering a new Uber product for the first time.
### **What You’ll Do**
- **Fuel Growth Bets:** Own the end-to-end lifecycle of new product initiatives. You’ll translate market insights into actionable strategies, moving from ideation to launch and scaling high-impact opportunities that hit revenue targets.
- **Elevate Venues:** Manage senior-level relationships with stadiums and event locations. You’ll lead commercial negotiations and then roll up your sleeves to design physical pickup zones, signage, and wayfinding to optimize rider/driver flow.
- **Operationalize Partnerships:** Design and launch consumer-facing campaigns that turn brand partnerships into measurable demand. You’ll build the "playbooks" that allow these partnerships to scale across the country.
- **Analyze & Optimize:** Define the KPIs that matter. You will track performance metrics conversion, reliability, and ROI and continuously iterate to improve the experience.
- **Collaborate across the "Forest & Trees":** Balance high-level strategic planning with the nitty-gritty of daily operations. You’ll work with leadership to set the direction for Germany while ensuring standard operating procedures are flawless.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- **Project Management Mastery:** Proven ability to manage complex, cross-functional projects. You can turn ambiguous requirements into structured, scalable processes.
- **Operational Rigor:** You have a "get it done" mindset and are comfortable translating strategy into detailed execution plans with clear outcomes.
- **Stakeholder Influence:** Exceptional communication skills with the ability to manage external partners and internal teams with diverse perspectives.
- **Language:** Business-level fluency in **German (B2)** and English.
### **Preferred Qualifications**
- **Experience:** 3+ years in technology, management consulting, or high-growth operational environments.
- **Analytical Depth:** Strong data skills. You are comfortable using data to derive recommendations and aren't afraid to dive into the details.
- **Technical Aptitude:** Experience with **SQL** is a major plus. If you don’t know it yet, you must have the appetite to learn it quickly, as it will be a core part of your toolkit.
- **Strategic Agility:** The ability to pivot quickly in a fast-paced environment while keeping the "big picture" in mind.

description:
**About the Role**
Uber Eats is looking for sharp, highly motivated individuals to join our Small-to-Medium Sized Business (“SMB”) Account Management Team to be focused on our Southeast markets (Miami based). As an SMB Account Manager, you will work efficiently and effectively with our SMB restaurant partners to optimize restaurant operations, strengthen our relationship with partners through excellent consultative recommendations for business growth, and identify upsell opportunities for other product offerings. description:

Leading at Uber means setting direction in motion, not waiting for perfect conditions. You’ll build high-performing teams in ambiguous environments, make hard calls under pressure, and carry the weight of decisions that matter. If you lead with clarity, build with heart, and thrive in challenge — this is where you’ll make your mark.
In this role, you will lead the Large & Mid Enterprise Segment team, managing a portfolio of national brands including Hans Im Glück, Five Guys, and Dean & David. This isn't a "business as usual" account management role; it’s a high-stakes leadership position focused on driving commercial excellence and sustainable growth while navigating a complex P&L. You will manage a multi-layered team of 6-8 people, including managers of managers, across Account Management and CSM functions. This role is for a leader who can stay resilient through tough negotiations, find purpose in the messy reality of global scale, and move fast to bridge the digital and real worlds.

title: Senior Software Engineer - Stateful Platform
description:
### **About the Role**
Engineering at Uber means building for real-world impact under real-world constraints. You’ll be responsible for the infrastructure that runs Uber’s global stateful systems - over 20 types of technologies including MySQL, Redis, and Kafka - across 50,000+ hosts and exabytes of data.
This role is based in our Aarhus, Denmark office, a key global hub for Uber’s Infrastructure and Platform teams. In Aarhus, we don’t just use technology; we build the core systems that keep Uber moving. The work is massive in scale and high in stakes. You will navigate the tension between moving fast to increase fleet-wide efficiency and maintaining the extreme reliability required for systems that never sleep.
We are looking for an engineer eager to grow into a role with greater technical ownership and leadership. As our Stateful Platform team continues to scale, we need someone who can go beyond execution to formulate a north star vision for our products. You will be expected to drive the team toward that goal independently, staying steady through the regular disruptions that come with operating at our scale. If you thrive in high-ambiguity environments, value ownership over hierarchy, you will grow here.
### **What You’ll Do**
- **Design, build, and maintain** the automation frameworks that deploy and run all database engines globally, ensuring high availability across on-prem and multiple cloud environments.
- **Solve high-impact infrastructure problems** where the solution isn't always obvious - such as automating kernel upgrades or storage cluster expansions across millions of containers.
- **Build and own** services written in Go, focusing on system reliability, resource forecasting, and intelligent placement to maximize utilization.
- **Navigate the messiness** of technical debt and shifting priorities, ensuring every code change is backed by rigorous testing while maintaining a bias for action.
- **Unblock fleet-wide efficiency** by optimizing scheduling, with the goal of eliminating manual on-call operations through automation.
- **Lead through influence** by mentoring peers and collaborating across global engineering sites (SF, Amsterdam, Seattle, Bangalore) to ship practical solutions at speed.
- **Engage with the local community** by participating in or leading tech meetups hosted at our Aarhus office to share knowledge and drive engineering excellence locally.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- **5+ years** of professional software development experience.
- **Proficiency** in at least one backend language (e.g., Go, Java, Python).
- **Experience with** software engineering fundamentals, including testing methodologies and quality documentation.
- **Technical depth** in building or managing distributed systems at scale.
- **Demonstrated ability** to lead technical direction and help a team navigate through ambiguity and complex organizational changes.
### **Preferred Qualifications**
- **Experience with** containerization and orchestration (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes).
- **Knowledge of** database and stateful systems (e.g., MySQL, Kafka, HDFS, or Redis).
- **A mindset for automation**—specifically a track record of replacing manual processes with self-healing systems.
**Perks & Benefits:**
- **Monthly Uber Credits:** Credits to use on Uber Rides and Uber Eats every month.
- **Equity Compensation:** Opportunity to be awarded stock options (RSUs) to ensure you own a piece of the mission you’re building.
- **Culture & Socials:** Frequent local social events and office clubs (chess, board games, running, crossfit, creative club and more)
- **Tech Community:** We host regular local tech meetups to stay connected with the Aarhus engineering scene, and give our engineers the opportunity to sometimes share what they’re working on with the community
- **Well-being & Fertility:** Global support programs for mental health, wellness, and family planning/fertility.
- **Parental Leave:** Generous, gender-neutral parental leave to support your life outside of work.
- **Modern Aarhus Hub:** Work in a center of technical excellence featuring catered lunches and top-tier collaboration spaces.
**Ready to ride?** Uber’s mission is to reimagine the way the world moves for the better. Here, bold ideas create real-world impact, challenges drive growth, and speed fuels progress. This isn’t the kind of place where you follow a playbook - it’s where you help write one. If you’re driven by impact, energized by challenge, and ready to shape how the world moves, this is the place for you. What moves us, moves the world - let’s move it forward, together.

title: SMB Sales Manager, Uber for Business, Argentina
description:
## About the Role
This is a high-stakes, hands-on opportunity to lead the Samll Business Sales Teams for Uber for Business in Argentina. We are looking for strategic builders and resilient sales leaders who are energized by the messy reality of doing big things in fast-scaling markets. As the lead for this segment, you will navigate the unique challenges of the Argentine market, making smart decisions with imperfect information to help local businesses thrive.
You will be responsible for setting direction in motion, carrying the weight of critical decisions, and building a high-performing team in a highly ambiguous environment. This role is for someone who leads with clarity and heart, and who is motivated by the tangible impact our platform has on local communities.
This is a hybrid role - our team collaborates in-person out of our incredible office in Buenos Aires 3 days/week. We encourage our employees to work from our office on additional days if they desire to do so.
## What You’ll Do
- **Lead and build** high-performing sales teams by setting a clear vision and inspiring them to pursue and realize it through a culture of risk-taking and learning from failure.
- **Navigate** the complex and fast-moving sales environment in Argentina, managing pipelines with urgency and discipline while building long-term trust-based partnerships.
- **Scale** the business by designing and implementing sales playbooks and repeatable processes that support sustainable growth and operational efficiency.
- **Own** the end-to-end sales lifecycle, from identifying new business opportunities to negotiating creative deal terms and overcoming difficult objections.
- **Unblock** your team by proactively identifying technical opportunities or process gaps and collaborating across marketing, operations, and product teams to resolve them.
- **Influence** stakeholders at all levels, using data to align interests and secure support for high-impact initiatives and complex deals.
- **Manage** and optimize team performance, making the hard calls when necessary and providing real-time coaching to help individual contributors reach their full potential.
- **Collaborate** cross-functionally to bridge the digital and real worlds, ensuring that client feedback is directly incorporated into our product development and strategy.
## Basic Qualifications
- **Experience with** leading successful sales teams and managing the full end-to-end B2B sales lifecycle, ideally in SaaS.
- **Proficiency in** English and Spanish, with the ability to communicate technical and business concepts clearly to diverse audiences.
- **Minimum 5 years** of professional experience in sales, account management, or a related high-growth commercial field.
- **Validated ability** to drive results and achieve ambitious revenue targets in a fast-paced and ambiguous environment.
## Preferred Qualifications
- **Systems thinking** and the ability to analyze complex data sets to identify market shifts and optimize investment strategies.
- **Domain knowledge** of SaaS, mobility, or platform-based B2B sales within the Latin American market.
- **Cross-functional collaboration** experience, particularly in building strategic alignments with product, legal, and finance teams.
- **Resourcefulness** in achieving big goals with limited resources and a track record of coaching underperforming team members to success.

description:

Are you ready to work in a world-class sales team inside one of the leading tech organizations in the world? description:

title: 2026 Account Management Intern, Strasbourg
description:
### **About the Role and Team**
This internship sits at the commercial heart of Uber Eats. You'll join an Account Management team and take on real ownership of partner performance across a portfolio of restaurant brands.
Account Management at Uber Eats is fundamentally a commercial function. Your job is to grow revenue for partners and for Uber. That means you'll be working with data daily, running business reviews, identifying where performance is lagging and why, and translating that analysis into concrete recommendations that move the needle.
This isn't an observational role. From week one, you'll carry a workstream, supper the team with a set of accounts, and be expected to show up to conversations with a point of view grounded in data. This internship offers hands-on exposure to commercial operations, account management, customer success, and cross-functional project management in a fast-paced environment.
**What You’ll Do**
## **Commercial Performance & Partner Growth**
- Support the AM team across a set of accounts, tracking performance against commercial KPIs: GMV, order volume, conversion rate, basket size, and customer retention.
- Engage directly with restaurant partners to identify growth opportunities and recommend commercial solutions.
- Support the sale of Ads & Offers products by articulating their value and helping partners adopt them.
- Manage outbound partner conversations with clear commercial objectives.
- Run structured business reviews: prepare the analysis, identify the growth levers, and land a clear recommendation.
- Build the narrative behind the numbers  translating data into a story that drives partner action.
- Support expansion conversations: new site launches, geographic growth, and cross-sell opportunities within existing accounts.
## **Data Analysis & Insight Generation**
- Run structured performance diagnostics: identify where a partner is underperforming versus benchmark and why.
- Build and maintain reporting dashboards that surface the metrics that matter.
- Develop and test hypotheses about what drives partner revenue then work with the team to act on them.
- SQL exposure is a plus but not required; comfort with Excel or Google Sheets and a structured analytical mindset is essential.
## **Marketing Execution & Demand Generation**
- Execute performance marketing campaigns promotions, visibility boosts, pricing experiments  and rigorously track ROI.
- Identify where marketing investment will generate the highest incremental return for a given partner.
- Analyse post-campaign data to draw conclusions and feed learnings back into future strategy.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- Currently enrolled in a Master’s degree program (e.g., Business Administration, Economics, Marketing, Operations, Data Analytics, or related fields), with graduation by early 2027.
- Available for a full-time, 6-month internship **starting 7th September 2026.**
- Proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets, including data extraction and analysis
- Native in French and fluency in English
- Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
- Open to exploring full-time opportunities with Uber following the internship in case a role becomes available.
- You are looking for a last year study internship.
# **Who Thrives Here**
The interns who get the most from this role arrive curious about the numbers, comfortable being challenged on their thinking, and genuinely interested in how commercial relationships work. They take ownership early, ask sharp questions, and know the difference between being busy and having impact.
If that sounds like you, we’d like to meet you

title: Partner Growth Manager (Fleets)
description:
## **Partner Growth Manager (Fleets) \| Germany mobility**
**Location:** Berlin /  L3
**Team:** Operations – Driver Growth
### **About the Role**
Our Operations team is the heart of Uber. In Germany, one of our top global priority markets, we don’t just move people; we move the world forward.
As a **Partner Growth Manager**, you will be at the core of our Driver Growth engine. You aren't just managing accounts; you are a strategic consultant and a growth hacker for our fleet partners. You will act as the face of Uber, helping fleet owners navigate regulatory landscapes and scaling their businesses on our platform. If you’re a data-driven problem solver who loves building relationships and seeing the direct impact of your work on the streets of Germany, this is your next move.
### **What You’ll Do**
- **Drive Fleet Expansion:** Own the growth and robustness of our fleet partner base. You’ll identify new supply pools and sell Uber’s app and services to bring new fleets into the ecosystem.
- **Strategic Relationship Management:** Build deep, long-lasting partnerships with our top fleet owners across Germany. You are their primary point of contact and their advocate within Uber.
- **Operational Excellence:** Be the face of Uber in your city. You’ll help partners navigate local challenges, regulatory complexities, and daily operational hurdles.
- **Incentive Design:** Shape and implement incentive strategies that drive engagement and maximize supply on the platform.
- **End-to-End Project Ownership:** Lead cross-functional projects from ideation to execution, working with Marketing, Legal, and Product teams to unlock growth.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- **Experience:** 3+ years of professional experience in Operations Management, Management Consulting, Sales, or a high-growth Start-up environment.
- **Ownership Mindset:** You are a self-starter who thrives in "white space." You can handle significant responsibility with minimal oversight.
- **Analytical Rigor:** You are data-driven and KPI-focused. You don't just find problems; you build the models to solve them.
- **Communication:** Fluent in **German and English** (both written and verbal).
- **Mission-Driven:** You are genuinely excited about Uber’s mission and enjoy working with a diverse range of partners.
- **Adaptability:** Willingness to travel to engage with partners on the ground.
### **Preferred Qualifications**
- **Technical Toolkit:** Proficiency in **Google Sheets** and **SQL** is a major plus. You’re comfortable diving into data to extract actionable insights.
- **Stakeholder Management:** Proven track record of managing complex external partnerships or high-stakes negotiations.

title: Safety Operations Manager, Screenings
description:
**About the Role**
We are searching for an analytically minded, highly motivated individual to join our Safety Operations team! This team focuses on the safety landscape in the United States and Canada, covering both on-trip safety and platform access safety for the Mobility and Delivery businesses.
In this role you will need to have a firm foundation in data analysis, proven ability to translate the "what" into the "so what", and a passion for eliminating root causes that lead to safety incidents and poor user experiences on the platform. You will work closely with safety and business partners to identify root causes to safety trends, measure the success of controls we have in place to mitigate risk on the platform, and develop strategic business recommendations to address gaps in our safety processes and concerning trends. You will have autonomy to find opportunity areas and independently work with product and data teams to craft strategic ways to mitigate risk on the Platform. Your work will ultimately cultivate safer user experiences on the platform!
## **What You’ll Do:**
- Scope and lead central initiatives focused on advancing safety and creating a best-in-class Screenings program while responding to the constantly evolving safety environment in the US&C
- Use a data focused approach to identify, understand, and measure the biggest risks on the platform, their safety controls, and the efficacy of controls
- Become a subject matter expert on access safety processes to improve operations monitoring and drive meaningful improvements in the earner onboarding experience
- Partner with key stakeholders on cross-functional initiatives to support company growth initiatives while maintaining a high-bar for safety standards
## **Basic Qualifications:**
- At least 3+ years of professional work experience in Project / Program Management, Operations, consulting and or in a related field
- Experience in using SQL
- Bachelors Degree
## **Preferred Qualifications:**
- Previous experience working strategy, operations, quality assurance, safety & trust, and/or risk management
- Experience in problem solving and proposing unique solutions to complex issues
- Ability to use data to develop insights and measure performance
- Ability to work with and effectively communicate with a large number of cross-functional stakeholders across different levels and parts of the organization
- Self-motivated with a demonstrated ability to take initiative in a fast-moving environment
- Experience working closely and communicating effectively with a diverse set of stakeholders in a constantly evolving, rapid growth environment with tight deadlines
- Demonstrated experience interpreting and presenting data to various audiences. Ability to translate quantitative findings into action plans
- Strong verbal and written communication skills. Able to synthesize a lot of information quickly, highlight key take-aways, and disseminate meaningful insights
- Demonstrated ability to develop business strategies and influence internal and external stakeholders

title: Manager of Account Management
description:
## About the Role
As a Manager of Account Management in SOCAL, you’ll be stepping into a high-stakes environment where leading a nimble team means more than just hitting targets—it means building for scale while navigating the messy reality of a fast-changing market. This role is a split between high-level leadership and deep operational grit. You’ll lead a team focused on upselling and optimizing performance for our merchant partners, but the real challenge lies in doing so under the pressure of constant ambiguity.
At Uber, we don't follow a playbook; we write it. You’ll need to stay calm when priorities shift, own your decisions even with imperfect information, and inspire your team to see the opportunity within every complex problem. This isn't a "business as usual" management role. It’s for the leader who is energized by the challenge of turning unstructured problems into focused execution and who understands that in the Greater LA segment, building trust and earning it fast is the only way to move the world forward. If you thrive in the heat of negotiation and find purpose in driving real-world impact at scale, you’ll grow here.
## What You’ll Do
- Lead and scale a small, nimble team of Account Managers, ensuring precise execution across portfolios while coaching them to navigate the tension of high-growth targets and complex partner needs.
- Navigate ambiguity by co-owning relationships with key restaurant brands, developing growth strategies that adapt to shifting market dynamics and internal priorities.
- Unblock revenue growth by identifying upsell and cross-sell opportunities, pushing beyond standard product adoption to craft creative solutions that solve real merchant pain points.
- Drive data-driven narratives, using performance metrics to identify "the why" behind the numbers and presenting recommendations that optimize merchant success under real-world constraints.
- Influence without authority, collaborating cross-functionally with Product, Operations, and Legal to be the voice of the customer and move projects through the inevitable messiness of a large-scale organization.
- Stay resilient through difficult partner conversations and pricing pushbacks, turning objections into long-term partnership wins through creative mediation.
- Own the details and the vision, managing the tiny operational "must-haves" while never losing sight of the strategic step-functions needed to stay ahead of the competition.
- Raise the bar for your team by modeling a "go-get-it" attitude, ensuring everyone stays accountable and motivated even when the path forward isn't predefined.
 **Basic Qualifications**
- Experience: A minimum of 5 years of professional experience, with at least 18 months in a dedicated Account Management role (preferably in tech, SaaS, or advertising).
- Education: Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
- Proficiency in: Managing partner relationships and using data to drive business decisions.
 **Preferred Qualifications**
- Leadership Mindset: Experience coaching or mentoring team members to hit ambitious targets in fast-paced environments.
- Grit & Resilience: A proven track record of overcoming client objections and staying steady through tough negotiations.
- Strategic Thinking: Ability to build quarterly and annual sales/marketing plans that balance short-term wins with long-term sustainability.
- Adaptability: Proficiency in navigating CRM tools like Salesforce and data tools like Excel/Google Sheets to translate messy data into clear, actionable insights.
- Systems Thinking: Experience selling into or partnering with large, complex enterprise brands where navigating internal and external stakeholders is key.
For Los Angeles, CA-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$107,000 per year - USD$119,000 per year.

title: Senior COE Specialist I
description:
**About the Role**
L4+ incident refers to a sequence of unwanted incidents taking place which lead to agitation and threat to the brand. It leads to uncertainty and causes major harm to the organization.The team detects early signs of brand risk and minimizes the damage against the negative consequences of the same. It not only affects the smooth functioning of the organization but also poses a threat to the brand name.

This is an opportunity to own and grow a portfolio of ~60 Global Strategic Accounts across the UK, helping some of the world’s largest organisations unlock more value across Uber’s U4B solutions beyond travel.

title: Growth Strategy Operations Manager, Uber Direct - Finland
description:
**About the Role**
Alongside Uber Eats, we are launching Uber Direct in Finland. Uber Direct is our white-label delivery service that enables merchants to offer fast, same-day delivery from their own websites, apps, or stores — with no need for customers to use the Uber app.
You will own the initial GTM commercial strategy (pipeline creation and sales) for the market. As we launch our first merchants, you will then transition to also focus on scaling our merchants’ operations and driving long-term commercial health of our partnerships. You will be the vital link that translates high-level digital strategy into scalable, on-the-ground delivery operations.
Success here requires intrinsic drive to solve tough problems and the ability to influence and align stakeholders with competing priorities. You must stay steady under pressure, using data to navigate ambiguity and drive results for your partners. You will build deep expertise in the local delivery ecosystem and help define the standard for how the Finish market experiences on-demand last mile delivery.
**What You'll Do**
As a Growth Strategy Manager, you will be responsible for:
**Sales:** Closing the best partnerships with the right merchants, at the right commercials, with a strong cross-functional commercial team. You will work alongside our Restaurant and Retail teams to identify the right GTM approach (pipeline and commercial strategy) and win our first accounts to go live.
**Account Management:** Driving growth, operational excellence and financial sustainability with our live merchant partners. Ensuring the end-to-end experience for our merchants is easy, and operations flow with high levels of reliability. Work closely with our scaled Operations team to escalate, resolve and improve when our experience is not at the desired level.
**Financial and operational efficiency:** You will build long-term relationships with your key accounts and be the main point of contact across the org, from Store Manager to HQ, focusing on improving their operations and optimizing our commercials.
**Analysis:** Quantify everything you do. Utilize an analytical approach to understand the performance of initiatives to ensure you are effectively prioritizing those with the highest return on investment.
**Be proactive:** This role will require fast thinking and moving swiftly through high-pressure environments.
This is the ideal role for someone with a strategic mindset coupled with next-level sales and account management skills and an operational curiosity. You will have the rare opportunity to launch a market from scratch, pilot new features, operating models, and commercial structures, which can be playbooked and scaled across Uber.
**Required Qualifications**
- 3+ years of customer or client facing experience ideally in account management or consulting. Previous experience in setting and achieving business targets
- Critical thinking and problem solving capabilities: Experience with challenging the status quo and identifying the root cause of hard problems to solve.
- Analytical skills: Comfortable with data analysis, spreadsheets and presenting findings and recommendations. Data driven decision making mentality and sound business judgment through strong analytical thinking.
- Commercial acumen: Understanding of how businesses and organizations work and how to navigate a partner’s business. Ability to build sales pitches, negotiate deals and creative commercial strategies for our partners in a fast-growing market.
- Communication: Excellent communication skills and the ability to manage and influence diverse stakeholders across businesses and levels.
- Project management: Able to create meticulously detailed execution plans for large-scale projects (e.g. launching a new product, onboarding a major enterprise) and then deliver them to plan regardless of any surprises or ambiguity along the way
- Stakeholder management: Experience working with cross-functional stakeholders, e.g., Commercial, Operations, Product (Tech), Finance and Legal teams.
- Highly autonomous self-starter, comfortable with ambiguity: Able to come up with new ideas and execute them autonomously. Eager to learn with a #gogetit attitude. You should not be afraid to roll up your sleeves and get your hands dirty!
- Finish and English fluency a must
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Knowledge of Last Mile Delivery space
- SQL experience

title: [2027年度新卒採用] Associate Acquisition Account Executive
description:
アソシエイト・アクイジション・アカウント・エグゼクティブ（日本（2027年度新卒採用)
営業、セールス＆アカウントマネジメント \| 日本・東京
**チームについて**
レストランは、Uber Eatsの根幹を成す存在です。日本には驚くほど多様で世界トップクラスのレストラン業界があり、それがUber Eatsの世界的な成長を強力に牽引しています。日本アクイジションチームは、競争の激しい市場においてUber Eatsのプレゼンスを拡大する上で、極めて重要な役割を担っています。
私たちの使命は、高い可能性を秘めたレストランパートナーを発掘し、説得力のある価値提案を構築し、それらをシームレスに当社のプラットフォームに導入することです。Uberにとって世界的に最も戦略的に重要な市場の一つである日本において、次の成長段階を共に築いていきましょう。
6月12日「金」に開催する就職説明会にもぜひお越しください！詳細は以下をご覧ください
[Here](https://pages.beamery.com/uber/page/pagesbeamerycomuberpageuber-japan-daijob-career-fair-june-12th-2026-labtyrdre0)
**職務内容**
アソシエイト・アクイジション・アカウント・エグゼクティブ（新卒レベル）として、日本の主要都市における優先度の高いレストランパートナーのエンドツーエンドのセールスライフサイクルを担当していただきます。単にUber Eatsで提供されるレストランの選択肢や品質を拡大するだけでなく、地元企業が新たな収益源を開拓できるよう支援し、ゼロから長期的なパートナーシップを構築していきます。
ここは、指示が常に明確に書かれているとは限らない、スピード感のある環境です。私たちが求めているのは、不確実性を問題解決のキャンバスと捉え、拒絶を学びの過程の一部として受け入れ、揺るぎない成長マインドセットをチームにもたらす、粘り強く自発的に行動できる人材です。
**主な業務内容**
担当エリアのオーナーシップ or 責任： 潜在性の高い地元のレストランの特定や初回コンタクトから、契約交渉、プラットフォーム上での初期ローンチ支援に至るまで、販売サイクル全体を推進します。
強固な関係構築： 個々のレストランオーナーが抱える固有の課題を深く理解し、Uber Eatsの価値提案を伝え、相互の信頼関係を築きます。
不確実性への対応： 担当エリア（東京、大阪、または地方都市）を戦略的に分析し、細かい指示を待つことなく、インパクトの大きい未開拓の機会を自ら特定します。
部門横断的な連携： オペレーション、マーケティング、アカウントマネジメントの各チームと緊密に連携し、加盟レストランがスムーズにプラットフォームへ移行できるよう支援します。
データを活用した効率化： 社内データシステムやCRMツール（Salesforceなど）を活用し、営業パイプラインの管理、アプローチの優先順位付け、日々のワークフローの構築を行います。
市場の声の代弁： 現場からの加盟店フィードバックを製品開発チームや経営陣に積極的に共有し、ツール、価格モデル、社内プロセスの改善に貢献します。
**求める人物像（必須スキル・経験）**
揺るぎない粘り強さと不屈の精神： 困難や挫折を乗り越えた実績（例：厳しい大学プロジェクト、スポーツ、クラブ活動でのリーダーシップ、顧客対応のアルバイトなど）。「ノー」を交渉の終わりではなく、始まりと捉える姿勢。
自発的な行動力と主体性： 高い自律性を持ち、自発的に行動する意欲があること。目標を与えられ、それを達成するための独自の道筋を自由に描ける環境下で、真価を発揮できる方。
成長志向： 積極的にフィードバックを求め、課題を成長の機会と捉え、変化する環境に素早く適応できる、自ら学び続ける姿勢を持つこと。
優れたコミュニケーション能力と文化的共感力： 多様な地元の飲食店オーナー（家族経営の店舗から地域チェーンまで）に対し、自信を持って提案を行い、積極的に耳を傾け、真摯な関係を築けるよう、ネイティブレベルの日本語能力を有すること。
基本的な英語力： 社内システムを操作し、Uberのグローバルネットワーク内でコミュニケーションを図るための、ビジネスレベルの英語の読解力およびライティングスキル。
**望ましい資格**
活気にあふれ、目標志向の環境（例：ホスピタリティ、テック系スタートアップ、学内就職活動支援、カスタマーサービス）でのインターンシップ、アルバイト、またはリーダーシップ経験。
日本の食文化や外食産業に対する深い情熱と好奇心。
基本的なデータセットや数値を用いて、単純な結論を導き出し、優先順位を整理できること。
最新の営業ツールや CRM ワークフロー（Salesforce など）に精通している、または習得する意欲が高いこと。

title: Machine Learning Engineer II - AV Labs
description:
**About the Role**
Uber is launching AV Labs to accelerate the autonomous technology ecosystem. We're building out a high-velocity team of multi-disciplinary experts to turn real-world operations into high-quality data for our autonomous partners. This team is focused on the hardest problem in AV today: unlocking real-world, long-tail driving data. Autonomy is now a data race—and Uber has an edge: We collect rare, real-world driving data at a scale and capital efficiency no one else can match.
As a ML Engineer, you will be at the forefront of **Physical AI**, building advanced autonomy algorithms and models to add rich semantics to our massive driving data. You will be responsible for the development and implementation of the latest machine learning techniques that enables better data mining, deep scene understanding, and causal modeling of ego vehicle behavior.The ideal candidate will be able to identify complex edge cases, provide robust algorithmic solutions, and set a high technical excellence bar.
**What You Will Do**
- **Algorithm Development**: Develop algorithms and foundation models that extract high-fidelity semantic meaning from complex urban edge cases to enrich our L4 data lake.
- **System Design**: Implement scalable ML systems, including management of upstream sensor dependencies.
- **Dataset Optimization**: Deliver high-quality datasets to accelerate ML technologies through advanced sensor data collection, processing, and auto-labeling.
- **Cross-Functional Collaboration**: Partner with platform, product, and security engineering teams to enable the successful deployment of the latest machine learning techniques into production.
**Basic Qualifications**
- 2+ years of working experience in the ML/Robotics industry.
- Bachelor’s degree (or higher) in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related fields.
- Proficient in Python and Linux environments.
- Familiar with modern AI/ML frameworks (e.g., PyTorch).
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience in the Autonomous Driving domain.
- Proven track record of deploying ML models in safety-critical physical systems.
- Master’s or PhD degree in Computer Vision, Robotics, or Machine Learning.
- Familiarity with C++ and high-performance computing.

title: Staff Software Engineer
description:
The Developer Platform is a foundational layer supporting thousands of services across a global technology organization. You will lead multi-year efforts to reimagine how engineers write, test, and deploy backend services at massive, cross-functional scale.
The work requires deep technical expertise as well as leadership in environments shaped by legacy systems, evolving priorities, and cross-organizational dependencies. This role offers broad organizational influence and the opportunity to shape the everyday experience of thousands of engineers.
While we're particularly interested in candidates with deep experience in strong experience in building developer tools and infra while championing Dev Ex (Developer Experience) to drive the agentic SDLC transformation shaping the way how features are shipped with highest velocity and quality
**Basic Qualifications:**
1.  10+ years of progressive experience in backend, platform, or infrastructure engineering roles within high-scale technology environments.
2.  Demonstrated experience operating at a Staff or Senior Staff or Principal Engineer level, providing technical leadership across multiple teams or domains.
3.  Deep hands-on experience building and operating distributed backend systems at scale.
**Preferred Qualifications:**
1.  Proven track record of designing, implementing, and scaling developer-facing platforms, internal tooling, or foundational CI/CD infrastructure used by large engineering populations
2.  Proven industry engagement, such as contributions to open-source tooling or frameworks, or experience collaborating with external partners on shared technical direction.
3.  Direct experience with large-scale build systems (e.g., Bazel or similar monorepo tooling) and dependency management at scale.
4.  Familiarity with data-intensive workflows and distributed systems, or experience building SDKs and internal libraries for other engineering teams.
5.  Demonstrated comfort leading complex, ambiguous initiatives that require balancing long-term architectural vision with urgent execution needs.
**What will candidate do:**
1.  Define and drive the agentic SDLC vision shaping how all builders at Uber should build features
2.  Lead and implement modernization initiatives across multiple orgs to improve developer productivity, reliability, and scalability within large shared-code or monorepo environments.
3.  Establish technical standards, governance, and consistency by aligning platform direction horizontally across hundreds of engineering teams and multiple geographic locations.
4.  Balance hands-on technical execution (e.g., platform upgrades, automation, dependency management) with strategic architectural thinking, ensuring near-term stability while building toward a long-term vision.
5.  Serve as a technical mentor and leader, elevating engineering craft and decision-making for Staff and Senior Staff engineers within the Developer Platform organization.

title: Senior Engineering Manager
description:
**About the role**
The Online Data Platform team at Uber builds and operates the foundational real-time data systems that power every critical use case across the company. The team owns multiple online data systems such MySQL, Vitess, M3DB, ZK, ETCD, Backup/Restore of storage techs, Real time analytics , Search, Flink and Kafka.
We are looking for a Senior Manager to lead our Vitess and MySQL engineering teams. This is a deeply technical leadership role at the intersection of database internals, distributed systems, and large-scale production operations. You will manage a multi-layer team of Senior Engineers, Tech Lead Managers (TLMs), and Engineering Managers, owning the full technical charter for Uber's relational database platform.
Basic Qualifications
1\. 10+ years of hands-on software engineering experience building and operating large-scale distributed systems, with deep expertise in database systems, online data platforms, or adjacent infrastructure (search, OLAP, streaming).
2\. 4+ years of engineering management experience, including direct management of Senior Engineers and at least one layer of management (Engineering Managers or Tech Lead Managers).
3\. Proficiency in Go and/or Java; strong command of distributed systems fundamentals Demonstrated ability to drive technical strategy and roadmap for complex, high-scale systems with multiple stakeholders and competing priorities.
Preferred Qualifications
1\. Experience in distributed database systems and internals
**What the candidate will do**
1\. Lead, grow, and mentor a multi-layer organisation of Senior Engineers, Tech Lead Managers (TLMs), and Engineering Managers — owning hiring, performance management, career development, and team culture.
2\. Build a high-performing, inclusive engineering team with a strong culture of technical excellence, ownership, high bar for quality and psychological safety.
3\. Define and drive the multi-year technical roadmap for Vitess and MySQL at Uber
4\. Provide hands-on technical oversight: lead architecture reviews, set engineering standards, and maintain a high bar for design quality, reliability, and operational hygiene across all team charters.
5\. Own SLO definition and reliability targets for the Vitess and MySQL platforms; drive incident response culture, runbook discipline, and systematic post-mortem processes.
6\. Partner with stakeholders across Engineering to align on
7\. Represent the team in senior leadership forums and cross-functional reviews;
8\. Engage with the open source Vitess and MySQL ecosystem

title: Merchandising Operations Manager
description:
**About the Role**
Uber Eats is looking for a Merchandising Operations Manager to support the Retail line of business. In this role, you will help create a best-in-class in-app shopping experience through curated assortments, thoughtful content strategies, and operational excellence.
You will be part of a collaborative team focused on scaling local merchandising efforts across Central and South America. This role combines data analysis, experimentation, and cross-functional collaboration to drive impactful customer experiences and support business growth. The ideal candidate is analytical, detail-oriented, adaptable, and excited to improve how customers discover and shop for retail products on Uber Eats.
**What You’ll Do**
- Support the execution of in-app merchandising strategies, including seasonal campaigns, curated landing pages, homepage visibility, and product prioritization.
- Analyze merchandising and selection performance using SQL and other analytical tools to generate insights that inform strategy, optimize campaigns, and improve merchant performance.
- Build dashboards, automate reporting processes, and help develop scalable frameworks to measure content and selection performance.
- Conduct audits and deep dives into user experience (UX), visibility gaps, and operational inconsistencies across cities and countries.
- Support experimentation initiatives by running and interpreting A/B tests and measuring the impact of content or selection changes on customer behavior and conversion.
- Identify opportunities to improve merchant engagement, merchandising quality, and customer journey outcomes through data-driven recommendations.
- Contribute to playbooks, standard operating procedures, and internal documentation that support operational excellence and knowledge sharing.
- Collaborate cross-functionally with teams including Marketing, Sales, Retail, Product, and Operations to localize strategies, test new features, and align on business priorities.
**What You'll Need**
**Basic Qualifications**
- At least 3 years of experience in retail, e-commerce, merchandising, digital marketing, category management, business analysis, or data analysis.
- Strong organizational and project management skills, with attention to detail and the ability to translate data into actionable insights.
- Proficiency in SQL and working knowledge of Google Sheets.\*SQL experience is a MUST for this role.
- Professional proficiency in English, both written and spoken, with the ability to collaborate across regional and global teams.
- Adaptable, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced and evolving environment.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience working in a technology company, startup environment, or the grocery, convenience, or retail industry.
- Familiarity with experimentation frameworks, including A/B testing and performance measurement methodologies.
- Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to clearly explain insights, strategies, and recommendations to stakeholders.
- Experience collaborating across multiple functions and working with teams such as Marketing, Retail, Sales, Product, and Operations.

title: Senior Merchant Strategy & Operations Manager
location: Sydney, New South Wales, Australia; Melbourne, Victoria, Australia
description:
**About The Team**
Our Grocery & Retail business is a startup within Uber, a team focussed on growth and innovation, but with the support and leverage of Uber’s large global ridesharing and delivery businesses. We own the delivery of everything that isn’t restaurant food - think groceries, essentials, alcohol, pharmaceuticals, hardware, electronics, beauty, and more... Many of the opportunities and challenges we face are new for Uber and require creative solutions that cater to different customers, but we’re able to solve them because we care deeply #BuildwithHeart.
**About The Role**
The Grocery & Retail Merchant Operations team’s mission is to enable new selection and enhance selection quality, while delivering a positive platform experience for our customers. As a Senior Merchant Operations Manager, you will work closely with Commercial teams providing thought partnership and technical support, liaise with Product teams to ensure product-market fit for strategic accounts, and implement operational solutions and resources to address local needs.
**What You’ll Need**
- **Product Expertise:** Become an expert on our product suite for Grocery & Retail Merchants on the Uber Eats marketplace - including Uber Eats Manager, Inventory & Catalog platforms, Merchant Invoicing platforms, and Loyalty solutions - assess suitability for different Merchant archetypes (verticals and segments), identify gaps and work with other Ops & Product teams on product improvement
- **Solutions Design:** Understand the needs of strategic accounts and identify and implement solutions to help accelerate go-to-market, enhance merchant propositions, and drive merchant success on Uber Eats, leveraging Ops tools and collaborating with global Product teams
- **Analytics & Insights:** Use data to understand Merchant performance and key improvement areas; support the Account Management team in defining metrics and building dashboards, bespoke reporting solutions and client-facing materials, leveraging the outsourced Analytics team
- **Process Improvement:** Partner with the Commercial team to understand what is required to support strategic accounts, and identify opportunities to scale our efforts through centralised processes, automations and tools
- Business Standards: Establish and maintain best practices for core activities - including Merchant Go-To-Market, Integrations, Loyalty, Reporting, and exchange learnings with global counterparts
**Basic Qualifications**
- 5+ years of experience in management consulting, business operations and/or strategy roles
- An analytical mindset and strong business acumen; you have experience using SQL and advanced excel, and are comfortable translating technical details to business outcomes
- Highly collaborative; you know how to engage work across different stakeholder groups to drive towards shared goals
- A go-getter attitude; you’re willing to take a hands-on approach to get the job done and bias towards action
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Partner-facing; you’ve worked with external stakeholders and are comfortable adapting and presenting materials to a variety of audiences

title: Senior Strategy & Operations Manager, Merchant Selection Strategy & Pricing
description:
##### **About the Role**
As a Merchant Selection and Pricing Sr Manager at Uber Eats Canada, you will play a critical role in shaping our merchant selection strategy and designing pricing to influence merchant success and Uber’s overall business performance.
This role sits at the intersection of strategy, analytics, and commercial execution. You will lead the design and implementation of data-driven strategies that balance selection density (having the right restaurants in the right places) with commercial sustainability. You will be the architect of our merchant portfolio, ensuring we have a competitive, high-quality selection that drives eater frequency while maintaining healthy business economics.
##### **What You’ll Do**
- **Develop Selection Strategy:** Create the strategic framework for merchant acquisition and retention; identify "must-have" selection gaps in specific geographic groups and design the commercial terms needed to win them
- **Lead the Deal Desk:** Manage all new and renewing merchant deals, ensuring they adhere to internal guardrails for pricing, legal compliance, and profitability
- **Optimize Marketplace Mix:** Analyze market dynamics to determine the optimal balance of merchant categories and develop pricing models that reflect the value each merchant brings to the platform
- **Data-Driven Insights:** Analyze performance trends, including merchant churn, price sensitivity, and selection gaps, to surface actionable insights for the Commercial and Operations teams
- **Cross-Functional Leadership:** Collaborate with Commercial, Operations, Finance, and Legal to operationalize pricing and selection decisions, ensuring our "Value Proposition" remains competitive
- **Governance & Reporting:** Build and maintain dashboards that track selection health (e.g., selection parity vs. competitors) and pricing performance to guide executive-level conversations
- **Strategic Initiatives:** Lead projects that improve merchant economics and engagement.
##### **Basic Qualifications**
- 4+ years of professional experience in Strategy & Ops, Commercial, Finance, or Consulting roles
- Proficiency in SQL, Excel, and data visualization tools like Tableau, along with modeling scenario impacts
- Undergraduate degree in Business, Finance, or Engineering
##### **Preferred Qualifications**
- Solid understanding of the restaurant or similar industries and how pricing influences customer behavior, conversion, and profitability
- Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret large datasets and translate them into clear pricing strategies that balance short- and long-term impact
- Proficiency in Python, R and similar tools
- MBA or Masters degree preferred

title: Staff Data Scientist (Fraud, Risk)
description:
**About the Team**
Uber is looking for an experienced and motivated Scientist to join our **Global Safety & Risk Team**. In this role, you will significantly contribute to the physical safety and security of millions of Uber users globally. You will apply your knowledge in data analysis, machine learning, and statistical modeling to generate insights and develop new algorithms that identify and prevent safety incidents before they occur.
This position offers the chance to innovate our technology stack by utilizing the latest breakthroughs in **Predictive Modeling**, **Causal Inference**, and **Real-time Risk Systems** to create intricate autonomous safety frameworks throughout the Uber ecosystem.
**About the Role**
- Conduct thorough analyses of large, imbalanced datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for improving **safety incident detection**.
- Design, implement, and optimize **binary classification models** and algorithms to predict the probability of high-severity incidents.
- Generate actionable insights from risk data and communicate findings to stakeholders, balancing safety interventions with marketplace growth.
- Work as a **thought expert** for your cross-functional partners (Product, Ops, and Engineering), pushing the boundaries of how Uber defines and mitigates risk.
- Design **complex experiments (Diff-in-Diff, Synthetic Controls)** and interpret results to draw impactful conclusions in a marketplace environment where classical A/B testing is often not feasible.
- Define how your cross-functional team measures success by developing **Safety & Risk metrics** (e.g., Recall, Precision-Recall AUC, Probability Calibration) in partnership with global stakeholders.
- Stay current with the latest advancements in **Supervised Learning, Causal Inference, and Anomaly Detection**.
**Technical Skills**
**Required**
- **Senior and/or Staff seniority** working as a Data Scientist, Applied Scientist, or Machine Learning Engineer.
- Experience building and deploying **Binary Classification systems** in production for large-scale, high-stakes applications (e.g., Fraud, Safety, or Risk).
- Deep experience in **Experimental Design** beyond classical A/B testing, including **Quasi-experiments** (Diff-in-Diff, Synthetic Control) or **Marketplace experiments** (Switchbacks).
- Expertise in handling **Extreme Class Imbalance** and optimizing models for rare event detection.
- Experience using **Python** and **SQL** to work with massive, high-dimensional data sets at scale.
- Solid foundation in **Statistical Methodologies** such as probability calibration, sampling, and hypothesis testing.
**Preferred**
- Experience with **Geospatial data analysis** (e.g., H3, S2 geometry).
- Background in **Real-time Inference** systems (working with streaming data like Kafka or Flink).
- Knowledge of **Causal Inference** to measure the incremental impact of safety interventions.
**_Please note:_** _this hybrid position is based in São Paulo or Rio de Janeiro, Brazil - welcoming both local professionals and those open to relocating to those cities._

title: Senior Localization Program Manager, Multilingual AI
description:
**About the role and team**
Uber is a place where digital systems meet the physical world, and our Localization Team is at the center of that intersection. As a Senior Localization Operations Program Manager for ML and GenAI, you will lead the evolution of our multilingual operations, transforming how Uber speaks to millions of users across the globe. This isn't just about managing translations; it’s about building the production-grade infrastructure that integrates Machine Translation and Large Language Models into the very fabric of our global platform.
The work is fast-paced, highly technical, and often ambiguous as we navigate the cutting edge of AI-driven language systems. You will need to balance the "perfect" with the "practical," making critical trade-offs between quality, latency, and cost in a high-stakes environment. We are looking for a systems-thinker who is energized by the challenge of moving experimental capabilities into reliable production workflows and who possesses the grit to unblock complex cross-functional hurdles. If you thrive on owning end-to-end impact and want to shape the future of global communication at scale, this is where you’ll grow.
**What you’ll do**
- Design, build, and scale end-to-end localization workflows that integrate CMS, TMS, and ML/LLM systems to support Uber’s global movement.
- Evaluate and operationalize MT and LLM solutions across high and low-resource languages, leveraging GCP services like Vertex AI and BigQuery to balance performance with cost.
- Partner closely with Engineering and MLOps to translate experimental AI capabilities into production-grade systems, including model hosting, monitoring, and lifecycle management.
- Navigate complex trade-offs between translation quality, turnaround time, and infrastructure costs, providing data-driven recommendations to leadership.
- Lead cross-functional initiatives across global time zones, aligning Product, Data, and Vendor teams to ensure seamless execution and guardrail implementation.
- Drive continuous improvement by establishing quality frameworks that combine automated metrics like COMET or BLEU with structured human evaluation.
**Basic Qualifications**
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Linguistics, or a related field; or 4 years of equivalent professional experience.
- 4+ years of experience in localization operations (CMS/TMS) within a technology or platform environment.
- 2+ years of experience working directly with MT, LLM, or ML-driven language systems in a production setting.
- Experience managing end-to-end technical programs, including scoping, risk management, and cross-functional stakeholder alignment.
- Working knowledge of Google Cloud Platform (GCP) or similar cloud services, including Vertex AI, AutoML, or BigQuery.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- 3+ years of experience leading large-scale MT/LLM transformation programs.
- Hands-on experience fine-tuning LLMs for multilingual use cases or implementing RAG pipelines for grounded translation.
- Proficiency in Python scripting or API integration to automate data flows and reduce operational overhead.
- Demonstrated ability to build data dashboards that track model performance, throughput, and cost-per-word metrics.
- Proven track record of navigating high-ambiguity environments and building frameworks for unique technical challenges.

description:
## **Business Development Representative, Eastern Europe — Uber for Business**
**Location: Warsaw** **Sales & Business Development \| Full-time**
### **About the Role**
**Uber for Business (U4B)** is transforming how organizations move and feed their employees and guests. We are growing exponentially, and we’re looking for a high-energy **Business Development Representative (BDR)** to help us dominate the Northern Eastern European market.
As a BDR, you aren’t just making calls; you’re the engine of our growth. You will work side-by-side with Account Executives to source new opportunities, craft innovative outreach strategies, and build deep partnerships across the region. We need a self-starter who thrives on the "hustle" and relishes a team environment of natural problem-solvers. If you have the drive to sell across Eastern Europe with passion and energy, we want to hear from you.
### **What You’ll Do**
- **Generate Pipeline:** Drive revenue growth through aggressive outbound prospecting, including cold calling, personalized emailing, and social selling.
- **Target Growth:** Identify high-potential business opportunities and navigate complex organizations to find the right decision-makers.
- **Crush Targets:** Meet and exceed assigned activity and pipeline creation KPIs consistently.
- **Innovate Outreach:** Bring a fresh, customer-oriented mindset to prospecting—thinking outside the box to secure meetings with Mid-Market leads.
- **Strategic Alignment:** Partner with Account Executives to iterate on prioritization models, internal tools, and prospecting workflows.
- **Full-Spectrum Communication:** Master the art of the pitch via phone, email, video conferencing, and occasionally in-person interactions.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- **Experience:** 6+ months of B2B sales or business development experience (highly preferred).
- **Education:** Bachelor’s degree required.
- **Execution:** A history of consistently exceeding activity targets and KPIs in a fast-paced environment.
- **Language:** Fluent Polish and English
- **Communication:** Exceptional interpersonal skills with the ability to lead mutually beneficial conversations with executive prospects.
- **Skillset:** Proficiency in building and managing multi-channel email sequences and managing your own time/projects with precision.
### **Preferred Qualifications**
- **Hunter Mentality:** A proven track record of outbound success using cold calling, email campaigns, and social outreach.
- **Tech Savvy:** Proficiency with the modern sales stack, specifically **Salesforce, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, ZoomInfo, and Groove**.
- **Adaptability:** Experience supporting multiple Account Executives and the ability to juggle shifting priorities without losing momentum.
- **Self-Starter Mindset:** A proactive approach to learning, with an obsession for exceeding goals and improving processes.

- **Run disciplined sales** **Ops**: account plans, Salesforce tracking, pipeline management, and conversion ownership.
- **Execute strategy locally**: adapt HQ priorities to each franchisee using data and local insights.
- **Continuously improve**: analyze performance, refine approach, and optimize results week over week.
- **Support team growth**: share best practices and act as a reference for peers.
- **Leverage data**: build insights, validate recommendations, and demonstrate impact.
- **Lead business reviews**: align on performance, priorities, and secure commitments.
- **Represent the field internally**: share insights with Ops, Product, and Marketing.
**Basic qualifications**
- **5+ years** in account management, consultative sales, or business development (book-of-business model preferred).
- **Strong sales skills**: structured discovery, pitching, closing, and proven impact on conversion metrics.
- Highly **organized and autonomous**, able to manage a large portfolio effectively.
- **Solid CRM discipline** (Salesforce or similar) and pipeline management.
- **Strong negotiation skills** and ability to influence cross-functionally.
- **Analytical mindset** with good Excel/GSheets skills; able to build business cases independently.
- Fluent in French & English; based in Paris with willingness to travel.
- **Bonus**: experience in franchise networks, QSR, restaurant tech, digital media, or ads platforms.
- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience.

title: Counsel, Product & Commercial, South Cone
description:
**About the Role**
We are seeking an exceptional product and commercial lawyer who excels in fast-paced, high-impact environments, partnering closely with Ops teams and enterprise sales organizations. In this role, you will play a central part in driving Uber’s strategic growth, leading complex product and commercial matters for our Mobility and Delivery businesses across the Southern Cone (Argentina, Chile, Paraguay, and Uruguay). You will report to the Legal Director for the Southern Cone and be based in Santiago, Chile.
As a trusted, high-impact adviser, you will navigate an evolving legal and regulatory landscape, delivering pragmatic and forward-thinking solutions that shape how we operate in one of Uber’s most critical priority markets globally. You will be expected to balance legal risk with long-term, sustainable growth, while leading transformative initiatives that have a tangible impact on our business and the communities we serve.
Success in this role requires the ability to collaborate seamlessly with diverse cross-functional stakeholders and to provide clear, strategic, and actionable legal guidance that enables confident decision-making and successful product launches. The ideal candidate will also bring strong commercial contracting expertise, supporting innovative, scalable solutions that move both our business—and the world—forward.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
- Provide proactive legal guidance to product and sales teams, enabling the design, rollout, and scaling of Mobility and Delivery offerings across the Southern Cone.
- Structure, draft, and negotiate a broad range of commercial agreements, including Terms & Conditions, supporting key partnerships and revenue-generating initiatives.
- Work closely with cross-functional stakeholders—including operations, policy, compliance, and business leaders—to align legal strategies with commercial priorities.
- Anticipate and manage legal and regulatory exposure in a rapidly evolving environment, ensuring thoughtful risk-taking that supports long-term growth.
- Contribute to regional and global projects with significant business impact, helping shape how Uber operates and expands in a critical market.
**Basic Qualifications**
- +8 years of experience at a top-notch law firm or in-house
- Strong commercial contracts, marketing, and consumer law training and experience, including dealing with international contracts and corporate law.
- Strong academic credentials and a law degree from a Chilean university
- Excellent business judgment, communication, and interpersonal skills
- Think on your feet and make quick decisions
- Thrive in high impact/high profile situations
- Fluency in Spanish and English
- Enthusiastic about Uber
**Preferred Qualifications**
- If you hold an LL.M Degree or master’s degree.
- If you maintain up-to-date knowledge of technology matters.

title: Senior Operations & Logistics Manager - Southern Europe Rider – Airports
description:
### **About the Role**
Airports and travel hubs are Uber’s first touchpoint for travelers and represent our largest strategic growth opportunity. As the Senior Operations Manager for Southern Europe, you will own the performance of one of Uber’s most high-impact mobility use cases across **Italy, Greece, and Malta**. You will bridge the gap between digital product and physical operations, building best-in-class partnerships and scaling seamless on-the-ground experiences.
### **What You’ll Do**
- **Strategic Partner Management:** Act as the primary lead for senior-level relationships with Airport Executives and commercial teams. You will own the full partnership lifecycle—from initial deal sizing and negotiation of commercial agreements to long-term joint business planning and roadmap development.
- **Performance & Growth Ownership:** Own the end-to-end P&L for Airports and Travel Hubs in your region. You will quantify and prioritize growth opportunities through rigorous modeling, ensuring we maximize rider acquisition, throughput, and revenue generation.
- **Cross-Functional Leadership:** Serve as the regional "quarterback" for travel initiatives. You will coordinate with Product, Marketing, and Business Development to drive localized marketing campaigns, implement product rollouts, and ensure operational accountability across all internal stakeholders.
- **Physical Experience Optimization:** Translate Uber’s digital excellence into the physical world. You will oversee pickup zone design, signage, and wayfinding strategy to ensure a frictionless transition from the terminal to the vehicle.
- **Operational Excellence:** Drive on-the-ground execution through regular site audits, billing management, and feedback loops. You will identify local pain points for riders and drivers, deploying rapid solutions and pilots (e.g., new pickup flows or in-app features) to maintain a premium brand perception.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- **6+ years of experience** in operations, strategy, consulting, or business development.
- **Partnership Mastery:** Strong negotiation skills and a track record of managing complex, high-stakes external stakeholders.
- **Analytical Rigor:** Proficiency in data-driven decision-making, with the ability to build business cases and translate complex datasets into actionable strategy.
- **Operational Execution:** Experience managing field operations or physical "on-the-ground" implementation in fast-paced environments.
- **Industry Knowledge:** Experience within mobility, travel, or complex marketplace businesses is highly preferred.
### **Bonus Points**
- Fluency in **Italian** or **Greek**.
- Experience navigating regulatory landscapes or commercial airport contracts in Southern Europe.

title: Enterprise & Strategic Account Manager
description:
**About the Role**
We’re looking for a highly motivated, analytical and relationship-driven Enterprise & Strategic Account Manager to help grow and retain a select portfolio of strategic restaurant partners with national coverage. You’ll be at the center of helping our most sophisticated partners thrive on the Uber Eats platform - driving strategic conversations, identifying growth opportunities, and improving operational health. This is a client-facing, individual contributor role where impact, autonomy, and customer success converge.
**What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points**
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
1. Own a high-value book of business: Manage a small portfolio of ultra-high value accounts, focusing on business strategy, revenue and profitability growth, operational health and client satisfaction.
2. Drive growth through consultative strategy: develop Joint Business Plans and support product adoption and location expansion.
3. Champion client success and health: Monitor operational metrics like defect rate, order reject rate, and ad spend efficiency. Take direct action to improve partner performance and experience.
4. Be the go-to expert for your partners: Serve as the trusted advisor—resolving conflicts, providing platform insights, and guiding partners toward business excellence.
5. Collaborate cross-functionally: Work with internal stakeholders like CSMs, Specialists, and Product teams to deliver holistic support and tailored solutions.
6. Turn feedback into action: Synthesize partner insights into recommendations that influence Uber’s product and commercial roadmap.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. 4-7 years of experience in a B2B, client-facing role (e.g. Account Management, Sales, Customer Success, or senior retail/foodservice leadership)
2. Demonstrated ability to drive commercial success across the full sales lifecycle - from identifying opportunities to retaining and expanding accounts
3. Proven track record of building strong client relationships and delivering measurable impact, both commercial and operational
4. Strategic thinker with strong analytical and problem-solving skills; you love diving into data to uncover insights
5. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with a knack for navigating complex conversations and aligning stakeholders
6. Self-starter mindset with the ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment
7. Native Portuguese and fluent in english
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. SQL
2. Consulting experience
3. Comfortable with CRM tools (Salesforce preferred) and Google Workspace

title: Partner Manager, Uber Advertising - Travel & Financial Services
location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Chicago, Illinois, United States
description:
About the Role
Advertising at Uber means navigating ambiguity, earning trust fast, and solving problems that don’t come with a script. As a Partner Manager for our Travel and Financial Services verticals, you aren't just pushing a product; you are building long-term partnerships in a fast-changing environment where performance and client satisfaction are the only metrics that matter.
This role is high-stakes and grounded in the messy reality of doing big things at scale. You’ll lead with impact by connecting major brands to our global user base, but you’ll also face the challenge of shifting priorities, complex executions, and the need to make smart decisions with imperfect information. We are looking for someone with an owner mindset who is energized by the pressure of managing a campaign lifecycle from first kick-off to final reporting. If you thrive in a "go-get-it" environment where excellence is the norm and you're ready to help write the playbook rather than just follow one, this is where you’ll grow.
What You'll Do
- Partner with our ad sales teams to develop and manage ad partnership strategies that deliver on our partners’ needs, business objectives and exceed quarterly growth goals
- Develop and maintain a strong understanding of clients’ marketing goals and challenges and proactively recommend solutions that support them
- Manage internal and external kick off calls to understand campaign specifications and discuss complex executions
- Manage the entire campaign lifecycle including creative showcase and best practices
- Proactively monitor campaign performance to pinpoint, communicate and solve issues
- Coordinate the launch, management and reporting of campaigns; while proactively communicating updates and setting expectations with all stakeholders
- Employ critical thinking and creative problem-solving skills to navigate relationships and difficult partner conversations.
- Uncover opportunities to drive better performance and increased investment (upsell) by delivering data-backed insights and recommendations to our partners
- Bring your analytical A-game. Develop methodologies to highlight trends and provide meaningful insights to partners during the campaign lifecycle
- Be the voice of the customer and effectively communicate product limitations and feedback to relevant internal teams
- Drive initiatives. You will help support the team lead to build best practice playbooks across our Ads team to drive success and efficiency. This involves wearing many hats and jumping in to help your teammates when the needs arise
- Collaborate effectively across cross-functional internal sales, product, and support teams to include Ad Ops, Insights & Measurement and other relevant parties.
Basic Qualifications
- 4+ years of full-time experience
- Experience in end-to-end large customer advertising account management
- Experience in digital advertising account management and/or sales
- Understanding of performance and brand marketing media channels, ad products, and core measurement metrics that are relevant to these
- Bachelor's Degree or equivalent
Preferred Qualifications
- Experience managing large-scale ads campaigns and partnering with large, complex advertisers
- Experience building relationships with key marketing decision makers, brand digital advertising managers and agency partners
- Ability to flex your analytical skills and utilize data to quantify opportunities and influence decision makers
- Excellent listening, communication, and organizational skills
- Excellent negotiation and problem solving skills
- Speed, resourcefulness, proactivity and a go-getter mentality. You are comfortable working in a fast-paced environment and navigating ambiguity
- SQL and other data qualifications a plus
