title: Engineering Manager II, Evaluation & Simulation - AV Labs
description:
**About the Role**
Uber is launching AV Labs to accelerate the autonomous technology ecosystem. We’re building out a high-velocity team of multi-disciplinary experts to turn real-world operations into high-quality data for our autonomous partners. This team will be focused on the hardest problem in AV today: unlocking real-world, long-tail driving data.
Autonomy is now a data race–and Uber has an edge: We collect rare, real-world driving data at a scale and capital efficiency no one else can match (millions of Uber trips every hour across cities, conditions, and edge cases create the data autonomy has been missing). We will build platforms that harness scale and real-world complexity to reimagine how the world moves.
You will be an engineering manager II in AV Labs and involved in the development and implementation of the latest technology for self-driving stacks. The ideal candidate will be able to identify issues, provide solutions and implement the fixes as well as setting a high technical excellence bar in all things we do.
**What You Will Do**
- Define the roadmap for AV evaluation and simulation.
- Define evaluation metrics for AV stack and drive technical architecture design.
- Communicate progress, opportunities and risks to leadership.
- Hire a high performing team. Raise the bar on engineering quality.
**Basic Qualifications:**
- 3 years of experience in software engineering management.
- 3 years of experience in the AV industry.
- 8 years of experience in the software engineering industry.
- Bachelor degree in computer science, computer engineering or related fields.
- Proficient in AV evaluation metrics and simulation technologies.
**Preferred Qualifications:**
- 5 years of experience in software engineering management.
- Experience in the other AV domains.
For Sunnyvale, CA-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$232,000 per year - USD$258,000 per year.
You will be eligible to participate in Uber's bonus program, and may be offered an equity award & other types of comp. All full-time employees are eligible to participate in a 401(k) plan. You will also be eligible for various benefits. More details can be found at the following link [https://jobs.uber.com/en/benefits](https://jobs.uber.com/en/benefits).

title: Operations & Logistics Manager
description:
## **Operations & Logistics Manager: Austria & Switzerland**
At Uber, we’re reimagining the way the world moves for the better. We are looking for an **Operations & Logistics Manager** to join our team in the DACH sub-region, with a specific focus on driving our growth in **Austria**.
This isn’t just a desk jobit’s an intense, high-impact role that combines savvy analytics with on-the-ground execution. You will be the architect of our marketplace, ensuring we have the right pricing, technology, and driver supply to keep our cities moving.
The outcome will be measurable (growing trips and revenues, improving marketplace reliability, growing supply) and will be directly impacted by your daily actions. You will help support decision-making within your team through your critical thinking, strategic behavior, and data-driven approach.
The Operations Manager role will need to develop robust and streamlined processes, work on delivering tech and product launches, ensure marketplace health, and attract, convert, engage, and retain riders and partners facing a multitude of complex needs and bottlenecks. This requires a dedication to operational excellence, a deep understanding of driver lifecycle and economics, and a customer-centric attitude.
### **What You’ll Do**
- **Strategize & Execute:** Collaborate with Local Operations teams and GMs to outline and implement Uber’s strategy across the DACH region.
- **Analyze the Marketplace:** Deep dive into data sets to identify product and pricing needs, getting to the root cause of operational bottlenecks.
- **Build & Scale:** Create streamlined processes that allow the business to grow efficiently across regions while maintaining marketplace reliability.
- **Optimize Performance:** Continuously monitor business metrics and KPIs, building dashboards to steer performance and improve the driver lifecycle.
- **Stay Compliant:** Rapidly adapt to and adhere to evolving regulatory requirements within the sector.
- **Collaborate:** Work closely with cross-functional teams and external stakeholders to ensure our local growth needs are met.
### **What You’ll Need**
**Basic Qualifications:**
- **Experience:** 2+ years of professional experience using analytics to solve complex business problems.
- **Technical Skills:** Proficiency in **SQL** is a must.
- **Language:** Professional proficiency in both **German and English**.
- **Mindset:** A data-driven decision-making mentality coupled with sound business judgment.
**Bonus Points:**
- **The "Doer" Attitude:** You are a self-starter who naturally mobilizes people, resolves problems, and goes above and beyond.
- **Project Mastery:** Experience owning projects end-to-end, managing multiple initiatives in parallel, and crafting optimized processes.
- **Relationship Building:** Background in partnership management or business development with strong stakeholder management skills.
- **Adaptability:** The ability to thrive and take initiative in a constantly changing work environment.
### **Why Join Us?**
You will have the opportunity to drive measurable impact every single daygrowing trips, increasing revenue, and improving the lives of our riders and partners. If you are ready to lean into execution and tackle the complex needs of a modern marketplace, we want to hear from you.

title: City Operations Manager, Uber Eats, Belgium
description:
### About the Role
As the **City Operations Manager** for Belgium, you are the strategic architect of our growth and expansion. This is a high-impact, autonomous role designed for a "hands-on" strategist who can pivot between deep data analysis and ground-level execution.
You will act as the primary bridge between local commercial and operations teams. Your mission is to ensure the operational roadmap is achieved by influencing stakeholders, taking smart risks on new innovations, and navigating the complex competitive landscape of the Belgian market. This role requires a blend of consulting rigor with a passion for "boots on the ground" operational execution.
### What You Will Do
**\- Launch & Expansion Strategy:** Define and execute a data-driven vision for Uber Eats Belgium. Synthesize interconnected KPIs—balancing growth and penetration with marketplace efficiency—to align local teams under a common goal.
**\- Local Connector & Stakeholder Management:** Own the strategy and alignment across all key stakeholders. Drive the country strategy forward by influencing local operations and commercial teams to meet ambitious targets without direct reporting lines.
**\- Innovation & Smart Risks:** Become an innovator at the core. Use daily local insights to identify opportunities, work with Strategic Finance to build business cases, secure buy-in, and execute proof-of-concepts (POCs).
**\- Regional Bridge & Thought Leadership:** Develop a forward-looking understanding of the competitive landscape, regulatory trends, and macroeconomic factors to proactively position Belgium for success and scale local playbooks regionally.
**\- Operational Excellence:** Stay close to the SMB (Small and Medium Business) landscape while maintaining a heavy focus on operations, ensuring that timeliness, order quality, and reliability standards are met at scale.
### Basic Qualifications
**\- Experience:** 2–3 years of high-quality experience in Operations, Management Consulting, Investment Banking, or Strategy & Planning.
**\- Strategic Execution:** A proven track record of operationalizing strategy and delivering measurable results in a fast-paced environment.
**\- Data-Driven Decision Making:** Highly comfortable making difficult, data-backed decisions. You can clearly articulate the "why" behind complex tradeoffs to senior and local stakeholders.
**\- Quantitative Appetite:** Strong analytical skills with a high proficiency in **SQL** and Excel/Google Sheets to derive actionable insights and build scalable tools.
**\- Hands-on Approach:** A resourceful "doer" who is comfortable moving from high-level narrative building to the granular details of day-to-day operations.
**\- Languages:** Fluency in **English** and either **French** or **Dutch**.

description:
**About the Role**
The Territory Consumer Operations team (formerly Eater) sits within Consumer Operations and is responsible for owning and driving territory and market-level consumer performance. This team operationalizes consumer growth strategy, generates actionable insights, and leads execution across markets to deliver against Territory and US growth goals.
Territory Consumer Operations is accountable for key consumer input metrics at the market and territory level to drive consumer acquisition, engagement, retention. While this team does not own every executional lever, it is responsible for ensuring performance outcomes are met by driving alignment, prioritization, and execution across cross-functional partners.
This team serves as the bridge between strategy and execution—translating US priorities into local action and elevating market insights to inform scaled solutions.
**What You'll Do**
- **Strategy Execution:** Execute market and territory-level strategic initiatives to drive performance and achieve input targets aligned to the growth framework
- **Performance Analytics:** Monitor and analyze territory performance; build and maintain dashboards to surface actionable insights
- **Problem Solving:** Conduct ad hoc analyses to identify opportunities, diagnose issues, and inform local decision-making
- **Consumer Operations Collaboration:** Partner closely with Consumer Operations pods to support scaled execution, inform product strategy and prioritization, and drive strong US and territory performance
- **Cross-Functional Partnership:** Partner with Territory GMs, Market Intelligence, Finance, and other teams (Tech, Brand Marketing, CRM, Performance Marketing, Legal) to shape strategy and prioritize initiatives
- **Business Ownership:** Share accountability for territory and market input targets, contributing to broader US business goals
- **Local Expertise:** Develop deep understanding of local market dynamics to inform strategy and support both territory-specific and scaled initiatives
- **Operational Excellence (BAU):** Own and optimize ongoing business-as-usual operations to ensure consistent execution and performance
**Basic Qualifications**
- 7+ years of experience in strategy, operations, consulting, or similar analytical roles.
- Bachelor's degree in a quantitative, business, or technical field.
- Experience in financial modeling, P&L management, capital allocation frameworks, or lifecycle segmentation and consumer strategy.
- Experience with SQL
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Demonstrated ability to lead complex, highly-ambiguous, cross-functional programs from strategy through execution, unifying diverse concepts (e.g., Finance metrics and Marketing segments).
- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; fluency in SQL and Excel/Google Sheets is highly preferred.
- Proven experience using data and insights to define customer segments, influence lifecycle strategy, and guide investment decisions.
- Highly effective communicator and collaborator; skilled at aligning diverse executive stakeholders and frontline teams across functions and levels.
- Strong bias for action and ownership; able to balance strategic big-picture thinking with hands-on delivery and operational detail.

title: Head of Marketplace, Mobility, Southern Europe
description:
**Head of Marketplace, Mobility (Southern Europe)**
At Uber, we’re reimagining the way the world moves for the better. We bridge the digital and real worlds, powering movement and shaping communities. For us, that starts with helping people go anywhere and get anything from takeout and groceries to rides in cars, on bikes, and scooters. We do it at the "speed of now," and we do it for millions of people who rely on our platform every day.
### **About the Role**
Our Southern Europe Operations team works where Uber’s technology meets the real world. We own the business outcomes for Italy, Greece, and Malta, bridging our global strategy with local market needs.
As the **Head of Marketplace**, you will sit at the core of our local operations, reporting directly to the local General Manager. You will lead the team in charge of pricing and incentives across three countries and multiple cities. Your mission is to use data and operational levers to ensure Uber offers the most competitive, reliable, and convenient service across Southern Europe.
### **What You’ll Do**
- **Problem-Solving:** Identify root causes and implement scalable solutions to marketplace reliability and efficiency challenges.
- **Pricing and investments**: manage pricing, prepare and execute price adjustment recommendations and launch new pricing technologies; design and execute rider and driver investment strategies
- **Data Analytics:** Use business modeling, statistical techniques, and experimentation to impact business outcomes.
- **Team Management:** Lead and inspire a team of four highly motivated professionals.
- **Competition**: monitor competition and contribute to creation and execution of competitive strategy
- **Regional Leadership:** Join the Southern Europe Leadership Team, contributing to strategic decisions and sharing accountability for Mobility goals.
- **Project Execution:** Plan and lead multiple projects within a constantly changing, complex environment.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- **Experience:** At least 8 years in a fast-paced, strategic, data-driven role (e.g., management consulting, investment banking, or data analytics).
- **Leadership:** Minimum 4 years of experience managing people.
- **Strategic Mindset:** Ability to move comfortably between big-picture strategy and granular operational details.
- **Adaptability:** High curiosity, eagerness to learn, and a "get-things-done" attitude in a complex matrix organization.
- **Communication:** Excellent skills to discuss and present strategies to senior executives.
- **Languages:** Fluency in **English** and at least one of either **Italian** or **Greek**.
- **Education:** Master’s degree in Engineering, Economics, Math, Physics, Statistics, or equivalent life experience.
### **Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience working within digital platforms or double-sided marketplaces.
- Hands-on experience in data analytics using tools such as **SQL, R, or Python**.
* * *
> **Ready to help us move Southern Europe?**We are looking for someone who enjoys finding patterns in data and connecting them with the real-world feedback of our riders and drivers.

title: 2026 Account Management Intern, Amsterdam
description:
### **About the Role and Team**
This internship sits at the commercial heart of Uber Eats. You'll join an Account Management team and take on real ownership of partner performance across a portfolio of restaurant brands.
Account Management at Uber Eats is fundamentally a commercial function. Your job is to grow revenue for partners and for Uber. That means you'll be working with data daily, running business reviews, identifying where performance is lagging and why, and translating that analysis into concrete recommendations that move the needle.
This isn't an observational role. From week one, you'll carry a workstream, supper the team with a set of accounts, and be expected to show up to conversations with a point of view grounded in data. This internship offers hands-on exposure to commercial operations, account management, customer success, and cross-functional project management in a fast-paced environment.
**What You’ll Do**
## **Commercial Performance & Partner Growth**
- Support the AM team across a set of accounts, tracking performance against commercial KPIs: GMV, order volume, conversion rate, basket size, and customer retention.
- Engage directly with restaurant partners to conduct churn-prevention interviews and gather qualitative feedback on the platform experience, identify growth opportunities and recommend commercial solutions.
- Support the sale of Ads & Offers products by articulating their value and helping partners adopt them.
- Manage outbound partner conversations with clear commercial objectives.
- Build the narrative behind the numbers translating data into a story that drives partner action.
## **Data Analysis & Insight Generation**
- Run structured performance diagnostics: identify where a partner is underperforming versus benchmark and why.
- Develop and test hypotheses about what drives partner revenue then work with the team to act on them.
- SQL exposure is a plus but not required; comfort with Excel or Google Sheets and a structured analytical mindset is essential.
## **Marketing Execution & Demand Generation**
- Execute performance marketing campaigns promotions, visibility boosts, pricing experiments  and rigorously track ROI.
- Identify where marketing investment will generate the highest incremental return for a given partner.
- Analyse post-campaign data to draw conclusions and feed learnings back into future strategy.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program (e.g., Business Administration, Economics, Marketing, Operations, Data Analytics, or related fields), with graduation by early 2027.
- Available for a full-time, **6-month internship starting 7th September 2026**
- Proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets, including data extraction and analysis
- **Native in Dutch** and fluency in English
- Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
- Open to exploring full-time opportunities with Uber following the internship in case a role becomes available.
# **Who Thrives Here**
The interns who get the most from this role arrive curious about the numbers, comfortable being challenged on their thinking, and genuinely interested in how commercial relationships work. They take ownership early, ask sharp questions, and know the difference between being busy and having impact.
If that sounds like you, we’d like to meet you

title: Account Representative, SMB UberEats, Northern California (SF-Based, Bilingual Required)
description:
**About the Role**
The Account Representative role is a great opportunity to demonstrate your sales skills and abilities in order to expand the number of merchants that work with Uber Eats. Who are we looking for? Someone who is natural at selling and knows where the best eats are around town, passionate about food and Uber, and enjoys negotiating to build the best partnerships.
The primary focus will be bringing on new small-and-medium sized merchants (not necessarily only restaurants) that fit our partnership criteria, and you’ll use your skills in emailing, cold-calling, and driving internal processes to set up our partnerships for success.
**What You'll Do**
- Leverage prospecting skills and relationships to build partnerships with merchants in your geographic region
- Manage contract negotiations aimed at establishing the foundation of a strong working relationship with our merchants, including sustainable economics for both parties
- Clearly communicate the value of Uber and its newest products to prospects
- Achieve and consistently exceed monthly targets
- Partner with our Operations and Account Managers to smoothly activate the best merchants
- Help us identify and build best practices
- Develop long-term relationships with key in-region partners
- Act as an ambassador of Uber Eats' mission, brand, and product
- Proactively maintain communication channels with prospects via email, phone, and face-to-face meetings
**Basic Qualifications**
- Minimum 1 year of experience in sales, account management, or similar client-facing functions
- Fluency in a second language is required
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience with CRM systems (Salesforce, Hubspot, etc) and managing an organized pipeline
- E-commerce or restaurant/hospitality commercial experience
- Multiple languages is a plus!
- Effective communication and sales skills via writing, phone, and in-person
- Strong critical thinking ability

title: Head of Entertainment & Media, Uber Advertising
location: Los Angeles, California, United States; New York, New York, United States
description:
**About the Team**
At Uber, we’ve spent the last three years building and scaling a fast-growing Uber Advertising division focused on unlocking the next era of advertising across our global platforms. With a foundation in place, Uber Advertising now empowers merchants, brands, and advertisers to connect with a highly engaged user base across our Mobility and Delivery apps in 32 markets.
**About the Role**
Our Entertainment & Media ads business is a key driver of this success, helping leading brands reach customers across the Uber ecosystem. As we enter our next phase of growth, we're looking for a dynamic leader to take our Entertainment & Media advertising efforts to new heights.
As Head of Entertainment & Media, you will be responsible for managing a high-performing sales and account management organization, rolling into our Head of Auto, Gaming & Entertainment & Media. This team drives revenue across Uber’s Delivery and Mobility platforms, managing an established and growing book of business. You’ll lead our Entertainment & Media Uber Advertising strategy, focusing on strengthening client partnerships, unlocking new growth opportunities, and operationalizing for long-term success.
**What You'll Do**
- 1\. Own and evolve the Entertainment & Media Ads sales and account management strategy with a focus on sustainable growth, client retention, and cross-platform expansion.
- Lead and inspire a high-performing team of Client Partners and Partner Managers, setting clear goals, tracking performance, and coaching top talent.
- Drive continued revenue growth from a maturing business by deepening relationships with key advertisers and unlocking incremental spend opportunities.
- Partner closely with cross-functional leaders (including Marketing, Product, Operations, and Analytics) to align on strategy, priorities, and customer experience.
- Optimize internal processes and tools to scale execution, improve performance visibility, and ensure strong operational rigor.
- Inform product strategy by working closely with the Ads Business Leads & Ads Leadership — advocating for features and solutions that enhance advertiser outcomes across awareness, trial, and conversion.
- Shape go-to-market plans and collateral in partnership with Ads Global Partnership Solutions, ensuring a consistent message across external channels and field teams.
**Basic Qualifications**
- Minimum of 8 years of experience
- Leadership experience within a digital media platform, enterprise advertiser, or agency.
- A track record of building and managing a multi-million dollar advertising business.
- Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience at or working with an Entertainment or Media company.
- Be customer obsessed (users and merchants) and be passionate about building great experiences for Uber users all over the world.
- Focus on building businesses, not features. Experience building and leading businesses, including ownership and optimization of business performance metrics
- Be able to work cross functionally with many other roles and stakeholders in the company
- Exceptional communication skills, both within your team and with leadership and partners
- Entrepreneurial spirit and a thirst for learning

title: Account Management Lead, Uber Eats - Lyon
description:
**About the Role**
As the **Account Management Lead for the Rhône-Alpes Hub**, you will be the driving force behind our success in one of France’s most dynamic regions. Reporting to the East Territory Lead, you will lead, coach, and inspire a team of Account Managers (AM) dedicated to growing our partnerships with the best restaurants in the area.
Your mission is to bridge the gap between high-level strategy and local execution. You will ensure your team prioritizes the right levers to drive business performance, maintains healthy marketplace metrics, and delivers an exceptional experience to our restaurant partners. This role is a unique blend of **people leadership**, **strategic planning**, **and hands-on operational excellence**.
\-\-\-\- What You Will Do ----
1. **Lead & coach our local team:** Manage a team of Account Managers. You will be responsible for their professional development, performance tracking, and daily guidance to ensure they hit their objectives.
2. **Strategic oversight:** Own the business performance of the Rhône-Alpes Hub. Define local priorities, set clear roadmaps, and ensure your team is focused on the high-impact actions that fuel Uber Eats' growth.
3. **High-stakes negotiations:** Support your AMs in complex negotiations with top-tier restaurant partners and local icons.
4. **Data analysis**: Uber wouldn't be Uber without all of our data! You will autonomously create analyses and dashboards and help your team "crunch the numbers" to identify gaps and opportunities.
5. **Stakeholder management:** Act as the local ambassador for the Rhône-Alpes hub. Work closely with the central teams in Paris to align local initiatives with the national strategy.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. At least 5 years of professional experience in account management, consulting, strategy work in a large business or fast=growing startup
2. Proven experience in team management or project leadership. You know how to motivate a team, provide constructive feedback, and drive collective results.
3. Strong data-driven mindset. You are comfortable using data to build dashboards, track performance, and translate complex insights into simple, actionable team priorities.
4. Exceptional interpersonal skills in French (native/fluent) and English. You can influence both internal stakeholders and external partners.
5. A "go get it" attitude. You thrive in ambiguous environments and can pivot quickly when priorities shift.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. A deep understanding of the Rhône-Alpes business landscape and a genuine passion for its restaurant industry.
2. Ability to see the "big picture" while staying obsessed with the small operational details that make a marketplace successful.

title: Head of Merchant Operations (m/w/d), Uber Eats, Germany
description:
**About the Role**
As the **Head of Merchant Operations (m/w/d)** for **Germany**, you will be the strategic lead responsible for the success, growth, and performance of our entire national merchant portfolio. Based in our Berlin office, you will sit at the center of the German marketplace, obsessing over the merchant lifecycle—from onboarding and catalog quality to retention and compliance.
This is a high-impact leadership role requiring a blend of operational excellence, commercial strategy, and people management. You will partner directly with the General Manager to define the national roadmap, build a high-performing team of "rockstars," and ensure our systems scale as rapidly as our business.

title: Program Manager II, Site Technology
description:
**About the Role**
As a Program Manager for Site Technology at Uber, you will spearhead high-impact, org-wide initiatives focused on implementation and optimization of Audio/Visual (A/V), Network, and on-prem IT technologies across our global footprint of over 300 offices. Based within the Digital Experience team, you will own and drive project execution for integrating these critical infrastructure technologies.
Your primary focus will be leading projects to ensure seamless and standardized deployment of A/V, network, and on-prem IT solutions. You will work closely with Workplace and business leaders to align cross-functional teams and ensure delivery with measurable outcomes.
You will champion efficient execution, track progress against key metrics, proactively resolve blockers, manage risks, and communicate effectively with leadership and stakeholders. This role requires a blend of technical understanding, strategic thinking, and the ability to energize teams towards ambitious goals, ultimately ensuring Uber's physical spaces are technologically robust and efficient.
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
1. Lead end-to-end delivery of strategic technology programs that enhance A/V, Network, and on-prem IT infrastructure across global sites.
2. Translate business and engineering objectives into actionable roadmaps, clear milestones, and measurable outcomes.
3. Ensure programs adhere to standards and best practices defined by the cross-functional IT engineering pillars (A/V, Network, Compute, and Security).
4. Identify cases where existing standards do not fit; work directly with engineering teams to design, validate, and implement scalable alternative solutions.
5. Coordinate multiple project workstreams, proactively managing dependencies, risks, and execution challenges to maintain delivery momentum.
6. Collaborate with regional and global partners to ensure consistency, alignment, and technical integrity across all deployments.
7. Manage program budgets and forecasts, ensuring cost-effective delivery and transparency in financial reporting.
8. Develop and maintain technical program documentation, playbooks, and process frameworks to enable repeatable and scalable execution.
9. Provide clear, data-driven updates to leadership on program health, progress, risks, and business impact.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. Proven track record as a Program Manager, successfully delivering complex, cross-functional technical programs, ideally in a large-scale enterprise environment with global offices.
2. Direct, practical experience with A/V systems, corporate networking (LAN/WAN), and on-prem IT infrastructure (e.g., server rooms, access points).
3. Solid technical aptitude; ability to understand technical challenges, architecture, and risks associated with IT infrastructure implementations.
4. Excellent communication (written and verbal), stakeholder management, and influencing skills across technical and non-technical audiences.
5. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; ability to make data-driven decisions objectively.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Experience with on-prem IT infrastructure projects for large, distributed organizations.
2. Understanding of network topologies and A/V control systems.
3. Degree in a relevant technical field or certifications (e.g., PMP, ITIL, CCNA, CTS).
4. Experience in change management related to technology adoption and new system rollouts.
5. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment with frequent changes and tight deadlines.

title: Account Representative - Uber Eats - Miami
description:
**About the Role**
Account Executives at Uber Eats bring the best local and regional small and medium restaurants to the Uber Eats platform. Who are we looking for? Someone who is natural at selling and knows the best local restaurants, passionate about food and Uber, and enjoys negotiating.
In this role you will lead the full sales cycle and sign partnerships with top local restaurants while being a champion for the Uber and Uber Eats brands. Your success in this role is measured by achieving and exceeding your monthly quota and proactively building your pipeline by managing and hitting the weekly metrics (cold-calls, emails, and field appointments).
**What You'll Do**
- Own and manage a pipeline of local small and medium restaurants to sign new partnerships in your geographic region with a mix of cold-calling (at least 50 dials per day) and in-person meetings
- Create a great first-impression and act as an ambassador of Uber Eats to develop long-term partnerships and clearly communicate the value of Uber
- Manage contract negotiation to establish the foundation of a strong working relationship with our restaurant partners, including sustainable economics for both parties
- Help us identify and build best practices and collaborate with the Account Management team to support a seamless onboarding experience for each new restaurant partner
- Proactively maintain communication channels with prospects via phone, email, and face-to-face meetings
- Hybrid role
**Basic Qualifications**
- Minimum 1+ years total professional sales experience or 0+ years with a Bachelor’s Degree (earned or in progress) and exposure to sales through internships or other experience
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Minimum 1+ year work experience in sales (full cycle sales preferred)
- Passionate about sales and about helping restaurants and merchants grow their business with Uber Eats
- Experience with Salesforce and Google Suite
- Good communicator and enjoy working on teams that value collaboration
- Willingness to roll up your sleeves and demonstrate persistence and perseverance, you aren't fearful of rejection or cold-calling
- Comfortable thinking on your feet, genuinely curious and like to ask questions
- Excited to learn - This is a great role to grow or build your career in sales
- No fear of picking up the phone - this role is 70% on the phone and 30% in the field

title: Applications Developer_Enterprise App SaaS
location: Bangalore, Karnātaka, India; Hyderabad, Telangāna, India
description:
**About the Role**
Uber Engineering operates in a high-performance environment driven by scale, reliability, and speed. We’re looking for an **Application Developer Engineer** who thrives at the intersection of IT, SaaS administration, automation, and customer impact. This role owns the operational health, configuration, and lifecycle of Uber’s core enterprise applications while acting as the highest escalation point for complex application issues.
You will partner closely with Engineering, Security, IT Ops, and Business stakeholders to ensure Uber’s critical collaboration and productivity platforms run securely, efficiently, and at global scale.
This is not a traditional service desk rol, it’s a **hands-on application ownership role** with real influence over tooling, processes, and user experience across Uber.
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
01. Act as **primary application developer and technical owner** for enterprise SaaS applications including (but not limited to): Google Workspace, Zoom, Slack, Figma, Asana, Jira, Confluence, ServiceNow, Okta / Identity-integrated applications
02. Manage application configuration, access models, permissions, integrations, and automation.
03. Own application lifecycle management: onboarding, offboarding, renewals, vendor coordination, and feature rollouts.
04. Serve as **Super Admin** with the highest level of access to supported applications.
05. Resolve complex, escalated L3 application issues beyond standard troubleshooting.
06. Lead incident response for enterprise application outages or degradations.
07. Design and implement automation and integration workflows using JavaScript, Python, APIs, and Enterprise AI applications (Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude, Claude Code, MCP) to reduce manual effort and improve reliability.
08. Analyze ticket and usage data to identify trends, systemic issues, and opportunities for optimization.
09. Possess strong programming language knowledge, including Python, JavaScript, Java, and SQL.
10. Demonstrate strong knowledge of databases such as BigQuery and Apache Hive.
11. Act as the bridge between IT Engineering, Service Desk, Security, and Business stakeholders.
12. Provide actionable feedback to IT Engineering leadership on application pain points and improvement opportunities.
13. Own internal and external-facing documentation for enterprise applications.
14. Develop and maintain runbooks, SOPs, and escalation guides.
15. Ensure procurement and licensing processes follow logistics and compliance guidelines.
16. Perform additional duties as assigned to support enterprise application operations.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. 3+ years of IT or Enterprise Application Developer experience.
2. 3+ years supporting SaaS platforms at scale with admin-level access.
3. Strong scripting or coding skills (Python, Bash, APIs).
4. Strong knowledge of Enterprise AI applications, including Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude, Claude Code, and MCP.
5. Familiarity with identity and access management (Okta, AD, SSO, SCIM).
6. Proven experience acting as an escalation point for complex technical issues.
7. Strong documentation, training, and communication skills.
8. Ability to respond quickly to incidents and operate effectively in high-pressure situations.
9. Willingness to participate in on-call rotations and work outside standard business hours when needed.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
01. 4+ years of experience in Enterprise Application Development or Software Engineering.
02. Demonstrated expertise in building and deploying automation using Python, JavaScript, and APIs.
03. Experience with data warehousing and querying large datasets (BigQuery, Hive).
04. Hands-on experience with version control systems (e.g., Git) and CI/CD tools.
05. Deep knowledge of Linux environments and cloud platforms (e.g., GCP, AWS).
06. Minimum of 3-4 years’ experience with escalations, process efficiency, and Tier 3 support.
07. Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, or equivalent combination of education and experience.
08. Strong problem solving, organization, and project management skills.
09. Ability to take initiative and work independently, as well as part of a team.
10. Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
11. Willingness to participate in on-call rotations and work outside standard business hours when needed.

title: Senior SMB Account Executive, Uber for Business Brazil
description:
### About the Role
Sales at Uber for Business (U4B) means navigating ambiguity, earning trust fast, and solving problems that don’t come with a script. It’s not about pushing a product—it’s about building long-term partnerships in a fast-changing environment. If you’re resilient, resourceful, and energized by the messy reality of doing big things—this is where you’ll grow.
Brazil is one of our most dynamic markets, and in this role, you’ll be the engine behind our SMB growth. You will lead the full sales cycle for our newest B2B suite of products, focusing on high-potential companies. This isn't just about hitting a quota; it's about owning your territory, unblocking complex integration barriers, and helping help write the playbook for a rapidly evolving region. If you have the grit to handle a high volume of prospects while providing a consultative, "Uber-first" experience, we want you on the team.
This is a hybrid role—our team collaborates in person out of our office in Sao Paulo at least 3 days/week (Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday).
### What You’ll Do
- **Navigate the Full Sales Cycle:** Lead the charge in prospecting, pitching, and closing partnerships with diverse decision-makers within the SMB segment for the latest U4B products.
- **Own the Pipeline:** Build and maintain a robust pipeline using Salesforce, forecasting with discipline and hitting aggressive bi-annual and quarterly targets.
- **Unblock Growth:** Identify and eliminate barriers to gain client commitment, using a consultative approach to probe for objections and provide tailored solutions.
- **Drive Regional Strategy:** Contribute directly to the launch and development of new U4B products in Brazil, sharing best practices and insights from the field.
- **Build Scalable Systems:** Create process playbooks and performance trackers that help the entire team move faster and more efficiently.
- **Collaborate Cross-Functionally:** Influence without authority by working alongside operations, marketing, and legal teams to resolve blockers and deliver solutions that scale.
- **Master the Details:** Manage hundreds of prospects simultaneously with high organization, ensuring every follow-through is professional and timely.
### Basic Qualifications
- Minimum 4 years of professional B2B sales or account management experience.

description:
## About the Role
As a Sales Representative for Uber Eats, you will identify and partner with the best local restaurants to expand our platform's selection. You will manage the full sales cycle, from prospecting to closing, ensuring top-tier merchants become long-term partners while acting as a brand ambassador.

title: Enterprise Application Developer
location: San Francisco, California, United States; Sunnyvale, California, United States
description:
Uber Engineering is a high-performance culture marked by perseverance and hyperproductivity. We’re looking for team players with natural customer service intuition who can work harder, faster, and smarter while taking technical excellence to a whole new level. You should be pumped about building a global transportation marketplace that spans a wide range of cities — varying from 100,000 to 10 million people in size — with unique regulatory, technical, and business requirements.
Application Developer (Enterprise Applications)
Uber Engineering operates in a high-performance environment driven by scale, reliability, and speed. We’re looking for an Application Developer Engineer who thrives at the intersection of IT, SaaS administration, automation, and customer impact. This role owns the operational health, configuration, and lifecycle of Uber’s core enterprise applications while acting as the highest escalation point for complex application issues.
You will partner closely with Engineering, Security, IT Ops, and Business stakeholders to ensure Uber’s critical collaboration and productivity platforms run securely, efficiently, and at global scale.
This is not a traditional service desk role, it’s a hands-on application ownership role with real influence over tooling, processes, and user experience across Uber.
What you’ll be doing:
1\. Act as primary application developer and technical owner for enterprise SaaS applications including (but not limited to): Google Workspace, Zoom, Slack, Figma, Asana, Jira, Confluence, ServiceNow, Okta / Identity-integrated applications
2\. Manage application configuration, access models, permissions, integrations, and automation.
3\. Own application lifecycle management: onboarding, offboarding, renewals, vendor coordination, and feature rollouts.
4\. Serve as Super Admin with the highest level of access to supported applications.
5\. Resolve complex, escalated L3 application issues beyond standard troubleshooting.
6\. Lead incident response for enterprise application outages or degradations.
7\. Design and implement automation and integration workflows using JavaScript, Python, APIs, and Enterprise AI applications (Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude, Claude Code, MCP) to reduce manual effort and improve reliability.
8\. Analyze ticket and usage data to identify trends, systemic issues, and opportunities for optimization.
9\. Possess strong programming language knowledge, including Python, JavaScript, Java, and SQL.
10\. Demonstrate strong knowledge of databases such as BigQuery and Apache Hive.
11\. Act as the bridge between IT Engineering, Service Desk, Security, and Business stakeholders.
12\. Provide actionable feedback to IT Engineering leadership on application pain points and improvement opportunities.
13\. Own internal and external-facing documentation for enterprise applications.
14\. Develop and maintain runbooks, SOPs, and escalation guides.
15\. Ensure procurement and licensing processes follow logistics and compliance guidelines.
16\. Perform additional duties as assigned to support enterprise application operations.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1\. 4+ years of IT or Enterprise Application Developer experience.
2\. 3+ years supporting SaaS platforms at scale with admin-level access.
3\. Strong scripting or coding skills (Python, Bash, APIs).
4\. Strong knowledge of Enterprise AI applications, including Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude, Claude Code, and MCP.
5\. Familiarity with identity and access management (Okta, AD, SSO, SCIM).
6\. Proven experience acting as an escalation point for complex technical issues.
7\. Strong documentation, training, and communication skills.
8\. Ability to respond quickly to incidents and operate effectively in high-pressure situations.
9\. Willingness to participate in on-call rotations and work outside standard business hours when needed.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1\. 4+ years of experience in Enterprise Application Development or Software Engineering.
2\. Demonstrated expertise in building and deploying automation using Python, JavaScript, and APIs.
3\. Experience with data warehousing and querying large datasets (BigQuery, Hive).
4\. Hands-on experience with version control systems (e.g., Git) and CI/CD tools.
5\. Deep knowledge of Linux environments and cloud platforms (e.g., GCP, AWS).
6\. Minimum of 3-4 years’ experience with escalations, process efficiency, and Tier 3 support.
7\. Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, or equivalent combination of education and experience.
8\. Strong problem solving, organization, and project management skills.
9\. Ability to take initiative and work independently, as well as part of a team.
10\. Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
11\. Willingness to participate in on-call rotations and work outside standard business hours when needed.

title: Manager, Analytics - Financial Operations
description:
**_This is a hybrid role and you must be located in, or willing to relocate to, San Francisco for the position._**
**About the Role**
Our team delivers the critical information and analyses that drive strategic decisions within Uber Financial Operations We equip our partners and their stakeholders with timely, accurate, and actionable insights to achieve their objectives. Simply put, while FinOps manages the engine of Finances operational transactions, FinOps Analytics provides the compass by leveraging AI, Tableau dashboards, and data analysis to chart our course.
- Manage a team of developer analysts in the creation and enhancement of insightful and actionable analytics
- Own the full lifecycle of operational metrics—from KPI design to dashboard deployment and stakeholder adoption to design metrics that drive accountability, surface inefficiencies, and guide automation.
- Utilize predictive modeling techniques to support scenario analysis, helping the FinOps team to understand potential financial outcomes based on various business strategies.
- Perform ad hoc analyses and create reports as requested by leadership to support strategic initiatives and decision-making.
- Drive automation efforts for reporting, leveraging AI and other data science tools to increase efficiency and accuracy.
**What You’ll Do**
**Data Privacy & Strategic Oversight**
- Fintech Partnership: Serve as the primary link between our business leaders and the technology teams, ensuring our technical resources are always focused on the projects with the highest financial return.
- Project Management: Oversee the end-to-end project lifecycle for our data initiatives, from gathering business requirements and vetting return on investment (ROI) to ensuring our technology partners deliver high-quality solutions on time.
- Business & Technology Liaison: Translate business needs into technical reality and ensure any system issues are resolved quickly to minimize impact on the business.
- Data Privacy: Manage the security of our sensitive financial information, ensuring that travel and third-party spending details are only visible to appropriate users.
**Team Leadership**
- Team Development & Mentorship: Lead a global team of analysts through structured weekly coaching and quarterly performance reviews, focusing on building the technical and strategic skills to be strategic partners who help their business leads make better decisions.
- Workflow Optimization: Manage our department’s "intake engine," using centralized tools to review, prioritize, and assign all incoming data requests to ensure our team’s and Fintech's capacity is always aligned with the highest-value business needs.
- Organizational Enablement: Act as a lead educator for the broader CAO organization, providing the training and resources necessary for non-technical teams to leverage our data for better decision-making.
**AI Strategy & Future Readiness**
- Internal AI Champion: Lead the transition from old-fashioned static charts to interactive AI Assistants that allow our teams to get instant answers to complex financial questions.
- AI Quality & Accuracy: Direct the feedback and "fine-tuning" process for our AI tools to ensure they are 100% accurate, reliable, and grounded in Uber’s official financial records.
- Advanced Problem Solving: Pioneer new tools that use AI to instantly explain the "why" behind changes in our global metrics, saving hours of manual research.
**Basic Qualifications**
- Experience managing global teams
- Project Management and Change Management
- Proficiency in Tableau, Jira, Asana, Excel, G-Suite
- Troubleshooting systems
- 6+ years of work experience in finance analytics, data analytics, or within a similar field
- Experience with SQL
- Bachelor’s Degree
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Master’s degree in related field
- Experience in Oracle Analytics Services and Fusion Reporting
- Experience with Tableau dashboard design
- Experience with Ramp
- Expert SQL skills.
- High technical proficiency in common analytics & reporting tools (e.g., Tableau, Google Data Studio, Google Sheets/Excel).
- Experience in Python
- Advanced data analysis skills leading to actionable recommendations.
- Experience in leading high-visibility projects and influencing others in a cross-functional team environment
- Strong sense of ownership and accountability
- Curious, self-directed, and impact-driven—you chase high-impact problems to deliver solutions.
- Comfortable with ambiguity and switching contexts in a fast-moving environment
- Strong storytelling ability: Distilling exciting and hard-to-find insights into a compelling, concise data story.
- Experience in data extraction, wrangling, and building pipelines.
- Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset
- Highly self-motivated, proactive, and able to drive projects independently.
- Detail-oriented mindset, with a focus on accuracy, efficiency, and process standardization.

title: Head of Enterprise Accounts, Uber Eats, Germany
description:
### About the Role
As the **Head of Enterprise (m/w/d)** for Germany, you will be a senior commercial leader responsible for the growth and success of Uber Eats’ most high-impact partners. You will sit at the intersection of leadership, commercial strategy, and executive-level relationship management, ensuring that our largest partners achieve long-term value on our platform.
In this role, you will lead a high-performing team of over seven Account Managers, driving a customer-centric culture that balances local market needs with Uber’s global objectives. We are looking for a strategic heavy hitter with a proven track record in complex negotiations, data-driven optimization, and leading teams through fast-paced, matrixed environments.
### What You Will Do
**Leadership & Team Development**
- **Lead with Vision:** Attract, coach, and empower a high-performing team of 7+ Account Managers.
- **Culture & Growth:** Foster a culture of performance, accountability, and customer obsession where your team can thrive.
- **Succession & Feedback:** Set clear goals, provide ongoing feedback, and build professional development pipelines to create the next generation of leaders.
**Commercial Strategy & Execution**
- **Strategic Growth:** Develop and execute a customer growth strategy for Germany that aligns with local operations and global go-to-market objectives.
- **Data-Driven Optimization:** Use analytical ability and operational rigor to optimize account segmentation, expansion plays, revenue targets, and pricing models.
- **Business Impact:** Steer joint business planning and revenue expansion, ensuring sales forecasting and pipeline management are effectively executed.
- **Process Excellence:** Implement best-in-class processes and project management skills to track KPIs and drive partnership metrics effectively.
**Customer & Market Engagement**
- **Trusted Advisor:** Build and cultivate trust-based relationships with C-level and senior stakeholders at Uber Eats Germany’s most strategic partners.
- **Solution Selling:** Negotiate complex deals and unlock long-term value by advancing partner priorities across the full Uber Eats Delivery product portfolio.
- **Market Insight:** Monitor trends, customer needs, and the competitive landscape to adjust strategies and expand Uber’s footprint in key segments.
**Stakeholder Management & Cross-Functional Alignment**
- **Internal Collaboration:** Partner closely with Territories, Restaurant Operations, Eater Operations, Product, and Marketing teams to deliver tailored solutions.
- **Regional & Global Synergy:** Engage with peers internationally to share learnings, contribute to global enterprise playbooks, and scale best practices.
- **Advocacy:** Use customer feedback to influence product development and represent Uber at industry events and executive-level engagements.
### Basic Qualifications
- **Experience:** 10+ years of relevant experience in sales, account management, or commercial leadership roles.
- **Leadership:** A proven track record of leading and developing high-performing teams, ideally in a fast-growth or tech environment.
- **Language Skills: English** is mandatory; **German** is highly preferred.
- **Technical Proficiency:** Full proficiency in Google Workspace for executive storytelling and data analysis.
- **Senior Engagement:** Excellent ability to engage, influence, and negotiate with C-level and VP-level stakeholders.
- **Analytical Mindset:** Strong data-driven approach to decision-making and experience working in complex, matrixed global organizations.
### Preferred Qualifications
- **Industry Expertise:** Experience operating in the tech, eCommerce, or German F&B industry (including FMCG partnerships).
- **Professional Background:** Experience in strategy consulting (MBB), business development in Big Tech, or high-growth scale-ups.
- **Technical Skills:** Knowledge of **SQL** is considered a significant advantage.

title: Lead Product Manager Earner CX Experience
description:
## About the Role
Are you a seasoned product manager looking to build experiences that millions of people rely on to discover new ways to make a living with Uber? Are you energized by creating user journeys where magical experiences, growth, and trust must work hand in hand? If so, this role might be for you.
As a Lead Product Manager your impact on the business side, onboarding, and making new drivers successful is the lifeblood that powers Uber's growth and creates a path to economic empowerment for millions of people every year.
In this role, you will envision and deliver a multi-gig experience that is intuitive, fast, globally scalable and tailored to different earner segments. You will own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for how drivers and couriers discover new earning opportunities, maximize their earnings and manage their platform access across all of their gigs.
This role requires strong product sense, a good understanding of the driver persona and their motivations and great crossfunctional agility. You will work closely with engineering, data science, design, operations, legal, and trust & safety partners to build experiences that support and scale with the speed of our business.
## About the Team
The Earner growth team sits at the heart of the earner lifecycle and plays a foundational role in Uber’s global growth. We are responsible for maximizing the number of quality, high performing earners on the platform. We achieve this through optimizing the onboarding experience, enabling earners to progress during their early lifecycle and by helping to maximize their earning potential through multiple gigs to increase retention. In the event that earners churn, we also focus on bringing them back to the platform.
## What You'll Do
1. Own the product roadmap and lead vision, definition and execution for building Uber’s earner multi gig experience.
2. Rethink the journey - deeply understand earners, their motivations and challenges and keep making the gig discovery and recommendation experience better.
3. Innovate - find new ways of simplifying the user experience with new technologies.
4. Distill vision and strategy for the team, get all members of cross-functional team #superpumped.
5. Own your numbers, drive your cross-functional team of engineers, data scientist, product ops and designers to set qualitative objectives and quantitative goals -- and overachieve them.
6. Clearly communicate product plans, benefits, and results to leadership.
## \-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. Minimum of 6 years of product management experience.
2. Experience delivering highly successful and creative consumer Internet products with your fingerprints all over them - you're proud of what you've accomplished.
3. Data-driven: you always get the data you need, and you can distill it into an insightful story. Most importantly, you leverage data to drive strategic decisions.
4. Technical skills. Ability to inform and evaluate engineering decisions.
## \-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Any experience working with gig economy earners
2. Launching products in different international markets
3. Experience in building and optimizing complex user acquisition funnels or recommendation engines

description:
**About the Role**
Uber Eats is looking for a dynamic and results-driven Account Executive to join our team. The Account Executive role is a great opportunity to demonstrate your sales experience to expand the number of restaurants that work with Uber Eats. Your primary focus will be bringing on new restaurants that fit our partnership criteria, and use your prospecting and negotiation skills through cold-calling and driving internal processes to set up our partnerships for success.
As an Account Executive, you will lead the full sales cycle and sign partnerships with top local restaurants while being a champion for the Uber and Uber Eats brands. Your success in this role is measured by achieving and exceeding your monthly quota and proactively building your pipeline by managing and hitting the weekly metrics (cold calls, emails, and field appointments).
**What You'll Do**
- Own and manage a pipeline of local small and medium restaurants to sign new partnerships in your geographic region with a mix of cold-calling (at least 50 dials per day) and in-person meetings
- Create a great first-impression and act as an ambassador of Uber Eats to develop long-term partnerships and clearly communicate the value of Uber
- Manage contract negotiation to establish the foundation of a strong working relationship with our restaurant partners, including sustainable economics for both parties
- Help us identify and build standard processes and collaborate with the Account Management team to support a seamless onboarding experience for each new restaurant partner
- Proactively maintain communication channels with prospects via phone, email, and face-to-face meetings
**Basic Qualifications**
- Minimum 3 years of experience in sales, operations, account management, or similar functions
- Passionate about sales and helping restaurants grow their business with Uber Eats
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Excited to learn and have no fear of picking up the phone - this is a great role to grow or build your career in sales
- Bachelor's Degree or equivalent
- Proven achievement in performance-based role
- Effective communication with colleagues and clients
- Experience multi-tasking work with attention to detail
- Coachability, interest in implementing feedback, and dedication to building your brand
For Chicago based roles: The total annualized on-target earnings (OTE) for this position are USD $106,250-$117,500. The OTE includes a base hourly rate of USD $30.65-$33.90 and a variable incentive target of USD $42,500-$47,000  The variable incentive target is based on individual sales performance and its payment is based on the terms of the Sales Incentive Plan.

title: Executive Assistant Business Partner II
description:
**About the Role**
Executive Assistant Business Partners (EABPs) partner with Uber’s leadership to provide sophisticated administrative and operational support, while also contributing to projects that help drive business priorities and strengthen team collaboration and morale.
Do you enjoy anticipating the needs of the team and solving complex admin and operational issues before they even arise? Do you want to be a part of an extraordinary EA community? If so, see the day-to-day functions below and apply!
**What You’ll Do**
- Manage complex calendars using Google Calendar including vetting, prioritizing, and providing recommendations
- Help ensure leaders are properly prepared and on time for all meetings.
- Assist with the scheduling of multiple stakeholder meetings
- Contribute to leadership meetings by taking notes and tracking action items
- Navigate the intricacies of supporting leaders remotely and a complex conference room landscape when returning to the office
- Assist with event planning and coordination for team meetings, all-hands meetings, summits, and QBRs
- Make domestic and international travel arrangements and process travel expense reports based on direction from traveler
- Draft detailed travel itineraries and provide remote assistance with logistics and travel arrangements with input from traveler, including visa processing
- Follow up and provide reminders on project deadlines, AIs, and deliverables
- Assist with ensuring the timely review and submission of expenses
- May assist with facility/space planning logistics
- May preview and provide feedback regarding team expense reports, and assist with managing leader’s or team’s morale budget
- May work on other tasks and projects as assigned
**Basic Qualifications**
- Minimum 6+ years of administrative experience
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Minimum 8+ years of administrative experience preferred
- Minimum 4+ years experience supporting a VP-level leader in any sized business or a Director-level leader in a global business
- Google calendar
- Tech company experience
- Experience supporting multiple leaders
- Excellent communication skills with ability to build strong relationships with both internal and external stakeholders
- Demonstrated ability to prioritize leaders’ calendar with ability to make judgment calls and decisions based on rapidly shifting priorities
- Proven ability to work in a fast paced, often changing environment with efficiency and accuracy
- Ability to take initiative and ownership over sometimes ambiguous tasks and situations with little oversight
- Strong organizational skills with a strong attention to detail
- Experience coordinating projects
- Experience with budget management
- Ability to work with confidential information
- Experience working in a complex, matrixed and global environment
- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience
For San Francisco, CA-based roles: The base hourly rate range for this role is USD$51.93 per hour - USD$57.70 per hour.
You will be eligible to participate in Uber's bonus program, and may be offered an equity award & other types of comp. All full-time employees are eligible to participate in a 401(k) plan. title: Implementation Lead, Uber for Business
description:
**About the Role**
As an Implementation Lead for the **Germany** region, you'll work efficiently and effectively with our Uber for Business customers and be responsible for the successful and flawless launch of new accounts within the Uber for Business platform. You'll support our Sales and Account Management teams to drive revenue growth, operational excellence, and second-to-none customer service.
**What You'll Do**
- Work with our customers and internal teams to understand the needs and goals of each customer. Use this understanding to create mutually beneficial goals and influence the customer.
- Create a strategy for a seamless launch and implementation that enhances employee adoption and usage - continuing to supervise for 2-4 months post-launch and provide data-based recommendations for improvement.
- Communicate effectively and manage internal and external stakeholders by setting and managing timelines and project plans for implementation.
- Ensure customers are properly trained - Create a video or written collateral, host office hours, and/or facilitate training with customers on how to navigate the tools and programs.
- Complete tactical and technical account set-up - including assisting with the setup of API, SFTP, or expense integrations and submitting Monthly Billing Applications to finance.
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams including product, marketing, and customer support to ensure a smooth onboarding and strong client experience. Think creatively about how we can solve problems and mitigate them from happening again
- Demonstrated experience in managing internal stakeholders, including those at a higher Comment start seniority level, with the ability to maintain one's position effectively.
**What You'll Need**
- Bachelor's Degree
- 3+ years of experience in a client-facing role and/or project management
- Strong analytical, organizational and problem-solving skills
- Proficient in data analysis, interpreting results to drive meaningful conclusions
- Experience in influencing stakeholders to drive decision-making
- Must be Fluent in German & English
**Bonus Points**
- Strong prioritization skills - able to manage your time effectively, prioritizing high-value clients, while working with multiple customers to deliver on goals and improve processes.
- Outstanding interpersonal skills, both written and verbal communication, and strong group facilitation skills.
- Outstanding organizational skills and the ability to balance your attention to detail with swift execution - we need to do things quickly and seamlessly.
- Speed, resourcefulness, and a go-getter attitude. Be agile and thrive when operating in new spaces and fast-paced environments.
- The drive to contribute to a positive team environment and collaborate by always sharing strategies with your peers.
- Tech-savviness. You'll be using multiple systems in your role, and Salesforce experience is a plus.
- Excellent communication and collaboration abilities

title: Director, Marketplace Central Operations Mobility EMEA
description:
**About the Team**
Uber's EMEA Mobility business is a vital engine for growth and profitability, operating across more than 40 countries on three continents. This region presents unique opportunities and challenges due to diverse regulatory, competitive, and economic landscapes.
The EMEA Central Operations team is responsible for all scaled operational management for EMEA Mobility. They collaborate closely with global product and engineering teams and local operational teams to drive growth and innovation across the region. Within Central Operations, the Marketplace Operations team acts as the technical "brain" of the business, managing real-time marketplace dynamics that are instantaneous and complex. This highly centralized and professional team is responsible for the health and efficiency of the marketplace across 1,000+ cities, overseeing a billion dollar budget dedicated to incentive deployment and core pricing. Working at the intersection of data science and product engineering, the team continuously optimizes pricing technology and rider-driver matching to drive incremental improvements that result in outsized financial impacts for the entire region.
**About the Role**
The Director, Head of Marketplace for EMEA is a senior leadership position, reporting directly to the Head of Central Operations EMEA and serving as a key member of the Central Operations Leadership Team.
This role is responsible for leading the strategy, execution, and technical management of marketplace dynamics—including pricing, incentives, and matching—across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. The ideal candidate will possess the technical depth to bridge the gap between data science and product engineering while effectively leading a high-performing team to optimize the health and efficiency of our mobility marketplace.
**What You’ll Do**
**Create Marketplace Strategy**
- Set a clear vision and roadmap for the Marketplace that guides the team’s long-term direction
- Translate Uber’s broader goals into an actionable operational strategy with clear objectives
- Use insights from rider and earner behavior to shape the regional marketplace strategy
- Partner with tech teams to design solutions that strengthen markets and products
- Define the priorities and scope for the sub-teams within the function
**Support Marketplace for Growth Bets**
- Partner with growth bet leaders and build marketplace architecture for new Serviceable Addressable Markets (SAMs)  that we plan to build for
- Defining scale-up strategies, experimentation plans, pricing and incentive models, and early operating playbooks that allow Uber to enter and scale categories with clarity and speed
- Drive the success of growth bets marketplaces through tight performance tracking, rapid issue resolution, and strong cross-functional alignment, ensuring new SAMs reach product-market fit, deliver healthy unit economics, and integrate seamlessly into the broader Mobility business
**Marketplace Execution & Optimization**
- Lead key initiatives across pricing, incentives, matching, and marketplace health
- Improve throughput/ ROI by applying global and local best practices
- Collaborate closely with Product and Tech to optimize marketplace parameters and build better tools
- Standardize processes and drive throughput gains through local optimizations and new tech
**Leadership & Stakeholder Management**
- Lead and develop a high-performing team, setting a high bar for impact
- Foster a culture focused on growth, ownership, and continuous improvement
- Work closely with cross-functional partners to pursue high-value opportunities
- Navigate a matrixed environment with strong, solutions-oriented stakeholder management
**What You’ll Need**
**Basic Qualifications**
- 14+ years of robust work experience across business analytics, strategy, finance and project management
- 4+ years of experience leading teams
- Combination of experience working in complex matrix organizations and fast-moving smaller ventures
- Undergraduate or advanced degree in a STEM-related field required (Science, Technology, Engineering, or Mathematics)..
**Capabilities**
- _You’re a strong problem-solver:_ You can comfortably dive into data and identify how our riders & drivers are engaging with the product and what they are after
- _You love to growth hack:_ you’ve got the business judgment and critical thinking skills with the ability to use data to quickly establish the highest value priorities and develop a strategy to pursue them
- _You’re an operator:_ you’ve got the executional excellence to build out a roadmap and to ensure that product launches, fixes and processes are done well. You love making sure that trips happen and customers (on all sides of the marketplace) love the experience
- _You have the GoGetIt mindset:_ you’re willing to embrace the grind, overcome adversity and play to win for the people we serve, seizing opportunity to push the envelope
- _You’re a trusted and effective partner:_ you can build deep, trust-based relationships with countries and cross functional teams; and you’re a good enough listener to genuinely seek and incorporate their views
- _You can build and motivate a very smart and capable team:_ you know how to support, challenge and develop a team of high performers and turn them into a high-performing team
- Embodies and promotes the values of Uber

title: Supply Growth Manager - EMEA Delivery
description:
**About the Role**
We're looking for a strategic and data-driven Supply Growth Manager to join our EMEA Central Ops team and lead the charge on scaling courier acquisition across the region. You'll own the end-to-end strategy for how we attract, convert, and onboard delivery partners more efficiently, building the playbooks and processes that our local markets rely on to grow their courier base.
This is a high-impact role for someone who loves turning ambiguity into structure, thrives on cross-functional collaboration, and wants to directly shape how millions of deliveries get fulfilled across EMEA.
**What you'll do**
- **Drive courier acquisition campaigns end-to-end across 20+ EMEA markets** — from strategy and design to execution and performance tracking, coordinating cross-functionally with product, CRM, finance and Local Ops
- **Build scalable processes and playbooks for managing courier incentives**, turning manual workflows into streamlined processes that empower local teams to deploy budget quickly and effectively
- **Advise on budget sallocation and ROI planning for supply growth investments**, ensuring every euro spent drives measurable impact while balancing near-term growth targets with long-term unit economics
- **Act as the regional bridge to product teams**, ensuring smooth rollout of global launches across EMEA by flagging risks early, adapting strategies, and driving local adoption
- **Partner with CRM** to design communications campaigns that maximize the reach and conversion of acquisition efforts, optimizing messaging, targeting, and channel mix across markets.
**What you'll need**
- **3+ years of experience** in management consulting, or in growth, operations or strategy roles ideally within a fast-paced marketplace tech platform
- **Strong analytical skills** with the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights, scalable processes and compelling Exec-level presentations — comfortable working seamlessly between SQL, Sheets and slides
- **Hands-on experience designing and deploying growth campaigns end-to-end**, from building the business case and securing budget to managing execution, running experiments, and measuring ROI to inform future investment decisions
- **Experience working across multiple markets or regions**, with an appreciation for local nuances and the ability to balance standardization with market-level flexibility
- **Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills**, with the confidence to influence senior leaders and collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams including marketing, product, and local operations.
**Preferred qualifications**
- **Experience in courier, driver, or gig-worker acquisition**, with a strong understanding of supply-side marketplace dynamics and the levers that drive sign-up, activation, and retention
- **Background in process design or operational excellence**, with a track record of building repeatable frameworks, SOPs, or playbooks that enable local teams to execute independently
- **Experience collaborating closely with product and engineering teams** to translate operational needs into scalable tooling, features, or platform improvements that drive acquisition efficiency
- **Exposure to incentive design and financial modeling**, with the ability to structure cost-effective promotional offers, referral programs, or bonus schemes while maintaining unit economics discipline.
