2. Research and develop our accounts Outbound prospecting via cold calling, email, and marketing campaigns

4. Excellent organization, project management, time-management skills, and ability to take initiative
Preferred Qualifications
1. Experience with email campaigns and Salesforce
2. Salesforce, LinkedIn Sales Navigator and Groove experience
3. A passion for technology and B2B sales

title: Brand Marketing Associate
description:
**About the Role**
The Japan Brand Marketing team plays a critical role when it comes to establishing deep connections with consumers representing the frontline of our business. They work closely with business stakeholders (Comms, Policy, Ops, S&P, R&I, Data Science, and Legal and CommOps) as well as external stakeholders (agency partners) to bring our products and brand to life in the market.
They’re empowered and responsible to assist and deliver brand campaigns from strategy and brief through to execution and measurement ensuring everything is delivered as expected, on time and on budget. Our Marketing Manager will be involved in the development and execution of the annual marketing plans, leading end-to-end projects for Japan Marketing – from conceptualization, ideation, executiion and performance evaluation.
**Your Impact in Role**
- Own Brand Campaign Development and Execution
- Develop and execute impactful marketing campaigns for Uber.
- Strengthen the Uber's brand presence in the Japanese market.
- Cross-Functional Growth Collaboration
- Collaborate with diverse teams to boost demand for Uber's services in Japan.
- Foster synergies across functions to achieve business growth goals.
- Brand Reporting & Measurement
- Act as the primary point of contact for all reporting and performance measurement queries, related to your designated projects.
- Ensure data-driven decisions for continuous improvement.
- Effective Agency Management
- Manage and leverage agency partnerships for marketing success.
- Optimize agency resources to achieve marketing objectives.
- Support Overall Business Growth
- Contribute to the broader business growth strategy beyond marketing.
- Align marketing efforts with the company's overall expansion goals.
What You Will Need
- **Brand Strategy and Creative Experience (mandatory):** Working alongside the Marketing Manager, you’ll collaborate with the internal and agency teams to deliver strategic and creative marketing campaigns and projects that consumers care about.
- **Marketing and Business Acumen:** Using a deep understanding of the business priorities, the macro and micro factors impacting us and the global business, you’ll ensure all campaigns and projects throughout the full marketing funnel are driving positive impact and are sufficiently evaluated taking on board key learnings.
- C **ollaboration:** You’ll work collaboratively with partners, internal stakeholders and agencies to maximize the impact of your work. Be renown for nurturing a culture of collaboration, be someone that inspires, motivates and clearly communicates to unite them to deliver the best work.

- **Good Balance Between Creative & Data-Driven Mindsets**: You care about the number and what the data is saying, but you also can dig deeper into the creative and visionary side of what makes a successful marketing campaign.

You’ll be able to work to tight deadlines at a fast pace and be capable of juggling several projects simultaneously. You’ll also have the ability to take the initiative in a constantly-changing work environment with a willingness to adopt a generalist mindset.

- **A strong customer-centric mindset** – you are always thinking about how our customers interact with the brand.
- **Native-level fluency in Japanese and business-level English**.

title: Senior Software Engineer - Application Security
location: Seattle, Washington, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
Uber is seeking a Senior Software Engineer to join our Application Security team as we evolve our defensive capabilities through advanced automation. You will help scale the traditional AppSec model of finding vulnerabilities manually to a fully automated and autonomous system. In this role, you will be tasked with designing, implementing, and deploying security automation that secures AI implementations within our applications and leverages model-driven analysis to detect security vulnerabilities—such as XSS, SQLi, CSRF, and SSRF—across Uber’s entire organization.
You will use your software engineering skills to raise the security bar across all mobile and web apps at Uber while exploring the use of automation to generate exploits and validate vulnerabilities at scale. This is a unique opportunity to work with all levels of engineers, making a real impact on Uber’s security posture and up-leveling your own engineering skills while building specialized security knowledge. Note: you will have access to unlimited model usage for your work!
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
1. Design end-to-end features and autonomous systems for Agent & MCP Security, Code Scanning, and Vulnerability Remediation applications.
2. Develop security automation capable of identifying vulnerabilities and performing automated exploit validation across Uber's microrepository landscape.
3. Collaborate and consult with multiple engineering teams and stakeholders from Privacy, Security, Compliance, Infrastructure, and Product for integration into our security platforms.
4. Design and develop new systems, such as automated security scoring and repository-to-service mapping, to empower fast, data-driven security decisions.
5. Build distributed backend systems, including workflows, that serve real-time analytics and security features at Uber scale.
6. Mentor junior engineers and lead technical design reviews for high-impact security projects.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. Understanding of Agents, MCP servers, and LLMs
2. BS/BE degree in Computer Science or related fields.
3. 5+ years of experience in software engineering.
4. Experience building applications from end to end.
5. Hands-on experience with Go, Java, C, or Python (Uber heavily utilizes Go).
6. Deep knowledge of datastore technologies, including RDBMS and NoSQL systems.
7. Experience implementing REST or gRPC APIs.
8. Service design and architecture experience.
9. Implementation of secure system design, including authentication, authorization, and encryption.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Experience with distributed messaging systems such as Kafka and building real-time data pipelines.
2. Experience designing, implementing, and deploying production-quality systems with high availability.
3. Experience building integrations with open source and vendor products.
4. Familiarity with securing agentic workflows within software applications.
5. Master’s in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

title: Staff Software Engineer, Production Engineering
description:
## **About the Role**
Engineering at Uber means building for real-world impact under real-world constraints. The problems are complex, the systems are massive, and the pace is fast. You'll need to make smart decisions with imperfect information, and own them. If you think in systems, thrive in ambiguous, high-stakes environments, and care about building things that actually work, this is where you'll grow.
Delivery Production Engineering is a software engineering team, not a traditional operations or sysadmin team. We solve reliability problems by writing code: orchestration systems, load testing frameworks,  automation tooling, and shared libraries. As a Production Engineer, you will blend multiple domains of software engineering to ensure Uber's services run reliably at massive scale improving compute efficiency  and accelerating developer productivity for a platform that serves millions of users around the world.
Our main focus areas:
- Outage mitigation tooling and automated failovers
- Capacity safety and compute efficiency, validated through load testing
- Observability and alerting platforms
- Graceful degradation
- Go language shared libraries
If you've worked in an SRE role with a heavy software engineering focus, or are energized by the challenge of solving complex engineering issues and building tools that empower an entire engineering  organization, you belong on this
team.
## **Basic Qualifications**
- 8+ years of experience in Go, Java, Python, or similar language
- Experience in delivering solutions end-to-end from defining problems to generating architecture plans, implementation, testing, and delivery.
- Writes clear technical proposals and RFCs; able to drive engineering alignment across teams through written design docs and verbal discussion.
## **Preferred Qualifications**
- Experience in various parts of SRE / reliability engineering / incident management at a large-scale company.
- Experience in platform/infrastructure engineering in related disciplines such as compute platform, software networking, online storage, developer platform, and observability.
- Experience mentoring and leading teams on projects while remaining hands-on and technical.
## **What the Candidate Will Do**
- Design, build, and maintain software to increase the reliability, scalability, and efficiency of thousands of stateless and stateful production services spread across multiple datacenter zones and regions.
- Lead initiatives end-to-end within the team, the Production Engineering org, and across engineering at large to increase reliability through automation, setting standards, developer tooling, and reusable frameworks.
- Work with other engineers to deeply understand their services and guide them towards practical and reliable architecture and implementation.
- Apply SRE concepts such as observability, integration/load/chaos testing, on-call, incident management, failovers, and disaster recovery to improve mean time between failures (MTBF), time to detection (TTD), and time to mitigation (TTM) of incidents.
- Participate in on-call rotations, responding to and leading mitigation of production incidents, and driving post-incident improvements.
For San Francisco, CA-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$232,000 per year - USD$258,000 per year.

title: Staff Software Engineer - Autonomous
description:
**About the Role**
The AM&D (Autonomous Mobility & Delivery) team owns the autonomous future of Uber’s Rides and Eats business. As we move towards a world where autonomous and human-driven vehicles coexist, Uber is tackling the challenge of building a hybrid marketplace that seamlessly integrates autonomous vehicles into the existing mobility and delivery ecosystem. This requires a delicate balance of optimizing experiences across human and AV drivers, ensuring the marketplace remains reliable, efficient, and scalable.
Our team touches nearly every surface and core service Uber provides. Over the past year, we’ve also partnered with some of the leading Autonomous Vehicle OEMs, enabling successful integrations in both Mobility and Delivery. As we look ahead, we expect significant business growth and are looking for a Staff Software Engineer to help drive this journey forward.
\-\-\-\- What You Will Do ----
1. Set the technical vision and lead the development of scalable systems that balance heterogeneous supplies of a hybrid marketplace.
2. Architect complex, distributed systems that handle the challenges of AV and human fleet coordination, ensuring Uber’s platform is robust, reliable, and ready for large-scale autonomous deployments.
3. Lead high-impact projects that span multiple teams and disciplines, collaborating with product, data science, operations, and external partners to drive innovation and deliver business-critical solutions.
4. Design and optimize algorithms that solve core marketplace problems such as routing, matching, pricing, and positioning, while ensuring AV and human vehicle operations work seamlessly together.
5. Innovate and lead the development of platform features that leverage autonomous vehicle data, pushing the boundaries of what is possible in mobility and delivery systems.
6. Mentor and guide senior engineers, driving the technical excellence of the team and fostering a culture of innovation and high performance.
7. Collaborate across the organization to ensure that autonomous components integrate smoothly into Uber’s broader ecosystem, setting the stage for years to come.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. Minimum 6+ years hands-on experience with backend development
2. Grit, drive and a deep feeling of ownership.
3. Expertise in system design and architecture, with the ability to lead the development of complex, scalable systems.
4. Strong leadership skills with a proven track record driving large, cross-functional projects and aligning stakeholders around a technical vision that delivers significant business impact.
5. A focus on impact, with a history of making strategic decisions that align with business goals and deliver measurable outcomes.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Background in autonomous vehicles (AV), with hands-on experience in developing, testing, or optimizing AV systems or related technologies.
2. Strong understanding of algorithms and data structures, especially in the context of real-time optimization, routing, or decision-making systems.
3. Experience with optimization techniques, such as linear programming, dynamic programming, or heuristic algorithms, and their application in large-scale systems.
4. Knowledge of marketplace dynamics, including supply-demand matching, pricing, and incentive systems, particularly in autonomous or transportation-related domains.
5. Familiarity with simulation environments and tools used for validating autonomous systems or optimizing large-scale networks.

description:

**About the Role**
As a Counsel in our Tokyo office, you will be a key driver of Uber’s strategic growth, managing complex regulatory, commercial, and litigation matters for our Mobility and Delivery businesses in Japan. You will serve as a high-impact adviser, navigating a fast-paced legal landscape to provide innovative solutions that shape our business operations in one of Uber's most critical global priority markets. By balancing legal risk with sustainable growth, you will have the unique opportunity to lead transformative projects that contribute to the evolution of Japanese society and help move the world toward a better place.
This position is based in Tokyo and reports to our Senior Counsel (Bengoshi) based in Japan. You will join a dedicated Japan Legal Team currently composed of three Japanese lawyers (Bengoshi) and one paralegal, working within our broader and diverse APAC Legal structure.
**\[Hybrid Work Policy\]** The Japan Legal Team currently maintains a flexible working style. Under our current policy, office attendance is required two days per week (Wednesday and Thursday). Please note that this is expected to transition to three days per week starting from the second half of 2027.
**Your Impact in Role**
- **Strategic Advisory:** Serve as a trusted adviser to senior business stakeholders in Japan, providing nuanced legal support on high-priority initiatives for the Mobility and Delivery business, empowering them to take calculated, smart risks.
- **Commercial Leadership:** Draft and lead negotiations for key strategic contracts and partnerships that are fundamental to Uber’s market expansion and operational sustainability.
- **Product & Regulatory Navigation:** Provide sophisticated product counseling and interpret current regulations to ensure ongoing compliance while refining business models in a dynamic legal environment.
- **Dispute Management:** Independently manage and advise on diverse legal proceedings and disputes, ensuring the business is protected across various practice areas.
- **Compliance & Education:** Conduct deep-dive risk evaluations and lead internal legal education programs to foster a culture of compliance and legal awareness across both legal and non-legal teams.
What You Will Need
- **Experience:** Extensive post-qualification experience as a Japan-qualified lawyer (Bengoshi) within a top-tier law firm or a fast-paced multinational corporation.
- **Languages:** Native-level proficiency in Japanese and a high level of business English (written and spoken) to effectively manage local stakeholders and global legal counterparts.
- **Core Skills:** Proven track record of managing a broad range of legal issues independently, including complex regulatory advisory, strategic commercial negotiations, and high-stakes litigation.
- **Communication & EQ:** Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with a high level of emotional intelligence to navigate complex internal and external relationships.
- **Adaptability & Execution:** Proven ability to juggle multiple projects in a fast-paced environment and re-prioritize quickly to address shifting business needs; demonstrated ability to thrive in high-impact and high-profile situations.
- **Specialized Expertise:** Experience in competition law or navigating evolving regulatory frameworks for tech platforms is preferred.
- **Leadership & Influence:** Demonstrated ability to manage senior stakeholders and operate effectively within a complex, matrixed organizational environment is preferred.
- **Strategic Thinking & Precision:** Exceptional analytical skills and mature judgment, combining a highly motivated and creative mindset with a laser-focused approach to details to ensure robust risk mitigation and "two steps ahead" problem-solving.
- **Adaptability:** A self-starter who thrives in high-profile situations and can rapidly re-prioritize multiple complex projects to meet shifting business needs.

title: Director, Tech Transformation, Field Operations
location: Amsterdam, Netherlands; New York, New York, United States
description:
**About the Team**
Community Operations (CommOps) is pioneering how Uber interacts with customers around the globe. The Field Operations (FieldOps) organisation is the arm of CommOps that runs all our in-house Centres of Excellence and Greenlight operations in the region. Field Operations supports all business lines and propositions (Mobility verticals like Rides, High-Capacity Vehicles or Autonomous Vehicles, Delivery verticals like Online-Food Delivery, Grocery, and specific propositions like Uber for Business) across X countries in Z languages. It is responsible for customer operations, including customer service, safety support, new partner onboarding, compliance, fraud, account management, manual evaluations of AI bots… The team comprises 7,000+ customer support agents across X countries (12 main centres of excellence and 100 In-Person Greenlights).
**About the Role**
FieldOps is undergoing a structural transformation to become a future-proof, GenAI-enabled, high-impact global operations organization. This transformation spans footprint optimization, operating model redesign, operational discipline and accelerated technology adoption.
The Tech Transformation Director will report directly to the Senior Director, Head of Global FieldOps and co-lead the end-to-end technology and GenAI transformation of the organization. The Tech Transformation Director is accountable for making FieldOps a model tech-enabled operations organization. The role should focus on transformational efforts such as — driving dogfooding, accelerating tech adoption, redesigning workflows for automation, and embedding a culture of tech-first problem solving. The role ensures FieldOps clearly articulates operational requirements, quantifies and tracks ROI, and drives disciplined adoption of technology solutions. The Director’s mandate is to shift FieldOps from volume-driven execution to productivity- and impact-driven performance through structured automation, workflow redesign, and cultural change
**What You’ll Do**
**_Own the business requirement of the FieldOps Tech roadmap_**
- Co-develop and execute a multi-year technology transformation roadmap aligned with the FieldOps transformation.
- Prioritize and sequence initiatives to unlock short-term efficiency gains (2026 impact) while building long-term structural capabilities.
- Identify and operationalize high-ROI opportunities in workflow redesign, automation and GenAI agent-assist.
- Partner with Product Ops, Product and Engineering teams to influence roadmap priorities based on operational value and impact.
**_Lead GenAI integration into frontline operations_**
- Drive the adoption of GenAI-powered solutions (agent assist like summarization and translation, quality monitoring…)
- Redesign workflows to integrate AI effectively — recognizing that scaling AI requires new operating processes, not just new tools.
- Build subject-matter depth in frontline workflows to ensure AI investments are outcome-driven.
**_Drive Workflow Automation and Structural Efficiency_**
- Assess the current ecosystem (Bliss and non-Bliss tools) and identify fragmentation and inefficiencies.
- Lead efforts to simplify tool stacks and increase operational coherence.
- Improve ownership clarity for workflows and tools across CommOps and cross-functional partners.
- Lead a systematic review of FieldOps workflows to identify, prioritize, and execute automation opportunities beyond incremental improvements.
- Define and own a structured automation pipeline across all COEs, including rule-based automation, GenAI-enabled automation, workflow elimination and system integrations.
- Establish clear automation targets (e.g., % automation rate, reduction in manual touches, productivity uplift).
- Partner with Product Ops, Product, Engineering, and Data teams to accelerate the implementation of automation solutions with measurable ROI, ensuring the right technologies are deployed while prioritizing scalable and reusable solutions.
- Ensure automation initiatives are tied to headcount strategy, footprint decisions, and quality improvement and cost-efficiency targets.
- Drive cultural adoption by repositioning automation as a value-creation lever rather than a threat to scope.
- Monitor and report on automation impact (quality, compliance, productivity, cost reduction).
**_Embed technology into the Operating Model_**
- Redesign metrics and incentives to reward productivity, quality and AI adoption rather than pure throughput.
- Integrate productivity visibility tools and monitoring best practices to strengthen operational discipline.
- Deploy GenAI-powered 'Leader Co-pilots' to automate administrative overhead, including instant synthesis of agent performance data, automated 1:1 prep kits, and real-time identification of coaching opportunities from complex contact transcripts
- Partner with Workforce Management, Analytics, and L&D to ensure technology adoption translates into measurable impact.
- Partner with the CST Experience team to drive adoption of new technologies and reinforce the transformation narrative across FieldOps.
- Support the shift from a volume-driven mindset to a productivity- and impact-driven culture.
**_Act as a strategic partner to FieldOps leadership_**
- Act as the internal expert, constantly benchmarking against best-in-class CX technology and bringing innovative, industry-leading Generative AI and Agentic AI solutions into the organization.
- Provide data-driven insights on ROI, cost efficiency, and quality uplift from technology initiatives.
- Contribute to footprint and sourcing decisions by assessing automation potential and capability requirements.
- Serve as the executive interface with Tech and Product.
**Basic Qualifications**
- 10+ years of progressive leadership experience in operations transformation, tech-enabled operations, consulting, or product-operations roles preferably in a high-growth, high-volume technology company.
- Deep understanding of AI/GenAI use cases in support environments (agent assist, automation, quality audit augmentation, etc.).
- Demonstrated experience deploying AI/GenAI solutions in operational settings.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Proven track record leading large-scale transformation programs in complex, global organizations.
- Strong exposure to contact center or service operations environments.
- Experience influencing senior stakeholders across Tech, Product, and Operations.
- Ability to translate operational workflows into technical requirements and vice versa.
- Strong financial acumen, including ROI modeling and cost-efficiency analysis.
- Comfort navigating complex system landscapes and tool integrations.
- Highly structured thinker with strong strategic clarity.
- Comfortable operating in ambiguity and driving change in environments with cultural resistance.
- Ability to balance long-term architecture with short-term impact delivery.
- Exceptional executive-level communication and presentation skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders and manage executive alignment across competing priorities
- High ownership, bias for action, and ability to challenge legacy ways of working.
**What Success Looks Like (12-18 Months)**
- Measurable productivity uplift across key workflows.
- Increased quality and compliance standards enabled by AI augmentation.
- Reduced tool fragmentation and clearer workflow ownership.
- Stronger strategic positioning of FieldOps as a tech-enabled partner.
- Tangible cost efficiency gains aligned with 2026 and 2027 targets.
- Clear roadmap and governance model for AI-enabled operations.
For New York, NY-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$239,000 per year - USD$265,500 per year.

title: Growth Strategy Operations Associate, Uber Direct, Denmark
description:
**About the Role**
Alongside Uber Eats, we are launching Uber Direct in Denmark. Uber Direct is our white-label delivery service that enables merchants to offer fast, same-day delivery from their own websites, apps, or stores — with no need for customers to use the Uber app.
You will own the initial GTM commercial strategy (pipeline creation and sales) for the market. As we launch our first merchants, you will then transition to also focus on scaling our merchants’ operations and driving long-term commercial health of our partnerships. You will be the vital link that translates high-level digital strategy into scalable, on-the-ground delivery operations.
Success here requires intrinsic drive to solve tough problems and the ability to influence and align stakeholders with competing priorities. You must stay steady under pressure, using data to navigate ambiguity and drive results for your partners. You will build deep expertise in the local delivery ecosystem and help define the standard for how the Danish market experiences on-demand last mile delivery.
**What You'll Deliver**
As the first Uber Direct hire in Denmark, you will be responsible for:
1. **Sales**: Closing the best partnerships with the right merchants, at the right commercials, with a strong cross-functional commercial team. You will work alongside our Restaurant and Retail teams to identify the right GTM approach (pipeline and commercial strategy) and win our first accounts to go live.
2. **Account Management**: Driving growth, operational excellence and financial sustainability with our live merchant partners. Ensuring the end-to-end experience for our merchants is easy, and operations flow with high levels of reliability. Work closely with our scaled Operations team to escalate, resolve and improve when our experience is not at the desired level.
3. **Financial and operational efficiency**: You will build long-term relationships with your key accounts and be the main point of contact across the org, from Store Manager to HQ, focusing on improving their operations and optimizing our commercials.
4. **Analysis**: Quantify everything you do. Utilize an analytical approach to understand the performance of initiatives to ensure you are effectively prioritizing those with the highest return on investment.
5. **Be proactive**: This role will require fast thinking and moving swiftly through high-pressure environments.
This is the ideal role for someone with a strategic mindset coupled with next-level sales and account management skills and an operational curiosity. You will have the rare opportunity to launch a market from scratch, pilot new features, operating models, and commercial structures, which can be playbooked and scaled across Uber.
**What You Bring to the Table**
- **2+ years** of customer or client facing experience ideally in account management or consulting. Previous experience in setting and achieving business targets
- **Critical thinking and problem solving capabilities**: Experience with challenging the status quo and identifying the root cause of hard problems to solve.
- **Analytical skills**: Comfortable with data analysis, spreadsheets and presenting findings and recommendations. Data driven decision making mentality and sound business judgment through strong analytical thinking.
- **Commercial acumen**: Understanding of how businesses and organizations work and how to navigate a partner’s business. Ability to build sales pitches, negotiate deals and creative commercial strategies for our partners in a fast-growing market.
- **Communication**: Excellent communication skills and the ability to manage and influence diverse stakeholders across businesses and levels.
- **Project management**: Able to create meticulously detailed execution plans for large-scale projects (e.g. launching a new product, onboarding a major enterprise) and then deliver them to plan regardless of any surprises or ambiguity along the way
- **Stakeholder management**: Experience working with cross-functional stakeholders, e.g., Commercial, Operations, Product (Tech), Finance and Legal teams.
- **Highly autonomous self-starter,** comfortable with ambiguity: Able to come up with new ideas and execute them autonomously. Eager to learn with a #gogetit attitude. You should not be afraid to roll up your sleeves and get your hands dirty!
- **Danish and English** fluency are a must!
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Knowledge of Last Mile Delivery space
- SQL experience
- Other Nordic languages (Swedish, Norwegian, Finnish)

title: Partner Manager, Retail, Poland
description:
**About The Role**
Uber Poland is looking for a Partner Manager - a hands-on generalist who can own business relationships - to shape and scale our new Retail vertical.
This role calls for a combination of project management, relationship management, operational optimization, data analysis and complex problem-solving.
You should be comfortable in an "all hands on deck" environment, love solving problems, thrive in a startup culture, and be passionate about driving excellent customer experience.
**About The Team**
Retail vertical is a startup within Uber, a team focussed on growth, but with the support and leverage of Uber’s large global delivery and ridesharing businesses. It is the delivery of everything that isn’t restaurant food - think groceries, essentials, alcohol, pharmaceuticals, beauty, electronics, and more... We are creating something brand new!
Many of the problems we face are new for Uber and require creative solutions that cater to different customers. If you have the curiosity, passion, and collaborative spirit, work with us, and let’s move the world forward together.
**What You’ll Do**
- **Growth**: Develop plans in collaboration with your Retail accounts to grow their business through increasing same-location sales (i.e. marketing, SKU range, operating hours expansion), launching new locations (i.e. locations deployment, expansion support), experimenting with new business models (i.e. dark stores) or other ideas you come up with.
- **Account management**: Be the main point of contact at HQ level for your accounts. You will create long-term relationships with the key accounts, ultimately maximizing their financials, marketing efforts, engagement and satisfaction. You'll be a key influence in their delivery business.
- **Data analysis and operational optimization**: Quantify everything you do. Utilize data on decision-making to increase performance and act on identified issues to ensure you meet the objectives. Last but not least, be a master of process building.
- **Stakeholders and project management:** Collaborate closely with operations, marketing and business development to ensure the team is meeting the growth objectives. Be a strategic thought partner in regard to the growth of Retail partners.
- **Marketing initiatives:** Build the category with the help of marketing initiatives in-store and digitally to make sure we educate our users into the Retail vertical.
**What You'll Need**
- **Comprehensive experience:** 2-4 years of experience in operations, consulting, business development, account management, project management, or enterprise partnerships.
- **Autonomous & proactive**: Able to work with minimal oversight, and to move forward from issues management to push growth initiatives and projects that will add value to our partners and help them fulfill their potential on Uber Eats. You are able to take initiative and easily adapt to the constantly-changing work environment.
- **Problem-solving**: When you see a problem, you work hard and think out of the box. You don’t stop until you fix it.Project management: You develop meticulously detailed KPI improvement plans in collaboration with partners and then execute them successfully, regardless of any surprises or ambiguity along the way.
- **Relationship building:** You build strong connections with both external partners and internal stakeholders, backed by outstanding communication and interpersonal skills. You can persuasively communicate recommendations and effectively champion your partners’ needs.
- **Data-driven judgement**: You leverage solid analytical thinking and commercial judgment to assess partner performance and financial viability, ensuring you make informed decisions that drive mutual success.
- **Hard skills**: Fluency in English and Polish, proficiency in Google Sheets, experience with SQL.
**Ready to ride?**

description:
### **About the Role**
Uber Eats is the fastest-growing food delivery platform in the world! Our team's work at Uber Eats directly impacts and continues to transform our communities. The **Merchant Intelligence** team is at the heart of this mission, building the foundational systems that help Uber Eats better understand, represent, and categorize every merchant on our platform.
As a Machine Learning Engineer on this team, you will focus on improving the quality, consistency, and usability of merchant-related data at a global scale. You will leverage our ML platform to build models that serve critical use cases across Uber, including **Sales and Outreach, Onboarding, Ads and Offers, and Feed Optimization**. This is a unique opportunity to work on large-scale systems where your ML solutions will directly power merchant selection and product experiences for millions of users.
### **What You’ll Do**
- **Innovate and Productionize ML Models:** Design and deploy state-of-the-art machine learning models to automate merchant data reconciliation, entity resolution, and data quality improvements.
- **Build Scalable ML Systems:** Architect and maintain end-to-end large-scale ML pipelines that ingest and process complex merchant datasets to power downstream products like Home Feed and Ads.
- **Feature Engineering:** Develop robust merchant embeddings and features that improve the precision of sales outreach and the efficiency of the merchant onboarding process.
- **Enhance Data Foundations:** Improve the ML quality, model serving foundation, and data infrastructure specifically for merchant intelligence.
- **Cross-Functional Collaboration:** Partner closely with Product, Backend Engineering, and Platform teams to translate business needs into scalable ML solutions.
- **Incremental Impact:** Maintain a bias toward shipping incremental improvements that have a clear, measurable impact on user experience and business growth.
### **Basic Qualifications**
- **Experience:** PhD or Master in relevant fields (CS, EE, Math, Stats, etc.) with recommendation system research experiences and 4 years minimum of industry experience with a strong focus on machine learning and recommendation systems.
- **Technical Proficiency:** Strong coding skills in at least one language such as **Python, Java, or Go**.
- **ML Frameworks:** Expertise with modern ML frameworks such as **PyTorch or TensorFlow**.
- **Systems Design:** Experience building and productionizing innovative, end-to-end Machine Learning systems that handle large or complex datasets.
### **Preferred Qualifications**
- **Domain Expertise:** Experience in simplifying and converting complex business problems (like data consistency and merchant classification) into actionable ML problems.
- **Large-Scale Systems:** Demonstrated ability to develop complex software systems scaling to millions of users with high reliability and monitoring.
- **Big Data Tools:** Familiarity with data processing and streaming tools such as **Spark, Hive, Kafka, or Cassandra**.
- **Advanced ML Techniques:** Experience with NLP, graph machine learning, or entity resolution is highly advantageous given the team's focus on merchant data.
- **Mentorship:** Proven track record of mentoring junior engineers and driving engineering excellence within a team.
- **Communication:** Strong teamwork and communication skills to effectively collaborate with stakeholders across the organization.

title: Operations & Logistics Manager
description:
**About the Role**
The Vertical Ops team's mission is to power ACAC Territories by partnering with teams to ideate and execute scalable solutions to strategic priorities. As an Operations Associate in this role, you will lead our efforts to continue making Uber the safest platform in the Andean, Central American and the Caribbean regions.
In this role, you will serve as the regional safety expert, driving impact through: analyzing incident rates and delivering actionable insights to Central and Local Operations; identifying and implementing best practices; designing scalable solutions to improve safety in our territories; building compelling business cases for safety initiatives; and partnering with Central Ops and other cross-functional teams on feature launches and related safety initiatives.
We are seeking a highly motivated and results-oriented individual with strong analytical and problem-solving skills, stakeholder management capabilities, and a genuine passion for safety and compliance. You will drive innovative solutions in a fast-paced environment.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
1. **Safety Initiative Assessment & Implementation:** Evaluate new initiatives from the Regional Safety Team, address questions, and provide recommendations to GMs and cross-functional teams for approval. Post-launch, monitor performance and communicate findings to local teams.
2. **Safety Feature Improvements/Rollouts:** Lead the rollout and enhancement of safety features across ACAC countries. Advocate for safety and compliance products/processes regionally, while managing and owning our safety controls.
3. **Safety Data Analysis & Risk Mitigation:** Leverage regional incident data to analyze trends and perform deep dives to identify safety improvement opportunities and generate actionable insights. Partner with the Safety Operations team to design and implement processes that address identified gaps and mitigate emerging safety-related risk patterns.
4. **Safety Perception Monitoring & improvement:** Measure safety perception for riders and drivers through quarterly surveys to share insights, identify opportunities, and develop improvement initiatives in collaboration with the Safety Operations team.
5. **Incident Investigation & Support:** Monitor social media and external sources to identify safety cases, escalate them to Incident Response teams, and ensure visibility across relevant stakeholders. Provide support in serious incidents by performing initial investigations, sharing insights that support subsequent actions, and offering recommendations based on findings.
6. **New cities/product launches support:** Support local teams when launching a new country, city, or product by enabling safety features, configuring safety-related settings, and providing safety recommendations.
7. **Project Management & Execution:** Plan and organize complex, multi-faceted projects with cross-functional teams. You will define clear timelines, deliverables, and resource allocations to operationalize processes in a fast-paced environment.
8. **Stand for Safety:** Be a driving force in transforming Uber's internal safety culture, helping to ensure we put safety at the heart of all that we do
**Basic Qualifications**
1. 4+ years of professional experience in operations, strategy, consulting, or a high-growth environment.
2. Data-driven mindset with experience getting "in the weeds" of data analysis to drive big-picture strategic decisions.
3. Proficiency in Spanish and English. Demonstrated experience interpreting and presenting data to various audiences in an engaging and comprehensive, yet succinct manner
4. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced and ambiguous environment
**Preferred Qualifications**
1. **Planning & Organization:** Ability to plan complex or multi-faceted projects with cross-functional teams, defining timelines, deliverables, interdependencies, and resource allocation
2. **Executional Excellence:** Ability to optimally manage and prioritize complex operations that require the support of peers and other teams, and deliver results in a fast-paced and ambiguous environment, while maintaining high-quality work standards
3. **Stakeholder Management**: Ability to engage, inform, and gain the support of various stakeholders across multiple seniority levels, being able to build helpful relationships; earning trust of others within the organization through strong situational awareness and reliability as well as reactive incident response

title: IAM Operations Engineer II
description:
**About the role and team**
As an IAM Operations Engineer, you’ll support the day-to-day identity and access management ecosystem, ensuring secure, compliant, and efficient access across enterprise platforms. You’ll focus on operational excellence through automation, AI-driven workflows, and bot-based process improvements, partnering closely with Security, IT, and Compliance teams to deliver scalable identity services.
**What you’ll do**
- Administer and maintain enterprise IAM platforms including Okta, Active Directory, Azure AD, and SailPoint IIQ to ensure global availability and security.
- Build and maintain automation scripts and bot flows using Python or PowerShell to streamline the user lifecycle, from seamless onboarding to secure de-provisioning.
- Integrate IAM workflows with AI-powered chatbots and RPA frameworks to enable self-service access, reducing manual intervention and improving response times.
- Leverage AI/ML-based insights to detect identity anomalies, enforce the principle of least privilege, and strengthen our Zero Trust architecture.
- Monitor system health and operational metrics using dashboards in Tableau or Jira to proactively identify and resolve bottlenecks before they impact the business.
- Collaborate cross-functionally with Security, IT, and Compliance teams to implement automated incident response playbooks that remediate IAM issues at scale.
**Basic Qualifications**
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Security, or a related field;
- 6+ years of experience in Identity and Access Management (IAM) operations or IT security administration.
- Hands-on experience with Okta, Active Directory, and Azure AD administration.
- Proficiency in Python, PowerShell, or Java scripting for process automation.
- Experience with Terraform or similar Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) principles for IAM deployment.
- Working knowledge of authentication and authorization protocols including SAML, OAuth, OIDC, and LDAP.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Hands-on exposure to AI/ML models, RPA, or bot frameworks specifically for security or chat-based workflows.
- Experience integrating IAM platforms with security monitoring tools like SIEM or SOAR for automated threat response.
- Deep understanding of Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) and implementing Least Privilege Access (LPA) at scale.
- Familiarity with compliance frameworks such as SOX, GDPR, or ISO 27001 in a global enterprise environment.

title: Staff Software Engineer - Media Platform (Backend)
location: San Francisco, California, United States; Sunnyvale, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
The Media Platform team builds the unified backend platform that powers media ingestion, processing, intelligence, and delivery for Uber’s Safety ecosystem. We enable real-time streaming, media processing, media intelligence, and compliant storage at global scale so Safety teams can build impactful products without worrying about scale, privacy, or infrastructure.
As a Staff Backend Engineer, you will shape the architecture and technical direction of a platform that directly impacts rider and driver safety worldwide. You will help build the systems that empower Uber to prevent safety risks, respond in real time, and drive accurate, trustworthy decisions using intelligent media insights.
**What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points**
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
1. Architect and evolve Uber’s Media Platform backend systems for ingestion, processing, storage, and delivery of real-time and recorded media.
2. Lead cross-team technical initiatives that improve reliability, scalability, cost efficiency, and compliance of media systems.
3. Define and enforce platform standards for performance, privacy, retention, and operational excellence.
4. Partner with Safety, Legal, and Product teams to translate complex requirements into scalable platform capabilities.
5. Mentor senior engineers and act as a technical multiplier across the organization.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. 10+ years of backend engineering experience, with deep expertise in distributed systems and large-scale service architecture.
2. Strong proficiency in one or more backend languages (e.g., Go, Java, or similar) and experience building high-availability microservices.
3. Proven experience designing and operating systems that handle high throughput, low latency, and strict reliability requirements.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Experience building or operating media systems (e.g., video ingestion, streaming pipelines, transcoding, storage, CDN integration). Familiarity with media container formats, codecs, and streaming protocols such as MP4, HEVC, H.264, VP9, JPEG, HLS, and DASH.
2. Strong understanding of event-driven architectures (Kafka/PubSub), real-time streaming systems, and asynchronous processing pipelines.
3. Experience working with privacy, data retention, compliance, and security-sensitive systems.
4. Demonstrated Staff-level impact: technical leadership across teams, setting architecture direction, mentoring senior engineers, and driving multi-quarter initiatives.

location: Seattle, Washington, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Sunnyvale, California, United States
description:
**About the Role (Sr AI/ML Engineer : Not Data Scientist)**
Core Security Engineering’s mission is to make the Uber production environment secure by default and provide industry leading products and services to all Uber's production services and infrastructure. We are focused on building both security primitives and end users products that help Uber engineers to secure their service, build trust, and advance security to enable our global business.
We are responsible for providing and managing systems, services, and libraries to provide access management, and enforcement at scale. The scope spans across multiple verticals like service-to-service authentication/authorization, employee to system auth, customer auth.
You’ll work on critical distributed services at a massive scale crafted with the best security practices at the forefront. You’ll be accountable for designing and implementing the AI/ML based solutions to continuously scale and operate such foundational security services.
What the Candidate Will Do ----
1. Translate business and security needs into well-defined problem statements and solve them with the AI-first mindset.
2. Develop, iterate, and productionize ML models that simplify access management and control.
3. Integrate ML systems into Uber’s critical systems (Identity, Access, Authorization).
4. Collaborate across Security, Risk, and Infra teams to deliver scalable, production-ready solutions.
5. Provide leadership by mentoring junior engineers, evangelize ML best practices, and help shape ML strategy within AI Secury.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. 5+ years experience in formulating ML problems from ambiguous business requirements, especially in risk, fraud, or security contexts.
2. Proficiency across a broad range of ML algorithms: tree-based models (XGBoost, LightGBM), classical statistical models (logistic regression, SVMs), and deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers), with the ability to select and apply the right approach based on context and data characteristics.
3. Hands-on experience with feature engineering, model development, and productionization of ML pipelines.
4. Proficiency in PyTorch, TensorFlow, or similar ML frameworks, and in Python or comparable languages for scalable, production-grade systems.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Proven ability to own ML systems end-to-end: from requirement discovery → feature design → modeling → deployment.
2. Deep experience with advanced ML techniques, including ensemble methods, neural networks, graph-based models, and handling challenges like imbalanced data, feedback loops, and iterative retraining.
3. Familiarity with large-scale data/infra systems (Kafka, Pinot, Hive, Cassandra, Spark, Flink).
4. Background in access control, authentication, or enterprise security systems.
5. Track record of technical leadership: mentoring engineers, driving cross-functional initiatives, or shaping ML/security strategy.

description:

The Sr. Executive Recruiter - Tech role is responsible for attracting and recruiting the world’s most sought after executives at Uber on a global scale. This team supports our Executive Leadership Team (ELT) in hiring our executives (Director+) roles across our Technology team. title: Staff Machine Learning Engineer, Rider Pricing & Incentives
location: San Francisco, California, United States; Sunnyvale, California, United States
description:
**About the Team**
Uber Marketplace is at the heart of Uber’s business, with Rider Pricing & Incentives playing a critical role by setting prices and targeting promotions for Uber riders. Our team’s mission is to drive revenue growth, ridership growth and Uber’s profitability through advanced machine learning and data science. We build reliable, scalable platforms that optimize rider pricing, real-time and offline promotions, including discounts, and personalized messaging.
The Rider Pricing & Incentives team is a fast-moving, high-opportunity space where you’ll have the chance to make a significant impact on the business. You’ll take ownership of one key pillar of the promotions or rider pricing domain and lead the technical direction for improving our pricing algorithms, promotion algorithms and models. You will work across ML, serving, and optimization system components, and set technical direction for modeling best practices across model building, evaluation and deployment.
**About the Role**
In this role, you'll apply advanced machine learning technologies—including deep learning, generative AI for personalized communications, causal modeling, and reinforcement learning—to optimize pricing strategies and promotional systems. You will also work with serving infrastructure and product teams to design and evolve the rider pricing and promotions systems to support new product and algorithm evolutions, promotion use cases and drive Uber’s top-line rider and revenue growth.
**What You'll Do**
- Lead a group of SWEs and MLEs working on rider pricing and promotions to develop and implement new machine learning and optimization techniques powering billions of rides around the world, and helping riders achieve their mobility needs.
- Improve the performance of models and algorithms powering pricing algorithms and promotion targeting.
- Own the problem E2E, including working with cross-functional teams to define the product and/or technical roadmap.
- Mentor more junior team members by role modeling ML best practices. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment and drive Uber’s ridership and revenue growth. Help Uber’s end-users by making mobility options accessible and affordable.
**Basic Qualifications**
- Masters degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field, with 7+ years of full-time engineering experience.
- Proficiency in one or more programming languages (e.g., C, C++, Java, Python, Go).
- Experience with machine learning and optimization algorithms.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- PhD in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field, with 2+ years of full-time engineering experience.
- Experience solving complex business problems by translating them into machine learning and optimization solutions.
- Familiarity with large-scale data systems (e.g., Spark, Hive) and experience building production-ready algorithmic systems.
- Strong background in deep learning, generative AI, causal modeling, and reinforcement learning.

title: Mid-Market Account Executive, Uber for Business, Nordics
description:
As an Account Executive, you will be responsible for driving the expansion of the Uber for Business platform across the **Nordics (Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Finland)**. You will manage the full sales cycle from prospecting high-growth mid-market leads to navigating complex, value-driven negotiations and ensuring successful long-term adoption in one of our most digitally mature markets.
### What You Will Do
- **Business Development:** Proactively prospect and close new partnerships within mid-market companies (100–5,000 employees) across the Nordic region.
- **Strategic Sales:** Execute a comprehensive go-to-market plan, leading consultative, value-based conversations with C-suite and departmental stakeholders.
- **Onboarding & Growth:** Lead client implementation to ensure high product adoption, focusing on the seamless integration of Uber’s travel and delivery solutions into corporate workflows.
- **Pipeline Management:** Maintain high-velocity sales activity and provide accurate forecasting for the Nordic territory using Salesforce.
- **Market Leadership:** Navigate local market nuances, remove barriers to entry, and act as a critical feedback loop for product teams to localize features for the Nordic market.

- **Mindset:** A self-starter with a "bias for action" and the ability to operate independently in a remote or satellite office environment.

title: Staff Machine Learning Engineer
description:
**About the Role**
Applied AI is a horizontal AI team at Uber partnering with product and platform teams across the company to deliver cutting-edge machine learning solutions for core business problems. We specialize in areas like Generative AI, Computer Vision, Personalization, and the ML infrastructure needed to scale these systems in production.
We’re looking for a Staff Engineer with deep experience in classical machine learning, generative AI, and ML systems design to help build and scale high-impact AI solutions. In this role, you’ll lead technically complex projects, influence the architecture of ML systems, and collaborate cross-functionally to drive innovation and impact across multiple Uber surfaces.
This is a great opportunity for a strong technical leader who thrives in a fast-paced, product-driven environment and wants to be at the forefront of applied AI at scale.
**What the Candidate Will Do:**
1. Design and implement ML-driven systems that power core Uber experiences, with a focus on scalability, reliability, and performance.
2. Lead the technical execution of key projects involving classical ML, deep learning, and generative AI technologies (e.g., LLMs, multimodal models).
3. Collaborate closely with product, data science, and infrastructure teams to develop AI solutions from ideation through production deployment.
4. Contribute to and influence the technical direction for Applied AI, particularly around system design, model architecture, and infrastructure decisions.
5. Champion engineering best practices in ML development — including experimentation workflows, model versioning, evaluation, monitoring, and responsible AI.
6. Provide mentorship to engineers on the team and across partner orgs to help raise the technical bar.
**What the Candidate Will Need:**
1. 10+ years of industry experience in machine learning or software engineering, with a proven record of delivering ML solutions to production.
2. Strong knowledge of machine learning, deep learning, and exposure to generative AI techniques (e.g., transformers, LLMs, diffusion).
3. Experience designing and scaling ML systems or platforms, including training pipelines, serving infrastructure, and model lifecycle tooling.
4. Fluency in ML frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX) and development in Python and/or scalable backend languages (e.g., Java, Go).
5. Excellent collaboration and communication skills with the ability to work across teams and functions.

title: Senior Staff Machine Learning Engineer – Moonshot AI
description:
**About the role**
The **Moonshot AI team** sits within Uber AI Solutions where we are building an enterprise AI data platform for leading AI labs and companies worldwide. We're a small, rapidly growing team tackling some of the most challenging problems in AI-assisted data annotation and validation, which means you'll have exceptional scope, ownership, and direct influence over both technical direction and business outcomes.
In this role, your focus will be on delivering machine learning solutions across four core areas:
1. **Marketplace Optimization:** Building intelligent supply-demand matching and ranking models to optimize our global gig marketplace.
2. **Custom Model Development:** Training custom models to automate critical annotation workflows for audio, video, and text data.
3. **Automated Quality Evaluation:** Utilizing GenAI and LLM-as-Judge frameworks to execute automated, enterprise-scale quality evaluation.
4. **ML Research:** Publish robust benchmarks designed to identify loss patterns within SOTA models.
You'll own projects end-to-end, from research and prototyping through production deployment, and your work will directly impact revenue growth, customer acquisition, and our competitive positioning. You'll collaborate with product managers, engineers, and cross-functional partners, in a fast paced startup-like environment.
**What You'll Do:**
- **Shape the technical vision and roadmap** for Moonshot AI's ML initiatives, identifying strategic investments that advance both technical excellence and business objectives
- **Architect foundational ML platforms and systems** for marketplace optimization and annotation automation—designing scalable, reliable solutions that serve as the technical foundation for multiple product areas
- **Drive end-to-end ML solutions** from conception through production deployment, owning critical technical decisions on architecture, tooling, and infrastructure that impact millions of tasks and revenue growth
- **Lead GenAI innovation**: design and implement cutting-edge systems using custom SLMs, computer vision, and LLMs to provide ML assistance for annotations across audio, video, and text workflows while establishing quality standards
- **Advance AI research capabilities**: establish research direction, design benchmarks, contribute to research and publications that positions Uber AI Solutions as a thought leader
- **Build industry-leading evaluation frameworks**: architect LLM-as-Judge systems and automated quality assessment platforms that set the standard for how AI outputs are validated at enterprise scale
- **Provide technical leadership** across Uber AI Solutions—collaborating with engineering, product, and data science leadership to align technical roadmaps with business strategy
- **Mentor and develop engineering talent**: lead and mentor Staff, Senior, and mid-level ML engineers, establishing best practices for ML system design, experimentation, and deployment while raising technical standards across the organization
- **Enable cross-functional impact**: partner with ML Ops, backend, and platform teams to ensure solutions demonstrate craftsmanship, reliability, and scalability while managing upstream and downstream dependencies
**Basic Qualifications:**
- 10+ years of industry experience developing and shipping production machine learning models
- Ph.D., MS, or Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a closely related discipline
- Proven track record of technical leadership on large-scale ML initiatives with measurable business impact
- Deep expertise across multiple areas: Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing, Deep Learning, and Generative AI
- Strong proficiency with modern ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX) and programming languages
- Extensive experience with distributed training infrastructure, large-scale model development, and ML platform design
- Demonstrated ability to collaborate with product, engineering, and data science leadership on technical roadmaps and strategic priorities
- Excellent problem-solving abilities with deep ML methodology expertise
**Preferred Qualifications:**

- 12+ years of ML/AI experience with demonstrated technical leadership across multiple organizations or product areas
- Publications at top-tier AI/ML conferences demonstrating research impact

- Deep experience with ML platform engineering including model serving, feature stores, experiment platforms, and observability systems
- Domain expertise in marketplace optimization, recommendation systems, multi-armed bandits, or anomaly detection
- Experience shaping technical vision and roadmaps for ML organizations
- Proven ability to mentor senior engineers and elevate technical standards across teams
- Background driving dataset development, including data collection, processing, labeling pipelines, and quality frameworks

title: Director, Product Management - Offers & Affordability
location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
[**Uber Eats**](https://www.ubereats.com/) is growing rapidly, and we are looking for a **Director of Product, Offers & Affordability** to lead the financial engine of our delivery business.
In this role, you will own the strategy and execution for a portfolio responsible for **$6B+ in annual offers redemption** and **hundreds of millions in profit contribution**. You will solve one of the hardest problems in delivery: How do we make food and retail delivery affordable for the everyday consumer while ensuring sustainable economics for our restaurant and merchant partners?
This isn't a role for someone who wants a polished playbook; it’s for a leader who can turn complexity into clarity at speed, navigate the often messy trade-offs of a three-sided marketplace, and lead through influence rather than control. You will build the tools that allow a local pizzeria or a global grocery chain to fund promotions effectively, and the consumer-facing experiences that help millions of people find their next meal at a price that works for them.
**What You’ll Do**
- **Steward a Massive P&L:** Own the product roadmap for the Eats Offers platform, maximizing order volume through $6B+ in redemptions while rigorously optimizing for incremental profit contribution and unit economics.
- **Lead & Scale the Team:** Manage and mentor a high-performing team of ~10 Product Managers, helping them navigate high-pressure trade-offs between growth, margin, and user experience.
- **Revolutionize the Merchant Experience:** Build self-serve growth tools that empower everyone from local pizzerias to global grocers to launch campaigns (BOGOs, "Spend $X Save $Y"), and prove ROI to merchants, ensuring they understand that every dollar they invest in offers drives incremental orders and loyal customers.
- **Elevate the Eater Experience:** Design intuitive, personalized discovery experiences that surface the right deal to the right eater, and reduce " _sticker shock_" at checkout by intelligently applying affordability mechanics that increase conversion and retention.
- **Navigate Global Complexity:** Work seamlessly across Latin America, EMEA, APAC, and North America, adapting product strategy to fit diverse market realities like high-frequency behavior in Taiwan vs. high-AOV family ordering in the US.
- **Bridge the Digital and Real Worlds:** Collaborate across engineering, data science, and local operations to ship practical solutions that work in the real world, often under tight constraints and shifting priorities.
- **Drive Technical Depth:** Partner with highly technical teams to answer complex questions about elasticity, cannibalization, and incrementality using large-scale datasets.
**What You’ll Need**
- **Quantitative Foundation:** Ph.D., M.S., or Bachelor’s degree in Math, Economics, Statistics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field.
- **Product Leadership:** 10+ years of product management experience, with at least 5 years dedicated to hiring and leading high-performing product teams.
- **Scale & Responsibility:** Proven experience managing high-scale consumer-facing mobile app products and/or B2B merchant tools.
**Bonus Points If**
- **Domain Expertise:** Deep understanding of delivery or retail unit economics, including basket sizes, service fees, and merchant margins.
- **Marketplace Mindset:** Experience designing for both sides of a marketplace—from friction-free consumer "Claim" buttons to complex B2B merchant dashboards.
- **Operational Grit:** Proven ability to partner with international operations teams and build global platforms that allow local General Managers to pull the right levers for their markets.
- **Systems Thinking:** Ability to use large datasets to solve for long-term sustainability while maintaining high execution velocity.
**Perks**
- The rare opportunity to change the world such that everyone around you is using the product you built. We’re not just another social web app, we’re moving real people and assets and reinventing transportation and logistics globally.
- Sharp, motivated co-workers in a fun office environment.
**Benefits (U.S.)**
You may be eligible for bonuses, equity, and other compensation, as well as a range of benefits. Our U.S. benefits include a 401(k) plan, ESPP, full medical/dental/vision package, gym reimbursement, and monthly Uber credits. We also offer an unlimited vacation policy. [Explore our benefits](https://jobs.uber.com/en/benefits/).

title: Software engineer II, Backend - Platform
description:
**About the Role**
Please note: this hybrid position is based in São Paulo, Brazil - welcoming both local professionals and those open to relocating to São Paulo.
The **Login Services team** sits within **Core Security Engineering** and owns Uber’s authentication platform for both **human and non-human identities**. We build the systems that make login **seamless, secure, and resilient** for Uber’s global workforce and services, so people and workloads can confidently access internal and third-party applications using **strong authentication** with **minimal operational cost**.
In this position you will be building the future of authentication at Uber—especially as **agentic workflows** and **non-human identities** become first-class citizens. You’ll drive architecture and execution across major initiatives like building a **security knowledge platform** that computes **risk levels for identities and assets** (human + NHI), and evolving next-generation authentication capabilities (e.g., stronger factors and policy enforcement across critical systems). If you’re excited about building foundational security platforms at massive scale (where reliability, security posture, and automation directly move the business) this is a rare chance to be in a space that’s getting more important every quarter.
### **What You'll Do**
- Own and deliver a well-scoped slice of Uber’s authentication stack (human + non-human identities), improving security and login reliability.
- Build/extend core auth services and workflows (APIs, policy checks, token/session flows) under existing architecture guidance.
- Improve tier-zero SSO/login health via bug fixes, performance tuning, and availability-focused changes.
- Ship production-ready code with strong fundamentals: tests, code quality, safe rollout/rollback, and clear operational docs/runbooks.
- Add observability (metrics/logs/traces), dashboards, and alerts; participate in on-call and drive incident follow-ups to completion.
- Implement targeted automation to reduce IAM operational toil (self-service flows, remediation tools, ticket/ops workflow helpers).
- Contribute to Security Knowledge Platform work by implementing specific data/graph/risk-signal features that power downstream use cases.
- Collaborate cross-functionally on your projects (Security/IT/Ops/Product), communicate risks early, and contribute in design/incident reviews.
### **What You'll Need**
- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or related field; OR 4+ years building backend services or platform systems in production environments.
- Proficiency in at least one major language (Go/Java/Python/TypeScript) and ability to learn the team’s stack if needed.
- Experience building and operating distributed services: APIs, data stores, async workflows, and service-to-service integrations.
- Demonstrated operational excellence: monitoring/alerting, debugging, incident participation, and improving SLAs through reliability work.
- Working knowledge of security fundamentals (auth flows, sessions/tokens, least privilege); identity/auth is a plus but not required.
- Ability to own a component end-to-end for a scoped area: design docs, implementation, rollout/rollback, and long-term maintenance.
- Strong engineering hygiene: tests, code reviews, documentation/runbooks, and measurable improvements over time.
**Bonus Ponits if**
- Hands-on experience building or operating authentication/authorization systems (AuthN/AuthZ) and improving observability (metrics/logs/traces) in production.
- Familiarity with identity/security concepts (SSO, MFA, session/token flows, access controls) and common failure modes at scale.
- Experience contributing to data pipelines or data-backed products (tables/streams/graphs), with an understanding of data quality and reliability.
- Troubleshooting skills in high-availability services: can debug incidents, find root cause, and drive fixes with good follow-through.

title: Sr Operations and Strategy Manager, Marketplace SoCo
description:
**About the Role**
As a member of the **Vertical Operations** team, you will drive strategic oversight for the **South Cone (SoCo) region**. This role focuses on tracking vital Marketplace KPIs across various cities, analyzing performance trends, and designing bespoke initiatives to maintain a robust ecosystem throughout these markets. By synthesizing data into actionable insights, you will ensure long-term platform health and operational excellence across all regional hubs.
Beyond monitoring, you will act as the **regional subject matter expert** for technical integration. This involves vetting and executing the rollout of experimental features and new tools, serving as the primary bridge between local needs and global innovations. As the designated point of contact for technical inquiries, you will translate complex marketplace mechanics into successful regional implementations.
You will work closely with Central Operations and will be responsible for maintaining strong, fluid, and effective communication between Local Operations and Central Operations. Some of the topics that this person will cover: Pricing opportunities, Segmentation Products, Reliability, Pricing tools.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
1. **Strategic Ownership:** Proactively identify business needs and issues. Prioritize among projects to define the best path to meet strategic objectives and complete it. Deliver valuable information to teams with strategic proposals to achieve operational efficiency.
2. **Business Sense:** Translate insights from operational and financial metrics into strategy and action plans. Develop and apply understanding of the levers that move our business. Understand the industry and act effectively in order to anticipate potential changes
3. **Problem Solving:** Identify the root cause of risks and opportunities, propose multiple, practical solutions, and weigh tradeoffs to recommend the best approach. Develop innovative "thinking outside the box" solutions to deliver essential decision-making information to leadership teams
4. **Process Optimization:** Implement, optimize, and scale core analyses and processes across our region.
5. **Product Improvement:** Propose features or improvements that could improve the experience on and efficiency of our marketplace, measuring their impact and prioritizing them, in order to advocate for their development.
**Basic Qualifications**
1. **Years of Experience/Industries**:​​ 5+ years of professional experience.
2. **Data-Driven Mentality:** Make strategic decisions based on data analysis and interpretation. Experience getting in the weeds of data, balanced with the ability to see the big picture
3. **Communication:** Spanish and English proficiency. Demonstrated experience interpreting and presenting data to various audiences in an engaging and comprehensive, yet succinct manner
4. **Teamwork:** Demonstrate optimism, respect others, and work well in a team. Ability to work with people from diverse backgrounds and responsibilities. Maintains healthy working relationships, even in disagreement
5. **Executional Excellence:** Ability to optimally manage and prioritize complex operations that require the support of peers and other teams, and deliver results in a fast-paced and ambiguous environment, while maintaining high-quality work standards
6. **Planning & Organization:** Ability to plan complex or multi-faceted projects with cross-functional teams, defining timelines, deliverables, interdependencies, and resource allocation
7. **Marketplace & Financial Judgment:** Able to assess trade-offs across demand, supply, and economics; anticipate downstream marketplace impacts; and prioritize actions that protect both service quality and P&L performance.
8. **Bias for Execution:** Comfortable making calibrated decisions in ambiguous, fast-moving environments and driving pricing or marketplace levers forward with incomplete data when needed.
9. **Influence Without Authority:** Builds trust with cross-functional stakeholders through clear reasoning, strong analytical grounding, and reliable follow-through—enabling alignment on city-level strategies and actions.
**Preferred Qualifications**
1. Expertise in Data Analytics with spreadsheets as a MUST and Coding (SQL mainly) as a preferred/nice-to-have.
2. Experience managing P&Ls while great knowledge of marketplaces (demand & supply)
