**About the Role**
The U4B Platforms Org in Bangalore is building and evolving the best-in-class business systems for Uber B2B Customers, which enables experiences and tools for reliable, configurable, and intelligent business lifecycle management. The platform enables end-to-end B2B experience enabling cost-efficient business growth through acquisition, retention, and resurrection.
The team is full-stack and cross-functional. There will be significant, challenging problems in the Backend, Frontend, Data and ML engineering spaces, and the team will have embedded collaborators from Product Management, Design, and Data Science.
As part of the team, you will have direct responsibility for vital systems and processes. You will take ownership of key features and initiatives in crafting and building high-quality, scalable systems to make incentives reliable and efficient. Your work will directly impact the lives of Uber’s partners (drivers, couriers, vehicles), riders, eaters and operations teams across the globe.
**What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points**
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
1. You have knowledge of a frontend stack (some examples include: React, Node.js etc).
2. Ability to learn quickly on the go. We're looking for software engineers who thrive while learning new technologies and don't believe in one-size-fits-all solutions. You should be able to easily adapt to meet the needs of our massive growth and rapidly-evolving business environment.
3. Bias toward action. You believe that speed and quality aren't mutually exclusive. You've shown good judgment about shipping as fast as possible while ensuring your products and platforms are built in a sustainable, responsible way--and you're comfortable making mistakes, provided you learn from them.
4. Passion and energy. Because you love what you do, you bring infectious enthusiasm to all your endeavors. You always perform at your absolute best and see new challenges as an opportunity to contribute, make an impact, and grow.
5. Unquenchable thirst for growth. You're not just looking for the next interesting challenge, but looking to push yourself to keep pace in our hyper-growth environment.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
• Masters or equivalent in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field
• 3+ years full-time Software Engineering work experience, including experience in one or more of the following areas:
• Front End Programming and scripting languages
• Designing end-to-end solutions and architecture by working with BE engineers
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Delivered multiple end-to-end projects by owning FrontEnd stack and collaborating with BackEnd engineers.
2. Worked with Ux designers and PMs to understand, influence and implement customer experiences
3. Worked on enterprise grade systems built for scale and are latency and availability sensitive
4. Have a data driven approach, understand meaning and impact of KPIs - worked on extracting data from data backends using SQL-like and nosql-like syntaxes

title: Staff Software Engineer - Fulfillment Logistics
location: San Francisco, California, United States; Sunnyvale, California, United States
description:
**About the Team**
The Fulfillment platform is the foundation powering the logistics behind fulfilling user orders from any Uber app. It operates life cycles of orders, trips, drivers, eaters, and other user sessions when they interact with Uber. These interactions are real-time and high-scale when millions of concurrent riders, drivers, and eaters are using Uber. We support both Uber Rider businesses (pool, airports, hourly rentals, intercity trips, reservations, package delivery, uber health) and Uber Delivery businesses (groceries, uber eats, delivery API).
Our tech challenges are in both the business layer (data modeling of complex products, building reusable higher order business APIs to share across products) and the Infrastructure layer (storing high write & transient data, language framework, search & ranking, events & data streaming systems).
**About the Role**
We are looking for a Software Engineer to join the Fulfillment logistics (Supply and Order Fulfillment) team. Over the next few years, this team will be innovating with new offerings such as Autonomous Vehicles integrations, Multi-Job Orders, Digital and Field Tasks, Third-Party Drivers, and Package Delivery, while reimagining and re-envisioning the Fulfillment Platform end to end. This is a high-impact role where you will partner closely with Engineering, Product, and Operations leaders across the company to launch new products, scale critical platforms, and improve the overall efficiency, reliability, and performance of the Uber marketplace.
You can get a glimpse of the work we do in Fulfillment
1. [https://eng.uber.com/fulfillment-platform-rearchitecture/](https://eng.uber.com/fulfillment-platform-rearchitecture/)
2. [https://eng.uber.com/building-ubers-fulfillment-platform/](https://eng.uber.com/building-ubers-fulfillment-platform/)
**What You'll Do**
- Lead the design and architecture of the next phase of fulfillment and product lines for our 1P, 3P, and SaaS customers, prioritizing scalability, reliability, and efficiency.
- Collaborate with several cross-functional teams to define requirements and translate them into technical specifications.
- Optimize existing Fulfillment platform capabilities for efficiency, speed, and accuracy.
- Devise and implement solutions that seamlessly scale with the growing needs of our user base.
- Mentor junior engineers, fostering a culture of knowledge exchange and continuous improvement
- Partner with engineering managers, product managers, and other engineering teams to define project requirements and execution plans.
- Ensure that system designs, including APIs, architecture, and dependency selection, result in scalable, resilient solutions that minimize support costs and enhance the partner experience.
**Basic Qualifications**
- A Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or a related field (or equivalent experience).
- 8+ years of full-time engineering experience
- Systematic problem-solving approach and knowledge of algorithms, data structures and complexity analysis.
- Strong understanding of distributed systems or stream processing.
- Strong understanding of computer science fundamentals, including object-oriented design, data structures, algorithm design, problem-solving, and complexity analysis.
- Proficient in at least one programming language, such as Golang, Java, C#, or C++.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- 5+ years of experience building and managing distributed systems.
- Highly efficient coding in Golang, Java or any similar languages.
- Comfortable dealing with system complexity
- Knowledgeable in software engineering best practices throughout the entire software development life cycle, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, testing, and operations.
- Great sense of ownership

title: Scaled Coordinator
description:
## About the Role
The Scaled Coordinator role is a great opportunity to apply strong analytical and commercial skills to drive scalable impact across a defined territory. This role focuses on post-sales management for Small-to-Mid Sized Business (SMB) restaurants, identifying efficient, scalable ways to improve retention and accelerate business growth with mutually beneficial outcomes.
Scaled Coordinators play a critical role in supporting Uber Eats’ large SMB partner base. By leveraging data, automation, and scalable processes, they help ensure restaurant partners are successful on the platform while driving improvements in key business metrics for both partners and Uber Eats.
## What the Candidate Will Do
- Lead scalable growth initiatives for the Small-to-Mid Sized restaurant segment, designing and executing growth strategies aligned with territory-specific needs
- Own revenue growth for the SMB segment by driving product adoption, upselling new features, and improving retention through mutual business value
- Own and manage the segment budget, ensuring efficiency while consistently achieving revenue targets
- Set, track, and report on performance metrics for the Small-to-Mid Sized restaurant segment, sharing insights with internal stakeholders
- Act as the voice of SMB restaurant partners internally, translating partner needs into actionable insights and advocating on their behalf across teams
## What You’ll Need
- 2+ years of professional experience, ideally in a commercial, analytical, or operations-focused role
- Strong confidence in making data-driven decisions and applying sound business judgment
- Very strong analytical skills combined with solid business acumen
- Experience leveraging AI tools and automation to execute tasks, build scalable processes, and create dashboards/reporting solutions
## Bonus Points
- Previous accountability for achieving business targets or delivering a quota
- SQL knowledge
- Experience with partner management and cross-functional projects
- Strong executive presence and clear verbal and written communication skills
- A fast-paced, ownership-driven mindset with speed, resourcefulness, and a go-getter attitude
- High level of autonomy and ability to operate with minimal oversight
- Fluency in Spanish and Business English
- Advanced Excel or Google Sheets skills

title: Partner Success Manager
description:
## About the Role
The Partner Success Manager Incubator role is a strong opportunity to lead, develop, and scale post-sales success across a high-potential SMB restaurant portfolio. This role combines people leadership with commercial ownership, focusing on retention, growth, and long-term partner value.
As a Partner Success Manager Incubator, you will manage and coach a team responsible for post-deal partner management, ensuring restaurant partners are successful on the Uber Eats platform while driving measurable business outcomes. This role is critical to retaining high-value SMB partners, improving core performance metrics, and identifying opportunities that benefit both our partners and Uber Eats.
## What the Candidate Will Do
- Lead and develop a team responsible for post-sales partner management for a high-value, high-potential SMB restaurant portfolio
- Own team performance, ensuring strong partner relationships that translate into measurable outcomes such as retention, revenue growth, and product adoption
- Drive revenue and retention targets through upselling new product features and creating mutually beneficial growth strategies

title: Senior Product Manager - Autonomous Delivery
location: San Francisco, California, United States; New York, New York, United States
description:
**About the Role**
At Uber, we’re building a future where autonomous vehicles and human drivers seamlessly work together to make transportation more reliable, affordable, sustainable, and safer. Our vision is one of a hybrid future, with AVs operating alongside drivers and couriers, each bringing their unique capabilities to the table. As the largest on-demand mobility and delivery platform in the world, with our deep expertise in marketplace management, fleet utilization, and local operations — Uber is uniquely positioned to help AV companies deploy and scale their technology globally.
As the Senior Product Manager for Autonomous Delivery Experiences, you will sit at the forefront of human machine interaction. You will be responsible for defining and delivering the end-to-end consumer journey for how consumers order and receive autonomous deliveries from sidewalk robots, on-road autonomous vehicles, and aerial drones.
You will turn a high-tech logistical interaction into an intuitive, delightful, and trustworthy experience. You will solve novel UX challenges, including how Merchants and Eaters smoothly rendezvous with AVs to load and receive their orders.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
1. Deeply Understand Users: dive deep into user archetypes and how they interact with autonomous delivery form factors
2. Define the Product Vision: Own the product roadmap for the Eater-facing autonomous delivery lifecycle, including discovery, education, tracking, and the physical handoff
3. Build Trust Through UX: Design intuitive experiences that meld robotic capabilities with Eater expectations
4. Handle the Unexpected: Architect resilient "unhappy paths" and recovery flows which ensure that, when something goes wrong, consumers have a path to make things right.
5. Cross-Functional Leadership: Serve as the connector between the Engineering, Design, Operations, and external AV Partner teams.
**Basic Qualifications**
1. Minimum 6+ years of product management experience, with a track record of shipping consumer-facing mobile products.
2. Bachelor's degree or equivalent
3. User-Centricity: Deep empathy for the user and experience working closely with Product Designers to solve complex UI/UX interactions.
4. Technical Fluency: Ability to discuss API constraints, hardware-software integration, and latency issues with engineering teams and external Partners. You don't need to be a roboticist, but you must be able to speak their language.
5. Strategic Execution: Proven ability to break down ambiguous problems into actionable milestones and ship them while working with numerous cross-functional teams.
6. Data-Driven: Experience using data to drive product prioritization, setting success metrics, and iterating based on results.
**Preferred Qualifications**
1. Prior experience in robotics or autonomous vehicles
2. Strong understanding of multi-sided marketplaces
3. Ability to iterate quickly, test hypotheses, be incorrect, and adapt based on results
4. A strong consumer intuition where your best guess proves to be correct much more often than not
For New York, NY-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$190,000 per year - USD$211,000 per year.
For San Francisco, CA-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$190,000 per year - USD$211,000 per year.

title: Software Engineer II - Machine Learning, Marketplace/Maps/Membership/AV
location: New York, New York, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Sunnyvale, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
Collaborates with stakeholders to design, develop, optimize, and productionize machine learning (ML) or ML-based solutions and systems that are used within a team to solve moderately complex problems.
**About the Team****(We are hiring for multiple teams)**
We are building the future of Uber's mobility and logistics platforms. As a software engineer, you will contribute to high-scale, strategically critical systems that impact millions of users and redefine the global transportation and membership landscape.Our teams drive innovation across critical areas including:
- **Maps & Routing:** Building the core technologies for location accuracy, sensor data processing, and state-of-the-art routing algorithms to power ETAs, navigation, and matching for all Uber products.
- **Uber One Membership:** Enhancing user experience and growth for Uber One, a fast-growing program providing members with exclusive benefits, best prices, and priority across the platform.
- **Delivery Marketplace:** Delivery Marketplace is a central pillar of Uber’s delivery products, serving as the "brain" of the operation. We drive every decision that enables orders to go from point A to point B — from Uber Eats & Grocery, to newer verticals like Uber Direct and Connect. We’re responsible for everything: from dispatch decisions, predicting food ready time, delivery times, and optimizing pickup times, to ensuring we deliver the most efficient and impactful solutions for Uber’s most critical business goals
- **Autonomous Mobility & Delivery (AM&D):** Pioneering the integration of autonomous vehicles into the existing ecosystem, tackling the complex challenge of building a reliable, efficient, and scalable hybrid marketplace for both Rides and Eats.
**What You’ll Do**
- Design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning models to production to solve real-world business problems.
- Collaborate with cross-engineering teams, data scientists and other partners to gather requirements and translate them into technical specification
- Write clean, testable, and efficient code to ensure models run with low latency and high reliability.
- Implement monitoring systems to track model performance, stability, and data drift in live environments.
- Stay up-to-date with standard machine learning algorithms and industry trends to continuously improve our tech stack.
## **Basic Qualifications:**
- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Machine Learning, AI, Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field with at least 1 year of full-time Machine Learning work experience OR PhD in Machine Learning, AI, Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field
- Proficiency in at least one programming language such as Java, C++, Python, or Go
- 1 year of experience with ML algorithms/modeling- developing, training, productionization and monitoring of ML solutions at scale.
## **Preferred Qualifications:**
- Master’s degree or higher in Machine Learning, AI, Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field.
- More than 3 years of full-time machine learning work experience
- Experience with the full ML lifecycle (at Uber Scale), including model deployment, containerization and workflow orchestration.
- Experience in translating ambiguous business problems into technical solutions in a structured and principled way.
- Strong communication skills, including through documentation and design discussions
- Experience with optimization techniques and algorithmic development
- Strong problem-solving skills, with expertise in algorithms, data structures, and complexity analysis
- High bar for quality as demonstrated by code reviews, documentation, unit and integration testing

We drive every decision that enables orders to go from point A to point B — from Uber Eats & Grocery, to newer verticals like Uber Direct and Connect. We’re responsible for everything: from dispatch decisions, predicting food ready time, delivery times, and optimizing pickup times, to ensuring we deliver the most efficient and impactful solutions for Uber’s most critical business goals
- **Autonomous Mobility & Delivery (AM&D):** Pioneering the integration of autonomous vehicles into the existing ecosystem, tackling the complex challenge of building a reliable, efficient, and scalable hybrid marketplace for both Rides and Eats.
**What You’ll Do**
- Design, develop, and maintain robust and scalable software solutions
- Collaborate with product managers, cross-engineering teams, data scientists and other partners to gather requirements and translate them into technical specifications
- Identify opportunities and lead of the entire development lifecycle end-to-end, from architecture design and coding to deployment
- Conduct thorough code reviews, offering constructive feedback to maintain high code quality and elevate coding standards
- Stay up-to-date with the latest technologies and industry trends, evaluating their potential impact on our software systems and recommending appropriate integrations or improvements
- Participate in periodic on-call rotations and be available for critical issues
## **Basic Qualifications:**
- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field with at least 1 year of full-time Software Engineering work experience OR PhD in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field
- Proficiency in at least one programming language such as Java, C++, Python, or Go
- 1 year of experience with backend development, building and delivering end-user products.
## **Preferred Qualifications:**
- Master’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or related field
- More than 3 years of full-time Software Engineering work experience
- Proven experience building highly available distributed systems (at Uber scale) and working across multiple backend services
- Experience working on scalable backend systems utilizing a microservices architecture
- High bar for quality as demonstrated by code reviews, documentation, unit and integration testing
- Experience with optimization techniques and algorithmic development
- Strong problem-solving skills, with expertise in algorithms, data structures, and complexity analysis

title: Sr Commercial Lead EMEA, Uber Direct
description:
**About the Role**
This role will be a part of the EMEA Direct team and will be based either in **Paris or in Amsterdam**. It will be working very closely with all EMEA Direct country teams, as well as with the Eats and Retail marketplace teams, and cross-functional counterparts (Product, Legal, Employment, CommOps, etc).
As the EMEA Commercial Lead for Uber Direct, you will be owning all multi-country commercial discussions for the region, at any stage of the merchant lifecycle.
You’ll play a critical role in accelerating our go-to-market through addressing a pipeline of multi-country potential partners and driving deal-making across verticals. You will also manage deal extension to additional countries, as well as multi-country renegotiation to ensure we grow our partnership with each merchant, leveraging our large geographical footprint. You’ll be responsible for the full sales cycle (from prospection to deal closing), collaborating closely with respective Direct country teams. You will be responsible for negotiating the right terms with each partner to ensure consistency on the market and balance our profitability overall. You’ll liaise with Partner Engineer and local Customer Success Manager team to ensure the operational & technical onboarding of our merchants goes well and hand over the relationship after the first delivery. You will play a leadership role on a cross-functional team, executing towards our vision and strategy of connecting e-commerce to final consumers. You will collaborate across internal teams including product, finance, legal, compliance, and operations to build the most compelling value proposition for our customers!
**What You Will Do**
This is an **individual contributor** position.
As the EMEA Commercial Lead for Uber Direct, a strategic role to support the growth of the EMEA business, you will:
- **Develop go-to-market strategy** to grow pipeline and identify opportunities to break into whitespace accounts for Uber Direct, with multi-country focus. Prospect into high-value accounts that are ideal targets for Uber Direct with wide coverage across EMEA
- **Build strategic relationships** to gain positioning with decision-makers while driving growth of new and existing Direct business
- **Lead complex commercial engagements**, including managing RFQs and RFPs from Enterprise clients, and aligning internal product and operational capabilities with client requirements. Craft and deliver compelling commercial proposals tailored to prospective partners’ needs to drive adoption and business growth, in collaboration with local Direct country teams.
- **Communicate the value of the Uber Direct to Enterprise prospects and customers. Seek and achieve logistics, same-day delivery, delivery as a service industry knowledge to have enriching conversations with Enterprise Accounts.**
- **Contribute to Uber Direct offering improvement**\- Identify ways to develop further Uber Direct offering to answer partner’s needs and build business case to unlock ops & product development
- **Partner cross-functionally with key internal stakeholders**—including Legal, Finance, Compliance, Operations, and Product—to align on priorities and ensure seamless deal execution. Influence product and operational roadmap planning by advocating for merchant needs and surfacing data-backed business cases for enhancements.
- **Monitor commercial and operational performance**, ensuring visibility into pipeline health, reporting progress against growth targets, and improving efficiency for scalable growth. Monitoring operational reliability and driving continuous improvement for merchant and end-customer needs, in collaboration with the Operations team.
**What You Will Need**
- Min 7 years of experience in Sales or Business Development or Account Management in a start-up / scale-up or e-commerce /digital department of a large retailer or growing a business or other relevant experience (ideally with exposure to long sales cycles and/or complex negotiations)
- Expertise on the full sales cycle: prospecting, pitching & closing, preferably but not necessarily in a logistics, delivery, B2B, or last-mile environment
- Experience in negotiation and complex deal-making
- Great business acumen and judgment. Comfortable dealing with financial information and thoughtful about overall strategic choices
- Empathetic when dealing with different people; able to quickly build relationships with external and internal partners
- Experience in influencing multi-country and x-functional teams, setting the right expectations and objectives and driving projects toward success
- Appetence for operational and tech topics
- Entrepreneurial mindset and pro-activity
- High adaptability to change and ability to execute quickly
- Full proficiency in English and ideally French
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Industry knowledge in the on-demand / same-day delivery space
- Experience operating within matrixed or global organizations with complex stakeholder environments

title: General Manager, Uber Eats, Belgium
description:
### **About the Role**
The General Manager of Uber Eats, is responsible for the strategy, growth, and P&L of the Uber Eats business across Belgium. This high-impact role requires an entrepreneurial mindset, exceptional commercial judgment, and robust operational skills to launch and lead one of Uber Eats' most dynamic European markets.
You will set the vision, define the local strategy, help build and lead the local team, and act as the primary face of Uber Eats to the media, government, and key partners in Belgium. You'll partner closely with the Regional leadership team to advocate for the market and leverage global resources for local success.
**What You’ll Do**
- #### **Full P&L Ownership & Strategy:** full responsibility for the financial health of Uber Eats, including forecasting, and driving key business levers (e.g., revenue, margin, gross bookings) to achieve sustainable, profitable growth
- #### **Strategic thinking:** drive competitive advantage through defining country strategy, including expansion into new cities or product verticals (e.g., grocery, retail) in collaboration with cross-functional stakeholders
- #### **Operational Excellence:** Design resilient, iterative systems that balance long-term goals with the agility to pivot, together with relentless continuous improvement with focus on the customer experience **.** You’ll also implementhigh-priority, cross-functional projects aimed at improving efficiency (e.g., reducing delivery times, improving reliability, preventing fraud, membership)
- #### **Team Leadership:** Hire, mentor, and lead a high-performing local team, fostering a culture of accountability, data-centric decision-making, and rapid execution.
- #### **Cross-Functional Collaboration:** foster strong partnerships by breaking down silos, engaging teams, and leveraging cross-functional expertise to achieve shared goals, drive innovation, and create impact across Uber
- #### **External Stakeholder Engagement:** You are the primary representative for Uber Eats, proactively engaging with top restaurant groups, strategic retail partners, policymakers, regulatory bodies, and media
- #### **Policy & Communications:** Work directly with the Policy and Communications teams to manage local narratives, proactively address regulatory challenges, and ensure the business operates in a compliant and sustainable manner.
**What you'll need**
- **Professional Experience:** 10+ years of experience in high-growth operations, general management, management consulting, or a similar strategic role.
- **P&L Management:** Experience working closely with a P&L (revenue, margin, forecasting, budgeting), with a proven track record of driving improvements that lead to sustainable, profitable growth in a complex market.
- **Leadership Experience:** Experience managing and developing high-performing, diverse teams; ideally in a fast-paced, matrixed environment.
- **Strategic Market Expansion:** Experience defining and executing country-level strategies, including launching new product verticals or expanding into new geographic territories.
- **Stakeholder Management:** Exceptional communication skills with experience acting as a primary representative to external partners, such as enterprise-level clients, regulatory bodies, and media outlets.
- **Operational Rigor:** Demonstrated ability to build and scale processes, with a relentless focus on data-centric decision-making and continuous process improvement.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Worked in roles with direct P&L accountability
- Experience in on-demand logistics, delivery, or a multi-sided marketplace business model.
- Understanding of the consumer market and the local competitive and regulatory landscape.

location: Sao Paulo, São Paulo, Brazil; Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
description:
### **About the Role**
**_Please note: this hybrid position is based in São Paulo or Rio de Janeiro, Brazil – welcoming both local professionals and those open to relocating._**
At Uber, Frontend Engineers build web experiences that power the way people move and receive things around the world. As part of our engineering team, you’ll work on large-scale applications that impact millions of users. You’ll collaborate with cross-functional partners (design, backend, data science, product).
### **About the Teams**
**Delivery:** The Delivery team is focused on innovating and streamlining how products and services reach our customers. In this role, you'll contribute to building efficient and robust systems that support order processing, logistics, and real-time tracking. Your contributions will help optimize the end-to-end delivery experience, making it more reliable and seamless for both drivers and customers.
**ADS:** The Ads team builds and scales the advertising platform and its features that power sponsored experiences across Uber’s apps, connecting riders, eaters, and advertisers in relevant and measurable ways. The team owns end-to-end ad delivery (from ad creation, rendering, and experimentation to tracking, measurement, and performance optimization) across mobile and web surfaces. The work sits at the intersection of product, platform, and data, ensuring ads are high-quality for users while driving impact for advertisers and Uber’s business.
**Mobility (Rider Core):** Millions of people depend on Uber’s web application to make ride reservations and get on-demand Uber rides. The Rider Core Team at Uber is redefining what it means to Uber and expanding our products beyond on-demand rides. In this role, you'll work with many distributed teams, ranging from designers, engineers, and data scientists, to provide market-defining products and trip planning experiences over the web.
**What You’ll Do**
- Design and implement user-facing features for critical web properties at scale, making engineering trade-offs between speed, maintainability, and user experience.
- Own the technical design and execution of features, from initial architecture discussions to debugging production issues.
- Collaborate across disciplines, Product, Design, Data Science, and Backend Engineering, to turn ambiguous business needs into clear, scalable front-end systems.
- Champion front-end engineering excellence, defining and leading the adoption of code health, testing, and design standards within your project area.
- Improve core application performance, efficiency, and system reliability, proactively identifying and tackling areas of technical debt in software and processes within your team.
- Mentor junior and mid-level engineers on best practices, system design philosophy, and navigating cross-functional project dynamics.
**What You’ll Need**
- 7 years of professional software engineering experience, with a focus on large-scale, high-traffic web applications.
- Hands-on experience and proficiency in a modern web framework such as JavaScript, React, VueJS, or Angular, plus HTML and CSS.
- Proficiency in fundamental algorithms and data structures, including hash tables, trees, stacks, queues, linked lists, DFS, and BFS.
- Demonstrated understanding of modern web application architecture and design patterns, and their implications for performance and scalability.
- Advanced English communication skills, written and verbal, to effectively collaborate with globally distributed cross-functional teams.
**Bonus Points If**
- Experience leading the technical direction of complex, cross-functional projects with multiple dependencies.
- Familiarity with back-end systems (e.g., Go, Python, TypeScript) to facilitate collaboration and diagnose full-stack issues.
- Experience with the full project lifecycle, from idea inception and stakeholder engagement to monitoring in production.

title: Sr Staff Software Engineer (Backend)
description:
The **Identity Organization** is the foundational gateway to Uber’s global ecosystem. We manage the core digital presence of millions of riders, drivers, earners, and merchants across the globe. Our mission is to deliver a **seamless, secure, and "invisible" identity experience** across every Uber product, partner, and acquisition.
Operating with a global footprint of **120+ engineers**, we tackle challenges at a scale few companies ever reach. As a **Sr. Staff Engineer**, you will serve as the **technical conscience** of our identity architecture. You’ll ensure our entire stack—from mobile SDKs to high-throughput backend services—is resilient, modular, and ready for the next decade of growth.
* * *
## **Your Impact**
- **Long-Range Architectural Stewardship:** You don’t just plan for the next sprint; you architect for the next era. You’ll anticipate shifts in the identity landscape—such as **Passkeys, Decentralized Identity, and Zero Trust**—ensuring Uber can pivot without massive rewrites.
- **The "Force Multiplier" Effect:** You elevate the entire 120+ person organization. By defining **GenAI-driven fraud detection** patterns and pioneering **"Identity-as-Code,"** you will reduce complexity and accelerate developer velocity across all of Uber Engineering.
- **Cross-Domain Influence:** You are the primary bridge between Engineering, Product, and Legal. You’ll translate complex global regulations (GDPR, DMA, etc.) into elegant technical solutions that protect users without compromising their experience.
- **Operational Excellence at 1B+ Scale:** You set the gold standard for reliability. You will oversee systems handling **hundreds of thousands of requests per second**, ensuring five-nines availability for Uber’s most critical path.
* * *
## **Qualifications**
### **Minimum Requirements**
- **Experience:** 12+ years of full-time Software Engineering experience.
- **Education:** BS or equivalent in Computer Science or a related field.
- **Distributed Systems:** A proven track record of designing, building, and migrating large-scale backend platforms to hundreds of millions of users.
- **Customer-Facing Platforms:** Experience scaling SaaS or consumer platforms to a global user base.
### **Preferred Expertise**
- **Hyper-Scale:** Experience managing systems supporting millons of users
- **System Integration:** Expertise in connecting complex systems between large-scale, tech-driven enterprises.
- **Organizational Leadership:** Proven ability to lead high-stakes projects that span across massive, multi-disciplinary organizations.
For Sunnyvale, CA-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$267,000 per year - USD$297,000 per year.

title: Director, Product Management, Earner Experience
description:
**About the Team**
Uber is a technology company that is changing the way the world thinks about transportation. We are building technology people use everyday. Whether it's heading home from work, getting a meal delivered from a favorite restaurant, or a way to earn extra income, Uber is becoming part of the fabric of daily life.
We're making cities safer, smarter, and more connected. And we're doing it at a global scale-energizing local economies and bringing opportunity to millions of people around the world.
Uber's positive impact is tangible in the communities we operate in, and that drives us to keep moving forward.
Every day, Earners complete millions of trips in the Uber driver app across various gig types, including rideshare, food delivery, groceries, and more. The Earner team is responsible for the Earner app and the end-to-end experience for millions of drivers and couriers around the world—people who rely on Uber to support a range of financial goals. The Earner app is a super app for earning flexibly used by 9.5M earners globally on average 6 hours a day.
**About the Role**
Uber is looking for a Director of Product - Earner, India. You will lead a team that focuses on helping earners maximize the value of their time on the Uber Platform by helping them plan and strategize their time to improve their earnings. You will also work on solving complex challenges around Earnings and Benefits platforms and comprehension and work cross-functionally across many teams at Uber to power the Earnings and information experience charter for Uber. This is a high-impact role with lots of visibility. We're looking for an empathetic product leader with experience managing and developing large teams and building applications with engaging end-to-end user experiences
**What You’ll Do**
- Build a vision: Define a product vision and strategy for the team and identify both near and long-term initiatives to build the product roadmap.
- Align teams: Work closely with teams across Uber including Eng, Data Science, and Operations to align business and product strategy.
- Execute: Translate user needs and insights into product requirements by distilling user problems, writing hypotheses, and describing desired outcomes.
- Iterate: Distill insights from various quantitative and qualitative data sources such as performance dashboards, experimentation results, and user research. Then apply the findings back into the product development process and create alignment.
- Help earners succeed within the context of Uber’s marketplace ⇒ collaborate with Marketplace, Fares, and the broader Uber Earner team on strategy and execution.
- Managing a team of geographically dispersed PMs across Bangalore and Amsterdam. Leader will be expected to set strategy and be quite autonomous.
**What You’ll Need**
- 15+ years of experience in driving complex product initiatives and 8+ years experience in driving a nimble and impactful team of PMs.
- Demonstrated experience in building and shipping consumer and/or marketplace centric products at global scale and complexity with millions if not billions of real time and dynamic touch points.
- Proven ability to deliver top class customer experiences with deep understanding of UX best practices.
- Experience defining market and product requirements, working with engineering organizations to rapidly deliver high-impact capabilities to market.
- Exceptional written and verbal communication skills with the ability to create compelling positioning / messaging strategy and present complex information clearly and succinctly.
- Strong analytical and quantitative skills with the ability to use data and metrics to justify requirements, features and drive management of product.
- High attention to detail and proven ability to manage multiple, competing priorities simultaneously with minimal supervision.

title: Corporate Communications Manager, Executive Storytelling and Social Media
location: San Francisco, California, United States; New York, New York, United States
description:
## **About the Role**
We are looking for a gifted writer and high-impact comms strategist to set direction and provide hands-on support for executive-level storytelling, primarily through social media. Reporting directly to Uber’s head of corporate communications, this person will partner closely with members of our executive leadership team and their staff, as well as comms and social media leads across the company, to identify the best moments and channels for leaders to “go direct.” They will build content calendars, issues platforms, and channel strategies, helping to sharpen each leader’s distinct voice.
## **About the Team**
The most successful technology companies are forward-thinking storytellers who can effectively engage business, technical, and consumer audiences alike to illustrate the magic behind—and the benefits of—their products.
Our team is responsible for telling Uber’s story, whether it’s transforming how we get around and supporting the lives of people who value independent, flexible work; helping reduce congestion in cities and complement public transportation; or empowering local commerce through our delivery offerings. This work is about building deep relationships with key journalists and opinion leaders, and partnering across the company to create campaigns that tell our story in new and effective ways.
## **What You'll Do**
- Work directly with executive leaders and their teams to identify the issues, moments, and perspectives that matter most—for them personally and for Uber’s business.
- Devise tailored platform and channel strategies that harness each leader’s unique voice, setting them up for even greater impact.
- Define success metrics, create reporting cadences, and iterate based on performance and broader objectives.
- Support real-time social decision-making during fast-moving news moments, including escalation pathways and coordination with policy/legal when needed.
- Shoot, edit, and create original content (both videos and graphics).
- Attend events and help capture content during key company moments.
- Provide broader executive communications support as needed, including presentation development, speechwriting, and other high-impact writing across the team.
## **Basic Qualifications**
- 7+ years of proven experience in a fast-paced corporate, agency, or political environment, supporting notable personalities or world-changing brands.
- Bachelor's degree
- Impeccable writing and editing skills, with strong judgment, a high bar for quality, and the ability to seamlessly switch between thought leadership, product narratives, culture/talent storytelling, and stakeholder comms—without losing authenticity.
- Creativity and strong visual instincts, with comfort translating ideas into compelling, platform-native content.
- Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple projects simultaneously, while staying focused on long-term goals.
- Proven experience building relationships and influencing cross-functionally, particularly with senior-level stakeholders.
- Strong understanding of news cycles, reputation management, and emerging trends in social and new media.
## **Preferred Qualifications**
- Managed candidate social media for a major political campaign.
- You are an outstanding video shooter and editor with an eye for graphic design, proficient in Adobe Premiere or CapCut for video editing, and Canva or Photoshop for graphic creation.

title: Senior Software Engineer (Storage Platform)
description:
**About the Role**
Engineering at Uber means building systems with real-world impact under real-world constraints. In this role, you’ll help operate and evolve the infrastructure behind Uber’s large-scale data storage platforms - spanning more than 20 technologies including MySQL, Redis, and Kafka, managing exabytes of data across 50,000+ hosts, running in containers. Our mission is to deliver storage platforms at massive scale with high availability, reliability, and performance, where operations are heavily automated and systems are designed to self-heal.
This role is based in our Aarhus, Denmark office - a key global hub for Uber’s Infrastructure and Platform teams. Since opening in 2014, the office has grown to more than 150 engineers and has become a center of excellence for control planes, distributed systems, and mission-critical storage and compute platforms. Beyond the technical challenges, you’ll join a highly collaborative team of exceptional engineers who are passionate about building reliable, world-class infrastructure.
Read more about our work here: [https://www.uber.com/dk/en/blog/odin-stateful-platform/](https://www.uber.com/dk/en/blog/odin-stateful-platform/)
**What You Will Do**
You will build Uber’s storage platform, your team focuses on components running on each host in the fleet. On a daily basis you will:
1. Continuously learn about complex systems, storage technologies, change management at fleet level, Linux.
2. Plan, design, and develop new capabilities of the system, and measure their impact on business objectives.
3. Monitor and debug the system in production. You will be part of an on-call rotation.
4. Work together with colleagues in your team, multiple related Stateful Platform teams on the floor, and partner teams in the US and India.
5. Write clean, tested, reviewed Go code, with best in class AI assistance.
6. Contribute to engineering culture in terms of coding and testing practices, dev experience practices, productivity.
You will build ownership of the team's charter (or parts of it) and a long-term vision for the involved components.
**What You Will Need/Bonus Points**
Basic Qualifications
1. 5+ years of experience.
2. BS, MS, or Ph.D. degree in computer science, similar technical field of study, or similar practical experience.
3. Grit, growth mindset, passion for engineering.
4. Strong at design, coding, testing, bash/ssh.
5. Experience operating containerized software at scale.
6. Team player, collaborative. Can coordinate projects with partner teams, lead ambitious engineering initiatives.
7. Attention to detail, particularly around software engineering fundamentals, and quality.
8. Embrace of AI in software development workflows.
Preferred Qualifications
1. Experience with resource management on Linux - CPU, memory, disk, network.
2. Experience with building large-scale distributed systems.
3. Excellent written communication in design documents, problem analysis or incident post mortems.
4. Passion to look inside and understand how fundamental platforms work (Linux, Kubernetes).
5. Experience with OCI, GCP, AWS.
6. Experience with multiple SQL, NoSql, Blob, Cache products.
This pipeline is intended for candidates with approximately 5+ years of relevant industry experience after completing their education.
The role level corresponds to an L5a position — Senior Software Engineer. Learn more about the L5a hiring process and how to prepare here: [https://jobs.uber.com/en/uber-interview-guide/backend-engineering-interview-guide/](https://jobs.uber.com/en/uber-interview-guide/backend-engineering-interview-guide/)
**Perks & Benefits:**
- **Competitive Compensation Package:** Includes base pay, bonus and the opportunity to be awarded stock options (RSUs) to ensure you own a piece of the mission you’re building.
- **Monthly Uber Credits:** Credits to use on Uber Rides and Uber Eats every month.
- **Culture & Socials:** Frequent local social events and office clubs (chess, board games, running, crossfit, creative club and more)
- **Tech Community:** We host regular local tech meetups to stay connected with the Aarhus engineering scene, and give our engineers the opportunity to sometimes share what they’re working on with the community
- **Well-being & Fertility:** Global support programs for mental health, wellness, and family planning/fertility.
- **Parental Leave:** Generous, gender-neutral parental leave to support your life outside of work.
- **Modern Aarhus Hub:** Work in a center of technical excellence featuring catered lunches and top-tier collaboration spaces.

If you’re driven by impact, energized by challenge, and ready to shape how the world moves, this is the place for you. What moves us, moves the world - let’s move it forward, together.

description:
**About the Role**
Sales at Uber means navigating ambiguity, earning trust fast, and solving problems that don’t come with a script. As an Account Manager for our French-speaking region (Wallonia & Brussels), you are the primary consultant for a portfolio of local favorite restaurants. You’ll bridge the gap between our technology and the real-world challenges of the restaurant industry, helping partners grow their business while ensuring long-term profitability on our platform.
This role is high-impact but requires a high degree of grit. You’ll lead complex commercial discussions and renegotiate contracts in an environment where market dynamics shift daily. You won't just be managing accounts; you’ll be solving messy, real-time problems that impact the livelihoods of your partners. If you are energized by the challenge of building deep partnerships in a fast-paced, high-stakes environment where the answer isn’t always in a playbook, this is where you’ll grow.

- Navigate internal complexity and collaborate across operations, marketing, and product teams to unblock challenges and deliver solutions for your portfolio.
- Lead tough commercial negotiations and contract renewals with confidence, staying resilient through objections and high-pressure conversations.
- Use data to identify growth gaps, building convincing pitches that help partners leverage the full suite of Uber products to optimize their menus and operations.

title: Senior Applications Engineer - Salesforce
location: Sunnyvale, California, United States; San Francisco, California, United States
description:
**About the Role**
The Sales Automation Engineering team is dedicated to empowering Uber Eats sellers by building tools and automation that make it easier to sell Uber Eats services to merchants. As a Senior Application Engineer, you will be a technical leader within the CRM and Revenue tech stack, driving the architectural vision for our Salesforce and CPQ ecosystem. You will lead complex integrations across Uber’s internal billing infrastructure and external platforms, ensuring our pricing and quoting systems are built for massive scale. Your work will directly influence Uber’s global revenue trajectory and the long-term efficiency of our global B2B sales force.
**What the Candidate Will Do**
1. Technical Leadership: Lead the design and end-to-end implementation of large-scale Salesforce and CPQ features, ensuring Quote-to-Cash processes are seamless and scalable.
2. Architecture & Strategy: Define the long-term technical roadmap for CPQ automation, including Product Catalog design, Price Rules, and complex Quote Template logic.
3. Complex Integrations: Architect and build sophisticated middleware and API integrations between Salesforce CPQ and Uber’s core microservices (built in Go/Java) and downstream billing/ERP systems.
4. Mentorship: Act as a technical mentor for Junior team members, conducting deep code reviews and fostering a culture of engineering excellence in Apex
5. Stakeholder Management: Partner closely with Sales Ops, Finance, and Product Managers to translate complex pricing strategies and legal requirements into technical specifications.
6. Operational Excellence: Drive improvements in CPQ deployment strategies (e.g., handling data-heavy migrations) and automated testing to minimize revenue-impacting errors.
7. Problem Solving: Tackle complex architectural bottlenecks in CPQ, such as optimizing calculation engine performance for large quotes.
**Basic Qualifications**
1. Education: A Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
2. Experience: At least 7+ years of professional software development experience.
3. Salesforce Specialization: Minimum of 5+ years of hands-on Salesforce application development.
4. CPQ Expertise: At least 2+ years of experience specifically designing and implementing Salesforce CPQ. Deep understanding of the CPQ object model, Quote Line Editor (QLE) customization, and Product/Price Rules.
5. Core Engineering: Expert-level proficiency in Object-Oriented Programming (Java, Go, or Python) and a deep understanding of distributed systems.
6. Technical Depth: Expert knowledge of Apex (Async, Triggers, Patterns), CPQ Quote Calculator Plugins (QCP), Lightning Web Components (LWC), and advanced SOQL/SOSL.
7. API Design: Proven track record of designing high-concurrency REST/SOAP/Bulk APIs and event-driven architectures (e.g., Kafka, Pub/Sub).
8. Communication: Demonstrated ability to influence technical and non-technical stakeholders and lead cross-functional technical discussions.
**Preferred Qualifications**
1. Advanced CPQ: Experience with CPQ API for "headless" quoting or external configurators.
2. Cloud Infrastructure: Hands-on experience with AWS/GCP services (Lambda, S3, SQS) and integrating them with SaaS platforms.
3. DevOps Culture: Deep experience with advanced CI/CD tooling (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, Copado) and managing complex CPQ data deployments.

title: Sr Operations and Strategy Manager, Airports
description:
**About the Role**
We are looking for an individual who has strong operational and analytical skills, as well as strategic business sense. In this role, you will manage our local operations and be a teammate in coming up with initiatives to drive growth and operational efficiencies related to airports and venues, from the rider and driver experience to pricing and partners management.
This is a key position that can make a big impact on business and change the way people move in our countries. We are currently operating in every city in Argentina, and we have a lot of exciting projects going on!
**What the Candidate Will Do**
1. **Strategic Ownership:** Proactively identify business needs and issues. Prioritize among projects to define the best path to meet strategic objectives
2. **Business Sense:** Translate insights from operational and financial metrics into strategy and action plans. Develop and apply understanding of the levers that move our business
3. **Problem Solving:** Identify the root cause of risks and opportunities, propose multiple, practical solutions, and weigh tradeoffs to recommend best approach
4. **Process Optimization:** Implement, optimize, and scale core analyses and processes, regularly reviewing performance
5. **Product Improvement:** Propose features or improvements that could improve the experience on and efficiency of our marketplace, measuring their impact and prioritizing them, in order to advocate for their development. Collaborate with Product teams around the world to execute new launches

**Basic Qualifications**
1. **Education**: We welcome people from all backgrounds to apply
2. **Years of Experience/Industries: 5+ years** of professional experience.
3. **Data-Driven Mentality:** Make strategic decisions based on data analysis and interpretation. Experience getting in the weeds of data, balanced with the ability to see the big picture
4. **Communication:** English proficiency. Demonstrated experience interpreting and presenting data to various audiences in an engaging and comprehensive, yet succinct manner
5. **Executional Excellence:** Ability to optimally manage and prioritize complex operations that require the support of peers and other teams, and deliver results in a fast-paced and ambiguous environment, while maintaining high-quality work standards
6. **Planning & Organization:** Ability to plan complex or multi-faceted projects with cross-functional teams, defining timelines, deliverables, interdependencies, and resource allocation
**Preferred Qualifications**
1. Experience working with Cross Functional teams.
2. Background with P&L responsibilities
3. Experience in Operations, Consulting or related.
4. Data analysis tools: SQL, Python, and visualization tools.

description:
**About the Role**
As an Account Manager at Uber Eats, you will manage a portfolio of local favourite restaurants in the Flanders region and be responsible for its long-term commercial relationships, growth and profitability.
This role is key to accelerating Uber Eats’ growth across the Flanders territory, which is why fluency in Dutch is essential.
To do so, you will collaborate efficiently and closely with your portfolio of restaurant partners to optimize their operations, foster strong relationships through customized growth recommendations and identify opportunities to upsell additional Uber products.
We are open to candidates residing in the Flanders region, Antwerp, and potentially Ghent, with a mandatory presence at the Brussels office once per week.
**What will you do**
**What you’ll do**
- Build and grow long-term partnerships by understanding what matters most to your restaurant partners - doing the right thing long term.

- Strong interpersonal and negotiation skills, with the ability to clearly frame a value proposition, demonstrate the true value of give/get in partnerships to ensure the best outcomes for both Uber Eats and our partners, probe for objections, remove obstacles, and eliminate barriers to gain commitment
- Strong motivation and passion to work with top restaurants and build a business together
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Capable of running analysis and transforming data into relevant insights
- Ability to think strategically and analyze restaurant needs to both unlock opportunities and creatively problem-solve
- Demonstrate time management and organizational skills with the ability to multitask and prioritize based on impact
- Willingness to hustle, roll-up sleeves, and get in the weeds with our highest value partners in order to hit quarterly and annual revenue, retention, and adoption targets.

This is a commercial role offering a competitive fixed salary plus a highly attractive variable component based on performance.

title: Sales Account Executive MAZATLAN
description:
**About the Role**
As an Account Executive, you will be responsible for expanding Uber Eats’ restaurant selection by acquiring high-quality SMB partners and driving deals from first contact to First Trip. This role requires strong negotiation skills, the ability to identify high-potential restaurants, and the judgment to balance quality and volume in complex, competitive geographies.
You will own your funnel end-to-end: prospecting, qualification, pitching, negotiation, contracting, and early-life follow-up to ensure merchants activate successfully and remain engaged from day one.
This role is ideal for sellers who are analytical, disciplined operators and strategic negotiators, not just activity-driven closers.
**_This is a field-based role in Mazatlan._** _Assigned territories may vary and may differ from the candidate’s city of residence. Willingness to travel as needed is required._
**What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points**
_\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----_
**What You’ll Do**
1\. Build and Manage a High-Quality Funnel
1. Independently design your weekly funnel construction plan to consistently close ~20 restaurants per month.
2. Source, qualify, and prioritize restaurants using geo insights, density logic, competitive dynamics, demand potential, and operational readiness.
3. Maintain a healthy stage distribution and ensure deals move with speed and clarity.
2\. Run Complex Negotiations
1. Lead multi-step negotiations that balance unit economics, merchant expectations, and long-term value—especially in competitive or price-sensitive segments.
2. Structure offers based on merchant type (SMB anchors, multilocations, long-tail SMBs).
3\. Prospect Strategically
1. Use street prospecting, referrals, targeted outreach, and data-driven prioritization—not just lists—to identify high-potential merchants.
2. Quickly identify restaurants that can drive incremental trips, category variety, and geo density.
4\. Master Pipeline & Salesforce Hygiene
1. Maintain a clean, accurate, inspection-ready pipeline in Salesforce at all times.
2. Log discovery insights, objections, pricing details, next steps, and reasons for loss.
3. Update forecasts weekly with discipline and accuracy.
5\. Drive Day-1 Retention Through Activation Quality
1. Ensure new merchants meet basic readiness standards (menu, photos, devices, delivery expectations) before launch.
2. Provide structured follow-up during the first 28 days to support their early performance.
3. Identify early churn signals and intervene proactively.
6\. Deliver Volume with Quality
1. Balance quantity (target FT/month) with quality (category fit, demand potential, menu quality & GBs).
2. Build a mix of SMBs and multilocations based on territory needs and strategic density.
7\. Represent Uber Eats with Excellence
1. Build trusted relationships with restaurant owners and decision makers.
2. Exhibit a strong understanding of merchant economics and our marketplace.
3. Be a brand ambassador in the field every day.
_\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----_
**Required Skills & Experience**
1. Proven track record of closing 15–20+ deals per month or equivalent productivity.
2. Strong negotiation experience: pricing, incentives, and multi-stakeholder deals.
3. Experience building your own pipeline, not just working assigned leads.
4. Ability to identify high-potential merchants using logic (location, category, demand fit).
5. Mastery of CRM tools (Salesforce preferred) with excellent documentation habits.
6. Experience working in field sales or hybrid field → phone models.
7. Analytical mindset: ability to draw insights from data to prioritize and improve conversion.
**Soft Skills**
1. Highly organized, structured, and disciplined.
2. Strategic thinker: balances volume targets with marketplace quality.
3. Comfortable in fast-paced, ambiguity-heavy environments.
4. Strong communication and objection-handling skills.
5. Team-first mindset with a desire to improve processes and share best practices.
_\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----_
1. **3+ years of full-cycle sales experience** in SMB, field sales, or high-velocity B2B environments, with a proven ability to own deals end-to-end—from prospecting to close and activation.
2. **Consistent track record of meeting or exceeding monthly acquisition targets** (≈15–20+ deals/month), including self-sourced pipeline, negotiation, and post-close activation.
3. **Strong Salesforce discipline and data-driven mindset**, with hands-on experience using SFDC to manage pipeline health, stage progression, forecasting accuracy, and deal hygiene.
**Success Attributes**
Customer CentricityMarket and Platform ExpertiseNegotiation & Deal-MakingSales Lifecycle ManagementStakeholder ManagementUber ValuesCase Study

title: Senior Manager, Safety Lead - AM&D Europe
location: Berlin, Germany; London, United Kingdom; Amsterdam, Netherlands
description:
**About the Role**
We are seeking an experienced Safety Leader for Uber's Autonomous Mobility & Delivery Safety Team to effectively represent Uber’s Safety standards and requirements to our partners in Europe.
This role requires a strong understanding of autonomous vehicle operations, knowledge of the relevant AV industry standards and deep understanding of the European automotive and AV regulations that inform. The candidate will contribute to regulatory discussions on safety standards and work closely with AV technology and fleet partners to promote adherence to safety protocols, industry standards, and Uber’s internal safety practices. This role will require the candidate to become an expert on the European Union and United Kingdom’s regulatory frameworks for the deployment of autonomous vehicles and the overall vehicle homologation process.
**What You'll Do**
- **Subject Matter Expertise:** Serve as the strategic expert on EU vehicle type-approval frameworks—including UN ECE regulations, including EU 2022/1426 and emerging UNECE WP.29 Global Technical Regulation for AVs —while advising partners and internal teams by translating complex technical and regulatory requirements into clear, actionable guidance.
- **External Stakeholder Engagement:** Collaborate with regulators, technical services, public safety officials, and industry bodies to interpret evolving standards for automated driving systems, ensuring partners’ certification pathways are well supported and internal safety and compliance strategies remain aligned with current and anticipated regulations.
- **Technical Policy Support:** Apply technical understanding of AV safety frameworks to inform and contribute to the development of local safety standards and regulatory guidance. Collaborate with internal teams to ensure alignment with global safety policies.
- **AV Tech Partner Onboarding:** Lead safety assessments of AV technology partners using Uber’s Safety Guidelines for Autonomous Mobility and Delivery, evaluating their organizational, operational, and engineering safety approach to ensure alignment with Uber’s safety expectations and industry best practices.
- **Fleet Partner Collaboration:** Work collaboratively with fleet partners to assure that operational safety requirements are met, maintaining high standards in line with Uber's internal safety guidelines.
- **Incident Response:** Liaise with various stakeholders, including Uber team members, fleet partners, and regulatory bodies, to manage and coordinate responses to any safety incidents or concerns.
- **EU Industry Awareness:** Maintain up-to-date knowledge of safety and health trends in the AV and transportation industries in the European Union markets.
- **Cross-functional Leadership:** Collaborate with many different skilled individuals across Uber, including business development, fleets, product management, legal, policy, safety operations, marketing, engineering, and regional operations teams
**Basic Qualifications**
- **Industry Experience**: Minimum of 8 years of demonstrated experience in transportation, automotive or autonomous vehicle development.
- **Vehicle Regulations:** Demonstrated experience with EU automotive safety standards and regulations related to autonomous mobility.
- **Transportation Safety Expertise:** Familiarity with functional safety, operational safety, safety management systems, safety cases, safety critical software development, and safety data analysis.
- **Communication & Relationship Building:** Exceptional communication, negotiation, and relationship management skills, with the ability to work across different cultures and industries.
- **Understanding of Safety Standards:** Familiarity with or experience contributing to discussions within industry standards organizations (e.g., SAE, AVSC, IEEE, ISO) focused on safety and best practices for autonomous vehicles or experience utilizing industry safety standards in practice.
**Preferred Qualifications**
- Advanced degree in safety, engineering, or related technical fields
- Experience working in the European markets or a similar regulatory landscape
- Familiarity with Uber's Safety Guidelines Program and relevant industry standards
- Experience performing data analysis and statistical analysis of operational safety data to generate safety performance indicators
- Experience representing a company or industry group in regulatory or policy discussions.
- Demonstrated ability to lead multi-stakeholder efforts or working groups related to AV safety standards.
- A continuous improvement approach, with a history of quick adaptation and self-directed learning
- A passion for creating empathetic, inspiring, and innovative transportation solutions in pursuit of positive societal outcomes

title: Account Executive, Uber Eats, France
description:
**About the Role**
**Go Anywhere, Get Anything : Go Get it.**
What was initially just a way to order a ride in a few clicks has led to billions of interactions: whether to travel, order a meal or even have your groceries delivered, Uber aims to become the operating system of daily life accessible to everyone.
In addition to bringing together the best talents in the Tech industry, our Paris office brings together the teams in charge of the VTC (Uber) and Uber Eats part. Our employees have the opportunity to take part in innovative projects that lead us to think about the mobility of tomorrow and solve very concrete problems to offer our users the best possible experience.
More specifically, the Uber Eats teams are working to develop meal delivery - via the application - among 45,000 partner restaurants and merchants in France, in less than 25 minutes. Uber's technology and know-how are used by restaurants and delivery people to simplify meal delivery and provide the best experience for users.
**The role within the team**
Within Uber Eats, the _Sales_ teams are responsible for the selection of restaurants available on the application in more than 300 cities in France . The objective: to offer a wide and varied offering of restaurants in each area covered by the application and to unearth local gems that will accelerate the development of Uber Eats.
To do this, _Sales_ identifies, canvasses, negotiates and supports restaurants to convince them to adopt a new sales channel: delivery via the Uber Eats application. All sales stages are covered: from making contact (canvassing) through negotiation to the completion of the first delivery by the restaurant.
Relations with restaurants are mainly by telephone or email, but tactical trips can sometimes be organized.
**Responsibilities of the role**
- Clearly present the services, values ​​and projects of Uber Eats to restaurants (prospects), in order to make them want to join us.
- Conclude partnerships with restaurants in your territory by managing the entire sales process, from prospecting to closing and the arrival of partners on the platform.
- Manage commercial negotiations in order to secure partnerships with restaurants with very high potential.
- Contribute to the development and operationalization of the commercial strategy to ensure that Uber Eats can successfully develop _at scale_ (everywhere and at high speed).
- Identify and prioritize the restaurants to approach for each area worked in order to balance the volume of contacts, conversion of prospects and business potential (local gem, diversified cuisine offering, etc.).
**Required profile**
We are looking for a curious and motivated _foodista_, with a strong interest in Food-tech, a strong taste for challenge and negotiation.
More precisely :
- Experience in a Sales position (CDI, CDD or Apprenticeship), with a strong cold-calling dimension
- Solid knowledge of telephone prospecting, negotiation and closing of contracts and support
- Good relational skills in order to quickly build relationships with different partners
- A determined, autonomous and results-oriented profile with organizational and initiative-taking skills in a constantly evolving environment
- Clear communication and strong persuasiveness
- A fluent level of French and a good level of English

title: Sales Manager, Uber Eats SMB
description:
**About the Role**
Uber Eats is seeking an exceptional **Sales Manager** to join our **US&C Restaurants Team**. In this role, you will lead a high-performing team of SMB Sales Representatives focused on driving new business acquisition and accelerating market growth. You’ll be responsible for achieving ambitious monthly revenue targets, developing your team’s skills, and ensuring strong collaboration across functions to deliver best-in-class results.
This is a highly visible leadership opportunity for a driven, strategic, and people-first leader who thrives in fast-paced environments and is passionate about building teams, growing businesses, and making an impact.
**What You Will Do**
- Lead & Develop: Coach, mentor, and inspire a team of Sales Representatives to achieve and exceed acquisition and revenue goals.
- Drive Results: Monitor performance, quota attainment, and pipeline health to ensure consistent execution and delivery against targets.
- Enable Success: Conduct regular deal reviews, call observations, and performance calibrations to identify strengths and growth areas.
- Foster Collaboration: Partner closely with cross-functional teams (Sales Operations, Account Management, Marketing, and City Operations) to align on market priorities and optimize performance.
- Strategic Influence: Serve as a key thought partner to Territory Leadership on short- and long-term business strategy, growth opportunities, and process improvement.
- Optimize Systems: Work with Sales Operations to analyze performance data, improve workflows, and ensure the team has the tools needed to sell effectively.
**Basic Qualifications**
- 4+ years of full-cycle sales experience
- Proven ability to meet or exceed sales targets in a fast-paced, metrics-driven environment
**Preferred Qualifications**
- 1-2 years of sales management experience
- Experienced people leader with strong communication skills and high emotional intelligence
- Consistent track record of developing top sales talent and leading teams to exceed revenue goals
- Analytical mindset with experience using data to inform coaching, forecast accuracy, and sales strategy

title: Senior account Manager Global
description:
**About the Role**
The Account Manager role is a fantastic opportunity to showcase your relationship-building and portfolio management skills within an assigned territory. This role focuses on post-sales management of Global Enterprises, strengthening partnerships to increase retention, and driving business growth with mutually beneficial outcomes. As an Account Manager, you play a key role in retaining our most valuable accounts, ensuring partners thrive on our platform, and identifying opportunities to enhance core business metrics for our restaurant partners and Uber Eats.
**What the Candidate Will Need / Bonus Points**
\-\-\-\- What the Candidate Will Do ----
1. Lead post-deal partner management for a high-value, high-potential portfolio of restaurant partners.
2. Build and maintain strong partner relationships to achieve measurable outcomes, including revenue growth targets, upselling new product features, and ensuring high retention by creating mutual business value.
3. Act as the voice of the customer to internal teams, translating partner needs and advocating on their behalf.
4. Develop a deep understanding of the partner landscape and cultivate positive relationships across our restaurant partner organizations.
5. Conceive, design, and lead growth initiatives with restaurant partners, working cross-functionally with Uber’s internal teams to drive projects to completion.
6. Set, track, and report on performance metrics, and conduct business reviews with partners.
\-\-\-\- Basic Qualifications ----
1. 3-5 years of experience, ideally in a similar role.
2. Confidence in making data-driven decisions and applying sound business judgment.
3. Strong project management skills, with the ability to develop detailed joint business plans with partners and consistently deliver results.
4. Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with strong executive presence and interpersonal abilities.
5. A proactive, resourceful, and go-getter attitude; this team moves fast and operates in dynamic environments.
6. Ability to work independently with minimal oversight.
7. A willingness to work hard, dive into details, and closely collaborate with our highest-value partners.
8. Experience in customer-facing roles with a proven track record of achieving business targets or meeting quotas.
\-\-\-\- Preferred Qualifications ----
1. Proficiency in English.
2. SQL knowledge is a plus.
